# nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/#)

NODRIVER[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/#nodriver "Link to this heading")

============================================================================================

[CLICK HERE FOR DOCS](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/#id1 "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**This package provides next level webscraping and browser automation using a relatively simple interface.**

*   **This is the official successor of the** [Undetected-Chromedriver](https://github.com/ultrafunkamsterdam/undetected-chromedriver/)
     **python package.**
    
*   **No more webdriver, no more selenium**
    

Direct communication provides even better resistance against web applicatinon firewalls (WAF’s), while performance gets a massive boost. This module is, contrary to undetected-chromedriver, fully asynchronous.

What makes this package different from other known packages, is the optimization to stay undetected for most anti-bot solutions.

Another focus point is usability and quick prototyping, so expect a lot to work `-as is-` , with most method parameters having `best practice` defaults. Using 1 or 2 lines, this is up and running, providing best practice config by default.

While usability and convenience is important. It’s also easy to fully customizable everything using the entire array of [CDP](https://chromedevtools.github.io/devtools-protocol/)
 domains, methods and events available.

### Some features[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/#some-features "Link to this heading")

*   A blazing fast undetected chrome (-ish) automation library
    
*   No chromedriver binary or Selenium dependency
    
*   This equals bizarre performance increase and less detections!
    
*   Up and running in 1 line of code\*
    
*   uses fresh profile on each run, cleans up on exit
    
*   save and load cookies to file to not repeat tedious login steps
    
*   smart element lookup, by selector or text, including iframe content. this could also be used as wait condition for a element to appear, since it will retry for the duration of <timeout> until found. single element lookup by text using tab.find(), accepts a best\_match flag, which will not naively return the first match, but will match candidates by closest matching text length.
    
*   descriptive \_\_repr\_\_ for elements, which represent the element as html
    
*   utility function to convert a running undetected\_chromedriver.Chrome instance to a nodriver.Browser instance and contintue from there
    
*   packed with helpers and utility methods for most used and important operations

# Quickstart guide - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/quickstart.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

Quickstart guide[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/quickstart.html#quickstart-guide "Link to this heading")

====================================================================================================================================

Installation[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/quickstart.html#installation "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Since it’s a part of undetected-chromedriver, installation goes via

\# todo. use pip install nodriver instead
pip install undetected\-chromedriver

* * *

Or as a seperate package via:

pip install nodriver

usage example[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/quickstart.html#usage-example "Link to this heading")

==============================================================================================================================

The aim of this project (just like undetected-chromedriver, somewhere long ago) is to keep it short and simple, so you can quickly open an editor or interactive session, type or paste a few lines and off you go.

import nodriver as uc

async def main():

    browser \= await uc.start()
    page \= await browser.get('https://www.nowsecure.nl')

    ... further code ...

if \_\_name\_\_ \== '\_\_main\_\_':
    \# since asyncio.run never worked (for me)
    uc.loop().run\_until\_complete(main())

More complete example[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/quickstart.html#more-complete-example "Link to this heading")

==============================================================================================================================================

import nodriver

async def main():

    browser \= await nodriver.start()
    page \= await browser.get('https://www.nowsecure.nl')

    await page.save\_screenshot()
    await page.get\_content()
    await page.scroll\_down(150)
    elems \= await page.select\_all('\*\[src\]')

    for elem in elems:
        await elem.flash()

    page2 \= await browser.get('https://twitter.com', new\_tab\=True)
    page3 \= await browser.get('https://github.com/ultrafunkamsterdam/nodriver', new\_window\=True)

    for p in (page, page2, page3):
       await p.bring\_to\_front()
       await p.scroll\_down(200)
       await p   \# wait for events to be processed
       await p.reload()
       if p != page3:
           await p.close()

if \_\_name\_\_ \== '\_\_main\_\_':

    \# since asyncio.run never worked (for me)
    uc.loop().run\_until\_complete(main())

Custom starting options[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/quickstart.html#custom-starting-options "Link to this heading")

==================================================================================================================================================

I’ll leave out the async boilerplate here

from nodriver import \*

browser \= await start(
    headless\=False,
    user\_data\_dir\="/path/to/existing/profile",  \# by specifying it, it won't be automatically cleaned up when finished
    browser\_executable\_path\="/path/to/some/other/browser",
    browser\_args\=\['--some-browser-arg=true', '--some-other-option'\],
    lang\="en-US"   \# this could set iso-language-code in navigator, not recommended to change
)
tab \= await browser.get('https://somewebsite.com')

Alternative custom options[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/quickstart.html#alternative-custom-options "Link to this heading")

========================================================================================================================================================

I’ll leave out the async boilerplate here

from nodriver import \*

config \= Config()
config.headless \= False
config.user\_data\_dir\="/path/to/existing/profile",  \# by specifying it, it won't be automatically cleaned up when finished
config.browser\_executable\_path\="/path/to/some/other/browser",
config.browser\_args\=\['--some-browser-arg=true', '--some-other-option'\],
config.lang\="en-US"   \# this could set iso-language-code in navigator, not recommended to change
)

Proxies (socks5, authenticated too)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/quickstart.html#proxies-socks5-authenticated-too "Link to this heading")

=======================================================================================================================================================================

You can create as many browser contexts, with each a different proxy, while normally you can only use 1 proxy for all your browser windows.

from nodriver import \*
browser \= await start()
proxied\_tab \= await browser.create\_context(
    url: ...
    proxy\_server \= "socks5://myuser:mypass@myproxyhost.local"
    proxy\_bypass\_list \= \["localhost"\]
)
await proxied\_tab.get('https://whatismyip.com')

Concrete example[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/quickstart.html#concrete-example "Link to this heading")

====================================================================================================================================

A more concrete example, which can be found in the ./example/ folder, shows a script to create a twitter account

import asyncio
import random
import string
import logging

logging.basicConfig(level\=30)

import nodriver as uc

months \= \[\
    "january",\
    "february",\
    "march",\
    "april",\
    "may",\
    "june",\
    "july",\
    "august",\
    "september",\
    "october",\
    "november",\
    "december",\
\]

async def main():
    driver \= await uc.start()

    tab \= await driver.get("https://twitter.com")

    \# wait for text to appear instead of a static number of seconds to wait
    \# this does not always work as expected, due to speed.
    print('finding the "create account" button')
    create\_account \= await tab.find("create account", best\_match\=True)

    print('"create account" => click')
    await create\_account.click()

    print("finding the email input field")
    email \= await tab.select("input\[type=email\]")

    \# sometimes, email field is not shown, because phone is being asked instead
    \# when this occurs, find the small text which says "use email instead"
    if not email:
        use\_mail\_instead \= await tab.find("use email instead")
        \# and click it
        await use\_mail\_instead.click()

        \# now find the email field again
        email \= await tab.select("input\[type=email\]")

    randstr \= lambda k: "".join(random.choices(string.ascii\_letters, k\=k))

    \# send keys to email field
    print('filling in the "email" input field')
    await email.send\_keys("".join(\[randstr(8), "@", randstr(8), ".com"\]))

    \# find the name input field
    print("finding the name input field")
    name \= await tab.select("input\[type=text\]")

    \# again, send random text
    print('filling in the "name" input field')
    await name.send\_keys(randstr(8))

    \# since there are 3 select fields on the tab, we can use unpacking
    \# to assign each field
    print('finding the "month" , "day" and "year" fields in 1 go')
    sel\_month, sel\_day, sel\_year \= await tab.select\_all("select")

    \# await sel\_month.focus()
    print('filling in the "month" input field')
    await sel\_month.send\_keys(months\[random.randint(0, 11)\].title())

    \# await sel\_day.focus()
    \# i don't want to bother with month-lengths and leap years
    print('filling in the "day" input field')
    await sel\_day.send\_keys(str(random.randint(0, 28)))

    \# await sel\_year.focus()
    \# i don't want to bother with age restrictions
    print('filling in the "year" input field')
    await sel\_year.send\_keys(str(random.randint(1980, 2005)))

    await tab

    \# let's handle the cookie nag as well
    cookie\_bar\_accept \= await tab.find("accept all", best\_match\=True)
    if cookie\_bar\_accept:
        await cookie\_bar\_accept.click()

    await tab.sleep(1)

    next\_btn \= await tab.find(text\="next", best\_match\=True)
    \# for btn in reversed(next\_btns):
    await next\_btn.mouse\_click()

    print("sleeping 2 seconds")
    await tab.sleep(2)  \# visually see what part we're actually in

    print('finding "next" button')
    next\_btn \= await tab.find(text\="next", best\_match\=True)
    print('clicking "next" button')
    await next\_btn.mouse\_click()

    \# just wait for some button, before we continue
    await tab.select("\[role=button\]")

    print('finding "sign up"  button')
    sign\_up\_btn \= await tab.find("Sign up", best\_match\=True)
    \# we need the second one
    print('clicking "sign up"  button')
    await sign\_up\_btn.click()

    print('the rest of the "implementation" is out of scope')
    \# further implementation outside of scope
    await tab.sleep(10)
    driver.stop()

    \# verification code per mail

if \_\_name\_\_ \== "\_\_main\_\_":
    \# since asyncio.run never worked (for me)
    \# i use
    uc.loop().run\_until\_complete(main())

---

# Browser class - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

Browser class[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/browser.html#browser-class "Link to this heading")

===================================================================================================================================

cookies[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/browser.html#cookies "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can load and save all cookies from the browser.

\# save. when no filepath is given, it is saved in '.session.dat'
await browser.cookies.save()

\# load. when no filepath is given, it is loaded from '.session.dat'
await browser.cookies.load()

\# export for requests or other library
requests\_style\_cookies \= await browser.cookies.get\_all(requests\_cookie\_format\=True)

\# use in requests:
session \= requests.Session()
for cookie in requests\_style\_cookies:
    session.cookies.set\_cookie(cookie)

Browser class[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/browser.html#id1 "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_class_ Browser(_config_, _\*\*kwargs_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/browser.html#Browser)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/browser.html#nodriver.Browser "Link to this definition")

The Browser object is the “root” of the hierarchy and contains a reference to the browser parent process. there should usually be only 1 instance of this.

All opened tabs, extra browser screens and resources will not cause a new Browser process, but rather create additional [`nodriver.Tab`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab "nodriver.Tab")
 objects.

So, besides starting your instance and first/additional tabs, you don’t actively use it a lot under normal conditions.

Tab objects will represent and control

*   tabs (as you know them)
    
*   browser windows (new window)
    
*   iframe
    
*   background processes
    

note: the Browser object is not instantiated by \_\_init\_\_ but using the asynchronous [`nodriver.Browser.create()`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/browser.html#nodriver.Browser.create "nodriver.Browser.create")
 method.

note: in Chromium based browsers, there is a parent process which keeps running all the time, even if there are no visible browser windows. sometimes it’s stubborn to close it, so make sure after using this library, the browser is correctly and fully closed/exited/killed.

_async_ create\_context(_url\='chrome://welcome'_, _new\_tab\=False_, _new\_window\=True_, _dispose\_on\_detach\=True_, _proxy\_server\=None_, _proxy\_bypass\_list\=None_, _origins\_with\_universal\_network\_access\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/browser.html#Browser.create_context)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/browser.html#nodriver.Browser.create_context "Link to this definition")

creates a new browser context - mostly useful if you want to use proxies for different browser instances since chrome usually can only use 1 proxy per browser. socks5 with authentication is supported by using a forwarder proxy, the correct string to use socks proxy with username/password auth is socks://USERNAME:PASSWORD@SERVER:PORT

dispose\_on\_detach – (EXPERIMENTAL) (Optional) If specified, disposes this context when debugging session disconnects. proxy\_server – (EXPERIMENTAL) (Optional) Proxy server, similar to the one passed to –proxy-server proxy\_bypass\_list – (EXPERIMENTAL) (Optional) Proxy bypass list, similar to the one passed to –proxy-bypass-list origins\_with\_universal\_network\_access – (EXPERIMENTAL) (Optional) An optional list of origins to grant unlimited cross-origin access to. Parts of the URL other than those constituting origin are ignored.

Parameters:

*   **new\_window** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **new\_tab** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **url** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **dispose\_on\_detach** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **proxy\_server** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **proxy\_bypass\_list** ([`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) –

    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) –

# Tab class - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

Tab class[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#tab-class "Link to this heading")

=======================================================================================================================

_class_ Tab(_websocket\_url_, _target_, _browser\=None_, _\*\*kwargs_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab "Link to this definition")

[Tab class](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#tab)
 is the controlling mechanism/connection to a ‘target’, for most of us ‘target’ can be read as ‘tab’. however it could also be an iframe, serviceworker or background script for example, although there isn’t much to control for those.

if you open a new window by using `browser.get(..., new_window=True)()` your url will open a new window. this window is a ‘tab’. When you browse to another page, the tab will be the same (it is an browser view).

So it’s important to keep some reference to tab objects, in case you’re done interacting with elements and want to operate on the page level again.

Custom CDP commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#custom-cdp-commands "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Tab object provide many useful and often-used methods. It is also possible to utilize the included cdp classes to to something totally custom.

the cdp package is a set of so-called “domains” with each having methods, events and types. to send a cdp method, for example [`cdp.page.navigate`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/page.html#nodriver.cdp.page.navigate "nodriver.cdp.page.navigate")
, you’ll have to check whether the method accepts any parameters and whether they are required or not.

you can use

`` `python await tab.send(cdp.page.navigate(url='https://yoururlhere')) ` ``

so tab.send() accepts a generator object, which is created by calling a cdp method. this way you can build very detailed and customized commands. (note: finding correct command combo’s can be a time consuming task, luckily i added a whole bunch of useful methods, preferably having the same api’s or lookalikes, as in selenium)

### some useful, often needed and simply required methods[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#some-useful-often-needed-and-simply-required-methods "Link to this heading")

[`find()`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.find "nodriver.Tab.find")
 | find(text)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#find-find-text "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

find and returns a single element by text match. by default returns the first element found. much more powerful is the best\_match flag, although also much more expensive. when no match is found, it will retry for <timeout> seconds (default: 10), so this is also suitable to use as wait condition.

[`find()`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.find "nodriver.Tab.find")
 | find(text, best\_match=True) or find(text, True)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#find-find-text-best-match-true-or-find-text-true "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Much more powerful (and expensive!!) than the above, is the use of the find(text, best\_match=True) flag. It will still return 1 element, but when multiple matches are found, picks the one having the most similar text length. How would that help? For example, you search for “login”, you’d probably want the “login” button element, and not thousands of scripts,meta,headings which happens to contain a string of “login”.

when no match is found, it will retry for <timeout> seconds (default: 10), so this is also suitable to use as wait condition.

[`select()`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.select "nodriver.Tab.select")
 | select(selector)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#select-select-selector "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

find and returns a single element by css selector match. when no match is found, it will retry for <timeout> seconds (default: 10), so this is also suitable to use as wait condition.

[`select_all()`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.select_all "nodriver.Tab.select_all")
 | select\_all(selector)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#select-all-select-all-selector "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

find and returns all elements by css selector match. when no match is found, it will retry for <timeout> seconds (default: 10), so this is also suitable to use as wait condition.

await [`Tab`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab "nodriver.Tab")
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#await-tab "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

calling await tab will do a lot of stuff under the hood, and ensures all references are up to date. also it allows for the script to “breathe”, as it is oftentime faster than your browser or webpage. So whenever you get stuck and things crashes or element could not be found, you should probably let it “breathe” by calling await page and/or await page.sleep()

also, it’s ensuring `url` will be updated to the most recent one, which is quite important in some other methods.

attempts to find the location of given template image in the current viewport the only real use case for this is bot-detection systems. you can find for example the location of a ‘verify’-checkbox, which are hidden from dom using shadow-root’s or workers.

await [`Tab.template_location`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.template_location "nodriver.Tab.template_location")
 (and await [`Tab.verify_cf`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.verify_cf "nodriver.Tab.verify_cf")
)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#await-tab-template-location-and-await-tab-verify-cf "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

attempts to find the location of given template image in the current viewport. the only real use case for this is bot-detection systems. you can find, for example the location of a ‘verify’-checkbox, which are hidden from dom using shadow-root’s or/or workers and cannot be controlled by normal methods.

template\_image can be custom (for example your language, included is english only), but you need to create the template image yourself, which is just a cropped image of the area, see example image, where the target is exactly in the center. template\_image can be custom (for example your language), but you need to create the template image yourself, where the target is exactly in the center.

example (111x71)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#example-111x71 "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

this includes the white space on the left, to make the box center

[![example template image](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_images/template_example.png)](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_images/template_example.png)

### Using other and custom CDP commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#using-other-and-custom-cdp-commands "Link to this heading")

using the included cdp module, you can easily craft commands, which will always return an generator object. this generator object can be easily sent to the [`send()`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.send "nodriver.Tab.send")
 method.

[`send()`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.send "nodriver.Tab.send")
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#send "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

this is probably THE most important method, although you won’t ever call it, unless you want to go really custom. the send method accepts a `cdp` command. Each of which can be found in the cdp section.

when you import \* from this package, cdp will be in your namespace, and contains all domains/actions/events you can act upon.

_async_ activate()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.activate)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.activate "Link to this definition")

active this target (ie: tab,window,page)

add\_handler(_event\_type\_or\_domain_, _handler_)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.add_handler "Link to this definition")

add a handler for given event

if event\_type\_or\_domain is a module instead of a type, it will find all available events and add the handler.

if you want to receive event updates (network traffic are also ‘events’) you can add handlers for those events. handlers can be regular callback functions or async coroutine functions (and also just lamba’s). for example, you want to check the network traffic:

page.add\_handler(cdp.network.RequestWillBeSent, lambda event: print('network event => %s' % event.request))

the next time you make network traffic you will see your console print like crazy.

Parameters:

*   **event\_type\_or\_domain** ([`Union`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Union "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`type`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.13)")\

    \]\]) –

_async_ find(_text_, _best\_match\=True_, _return\_enclosing\_element\=True_, _timeout\=10_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.find)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.find "Link to this definition")

find single element by text can also be used to wait for such element to appear.

Parameters:

*   **text** ([_str_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – text to search for. note: script contents are also considered text
    
*   **best\_match** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
    param best\_match:
    
    when True (default), it will return the element which has the most comparable string length. this could help tremendously, when for example you search for “login”, you’d probably want the login button element, and not thousands of scripts,meta,headings containing a string of “login”. When False, it will return naively just the first match (but is way faster).
    
    type best\_match:
    
    bool
    
*   **timeout** ([_float_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    _,_[_int_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – raise timeout exception when after this many seconds nothing is found.
    

_async_ find\_all(_text_, _timeout\=10_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.find_all)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.find_all "Link to this definition")

find multiple elements by text can also be used to wait for such element to appear.

Parameters:

*   **text** ([_str_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – text to search for. note: script contents are also considered text
    
*   **timeout** ([_float_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    _,_[_int_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – raise timeout exception when after this many seconds nothing is found.
    

Return type:

[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Element`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element "nodriver.core.element.Element")\
\]

_async_ find\_element\_by\_text(_text_, _best\_match\=False_, _return\_enclosing\_element\=True_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.find_element_by_text)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.find_element_by_text "Link to this definition")

finds and returns the first element containing <text>, or best match

Parameters:

*   **text** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **best\_match** ([_bool_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – when True, which is MUCH more expensive (thus much slower), will find the closest match based on length. this could help tremendously, when for example you search for “login”, you’d probably want the login button element, and not thousands of scripts,meta,headings containing a string of “login”.
    
*   **return\_enclosing\_element** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) –
    

Returns:

Return type:

_async_ find\_elements\_by\_text(_text_, _tag\_hint\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.find_elements_by_text)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.find_elements_by_text "Link to this definition")

returns element which match the given text. returns element which match the given text. please note: this may (or will) also return any other element (like inline scripts), which happen to contain that text.

Parameters:

*   **text** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **tag\_hint** ([_str_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – when provided, narrows down search to only elements which match given tag eg: a, div, script, span
    

Returns:

Return type:

_async_ get(_url\='chrome://welcome'_, _new\_tab\=False_, _new\_window\=False_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.get)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.get "Link to this definition")

top level get. utilizes the first tab to retrieve given url.

convenience function known from selenium. this function handles waits/sleeps and detects when DOM events fired, so it’s the safest way of navigating.

Parameters:

*   **url** – the url to navigate to
    
*   **new\_tab** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – open new tab
    
*   **new\_window** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – open new window
    

Returns:

Page

_async_ js\_dumps(_obj\_name_, _return\_by\_value\=True_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.js_dumps)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.js_dumps "Link to this definition")

dump given js object with its properties and values as a dict

note: complex objects might not be serializable, therefore this method is not a “source of thruth”

Parameters:

*   **obj\_name** ([_str_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – the js object to dump
    
*   **return\_by\_value** ([_bool_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – if you want an tuple of cdp objects (returnvalue, errors), set this to False
    

Return type:

[`Union`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Union "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`RemoteObject`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject")\
, [`ExceptionDetails`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails")\
\]\]

Example

x = await self.js\_dumps(‘window’) print(x)

> ‘…{ ‘pageYOffset’: 0, ‘visualViewport’: {}, ‘screenX’: 10, ‘screenY’: 10, ‘outerWidth’: 1050, ‘outerHeight’: 832, ‘devicePixelRatio’: 1, ‘screenLeft’: 10, ‘screenTop’: 10, ‘styleMedia’: {}, ‘onsearch’: None, ‘isSecureContext’: True, ‘trustedTypes’: {}, ‘performance’: {‘timeOrigin’: 1707823094767.9, ‘timing’: {‘connectStart’: 0, ‘navigationStart’: 1707823094768, \]… ‘

_async_ mouse\_click(_x_, _y_, _button\='left'_, _buttons\=1_, _modifiers\=0_, _\_until\_event\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.mouse_click)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.mouse_click "Link to this definition")

native click on position x,y :type y: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
 :param y: :type y: :type x: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
 :param x: :type x: :type button: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
 :param button: str (default = “left”) :type buttons: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\] :param buttons: which button (default 1 = left) :type modifiers: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\] :param modifiers: _(Optional)_ Bit field representing pressed modifier keys.

> Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8 (default: 0).

Parameters:

**\_until\_event** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`type`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]) – internal. event to wait for before returning

Returns:

_async_ mouse\_drag(_source\_point_, _dest\_point_, _relative\=False_, _steps\=1_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.mouse_drag)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.mouse_drag "Link to this definition")

drag mouse from one point to another. holding button pressed you are probably looking for `element.Element.mouse_drag()` method. where you can drag on the element

Parameters:

*   **dest\_point** ([`tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    , [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) –
    
*   **source\_point** ([`tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#tuple "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    , [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) –
    
*   **relative** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – when True, treats point as relative. for example (-100, 200) will move left 100px and down 200px
    
*   **steps** ([_int_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – move in <steps> points, this could make it look more “natural” (default 1), but also a lot slower. for very smooth action use 50-100
    

Returns:

Return type:

_async_ mouse\_move(_x_, _y_, _steps\=10_, _flash\=False_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.mouse_move)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.mouse_move "Link to this definition")

find single element based on css selector string

) – css selector(s)

_async_ query\_selector\_all(_selector_, _\_node\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.query_selector_all)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.query_selector_all "Link to this definition")

equivalent of javascripts document.querySelectorAll. this is considered one of the main methods to use in this package.

it returns all matching [`nodriver.Element`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element "nodriver.Element")
 objects.

Parameters:

*   **selector** ([_str_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")

_async_ save\_screenshot(_filename\='auto'_, _format\='jpeg'_, _full\_page\=False_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.save_screenshot)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.save_screenshot "Link to this definition")

Saves a screenshot of the page. This is not the same as [`Element.save_screenshot`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.save_screenshot "nodriver.Element.save_screenshot")
, which saves a screenshot of a single element only

Parameters:

*   **filename** (_PathLike_) – uses this as the save path
    
*   **format** ([_str_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – jpeg or png (defaults to jpeg)
    
*   **full\_page** ([_bool_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – when False (default) it captures the current viewport. when True, it captures the entire page
    

Returns:

the path/filename of saved screenshot

Return type:

[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")

_async_ send(_cdp\_obj_, _\_is\_update\=False_)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.send "Link to this definition")

send a protocol command. the commands are made using any of the cdp.<domain>.<method>()’s and is used to send custom cdp commands as well.

Parameters:

*   **cdp\_obj** ([`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    , [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \], [`dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    , [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \], [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – the generator object created by a cdp method
    
*   **\_is\_update** – internal flag prevents infinite loop by skipping the registeration of handlers when multiple calls to connection.send() are made
    

Return type:

[`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")

Returns:

_async_ set\_window\_size(_left\=0_, _top\=0_, _width\=1280_, _height\=1024_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.set_window_size)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.set_window_size "Link to this definition")

set window size and position

Parameters:

*   **left** – pixels from the left of the screen to the window top-left corner
    
*   **top** – pixels from the top of the screen to the window top-left corner
    
*   **width** – width of the window in pixels
    
*   **height** – height of the window in pixels
    

Returns:

Return type:

_async_ set\_window\_state(_left\=0_, _top\=0_, _width\=1280_, _height\=720_, _state\='normal'_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.set_window_state)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.set_window_state "Link to this definition")

sets the window size or state.

for state you can provide the full name like minimized, maximized, normal, fullscreen, or something which leads to either of those, like min, mini, mi, max, ma, maxi, full, fu, no, nor in case state is set other than “normal”, the left, top, width, and height are ignored.

Parameters:

*   **left** ([_int_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – desired offset from left, in pixels
    
*   **top** ([_int_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – desired offset from the top, in pixels
    
*   **width** ([_int_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – desired width in pixels
    
*   **height** ([_int_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – desired height in pixels
    
*   **state** ([_str_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
    can be one of the following strings:
    
    *   normal
        
    *   fullscreen
        
    *   maximized
        
    *   minimized

_async_ template\_location(_template\_image\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.template_location)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.template_location "Link to this definition")

attempts to find the location of given template image in the current viewport the only real use case for this is bot-detection systems. you can find for example the location of a ‘verify’-checkbox, which are hidden from dom using shadow-root’s or workers.

template\_image can be custom (for example your language, included is english only), but you need to create the template image yourself, which is just a cropped image of the area, see example image, where the target is exactly in the center. template\_image can be custom (for example your language), but you need to create the template image yourself, where the target is exactly in the center.

### example (111x71)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#id1 "Link to this heading")

this includes the white space on the left, to make the box center

[![example template image](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_images/template_example.png)](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_images/template_example.png)

type template\_image:

[`TypeVar`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.TypeVar "(in Python v3.13)")
(`PathLike`, bound= [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
 | [`Path`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html#pathlib.Path "(in Python v3.13)")
)

param template\_image:

type template\_image:

return:

rtype:

_async_ wait\_for(_selector\=''_, _text\=''_, _timeout\=10_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.wait_for)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.wait_for "Link to this definition")

variant on query\_selector\_all and find\_elements\_by\_text this variant takes either selector or text, and will block until the requested element(s) are found.

it will block for a maximum of <timeout> seconds, after which an TimeoutError will be raised

Parameters:

*   **selector** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – css selector
    
*   **text** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – text
    
*   **timeout** ([`Union`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Union "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    , [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    , [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) –
    

Returns:

Return type:

[Element](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element "nodriver.Element")

Raises:

asyncio.TimeoutError

_property_ websocket_: ClientConnection_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.websocket "Link to this definition")

_async_ xpath(_xpath_, _timeout\=2.5_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/tab.html#Tab.xpath)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.xpath "Link to this definition")

find elements by xpath string. if not immediately found, retries are attempted until timeout is reached (default 2.5 seconds). in case nothing is found, it returns an empty list. It will not raise. this timeout mechanism helps when relying on some element to appear before continuing your script.

\# find all the inline scripts (script elements without src attribute )
await tab.xpath('//script\[not(@src)\]')

\# or here, more complex, but my personal favorite to case-insensitive text search

await tab.xpath('//text()\[ contains( translate(., "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ", "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz"),"test")\]')

Parameters:

*   **xpath** ([_str_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **timeout** ([_float_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – 2.5
    

:return:List\[nodriver.Element\] or \[\] :rtype:

ContraDict class[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#contradict-class "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Many components in this package are built using a base class of [`nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#id0 "nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict")
.

It’s nothing more than a dictionary which has attribute access AND is JSON serializable.

_class_ ContraDict(_\*args_, _\*\*kwargs_)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict "Link to this definition")

directly inherited from dict

accessible by attribute. o.x == o\[‘x’\] This works also for all corner cases.

native json.dumps and json.loads work with it

names like “keys”, “update”, “values” etc won’t overwrite the methods, but will just be available using dict lookup notation obj\[‘items’\] instead of obj.items

all key names are converted to snake\_case hyphen’s (-), dot’s (.) or whitespaces are replaced by underscore (\_)

autocomplete works even if the objects comes from a list

recursive action. dict assignments will be converted too.

clear() → None.  Remove all items from D.[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict.clear "Link to this definition")

copy() → a shallow copy of D[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict.copy "Link to this definition")

fromkeys(_value\=None_, _/_)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict.fromkeys "Link to this definition")

Create a new dictionary with keys from iterable and values set to value.

get(_key_, _default\=None_, _/_)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict.get "Link to this definition")

Return the value for key if key is in the dictionary, else default.

items() → a set-like object providing a view on D's items[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict.items "Link to this definition")

keys() → a set-like object providing a view on D's keys[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict.keys "Link to this definition")

pop(_k_\[, _d_\]) → v, remove specified key and return the corresponding value.[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict.pop "Link to this definition")

If the key is not found, return the default if given; otherwise, raise a KeyError.

popitem()[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict.popitem "Link to this definition")

Remove and return a (key, value) pair as a 2-tuple.

Pairs are returned in LIFO (last-in, first-out) order. Raises KeyError if the dict is empty.

setdefault(_key_, _default\=None_, _/_)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict.setdefault "Link to this definition")

Insert key with a value of default if key is not in the dictionary.

Return the value for key if key is in the dictionary, else default.

update(\[_E_, \]_\*\*F_) → None.  Update D from dict/iterable E and F.[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict.update "Link to this definition")

If E is present and has a .keys() method, then does: for k in E: D\[k\] = E\[k\] If E is present and lacks a .keys() method, then does: for k, v in E: D\[k\] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k in F: D\[k\] = F\[k\]

values() → an object providing a view on D's values[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#nodriver.core._contradict.ContraDict.values "Link to this definition")

Helper functions[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/others_and_helpers.html#module-nodriver.core._contradict "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_async_ save\_to\_dom()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.save_to_dom)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.save_to_dom "Link to this definition")

saves element to dom :return: :rtype:

_async_ remove\_from\_dom()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.remove_from_dom)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.remove_from_dom "Link to this definition")

removes the element from dom

_async_ update(_\_node\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.update)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.update "Link to this definition")

updates element to retrieve more properties. for example this enables [`children`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.children "nodriver.Element.children")
 and [`parent`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.parent "nodriver.Element.parent")
 attributes.

also resolves js opbject which is stored object in [`remote_object`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.remote_object "nodriver.Element.remote_object")

usually you will get element nodes by the usage of

[`Tab.query_selector_all()`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.query_selector_all "nodriver.Tab.query_selector_all")

[`Tab.find_elements_by_text()`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.find_elements_by_text "nodriver.Tab.find_elements_by_text")

those elements are already updated and you can browse through children directly.

The reason for a seperate call instead of doing it at initialization, is because when you are retrieving 100+ elements this becomes quite expensive.

therefore, it is not advised to call this method on a bunch of blocks (100+) at the same time.

Returns:

Return type:

_property_ node[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.node "Link to this definition")

_property_ tree_: [Node](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.Node "nodriver.cdp.dom.Node")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.tree "Link to this definition")

_property_ attrs[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.attrs "Link to this definition")

attributes are stored here, however, you can set them directly on the element object as well. :return: :rtype:

_property_ parent_: [Element](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element "nodriver.core.element.Element")
 | [None](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.parent "Link to this definition")

get the parent element (node) of current element(node) :return: :rtype:

_property_ children_: [List](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[Element](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element "nodriver.core.element.Element")\
\] | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.children "Link to this definition")

returns the elements’ children. those children also have a children property so you can browse through the entire tree as well. :return: :rtype:

_property_ remote\_object_: [RemoteObject](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.remote_object "Link to this definition")

_property_ object\_id_: [RemoteObjectId](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.object_id "Link to this definition")

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.click "Link to this definition")

Click the element.

Returns:

Return type:

_async_ get\_js\_attributes()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.get_js_attributes)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.get_js_attributes "Link to this definition")

_async_ apply(_js\_function_, _return\_by\_value\=True_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.apply)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.apply "Link to this definition")

apply javascript to this element. the given js\_function string should accept the js element as parameter, and can be a arrow function, or function declaration. eg:

> *   ‘(elem) => { elem.value = “blabla”; consolelog(elem); alert(JSON.stringify(elem); } ‘
>     
> *   ‘elem => elem.play()’
>     
> *   function myFunction(elem) { alert(elem) }
>     

Parameters:

*   **js\_function** ([_str_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – the js function definition which received this element.
    
*   **return\_by\_value** –
    

Returns:

Return type:

_async_ get\_position(_abs\=False_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.get_position)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.get_position "Link to this definition")

Return type:

`Position`

_async_ mouse\_click(_button\='left'_, _buttons\=1_, _modifiers\=0_, _\_until\_event\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.mouse_click)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.mouse_click "Link to this definition")

native click (on element) . note: this likely does not work atm, use click() instead

Parameters:

*   **button** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – str (default = “left”)
    
*   **buttons** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – which button (default 1 = left)
    
*   **modifiers** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Bit field representing pressed modifier keys. Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8 (default: 0).
    
*   **\_until\_event** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`type`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – internal. event to wait for before returning
    

Returns:

_async_ click\_mouse(_button\='left'_, _buttons\=1_, _modifiers\=0_, _\_until\_event\=None_)[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.click_mouse "Link to this definition")

native click (on element) . note: this likely does not work atm, use click() instead

Parameters:

*   **button** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – str (default = “left”)
    
*   **buttons** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – which button (default 1 = left)
    
*   **modifiers** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Bit field representing pressed modifier keys. Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8 (default: 0).
    
*   **\_until\_event** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`type`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – internal. event to wait for before returning
    

Returns:

_async_ mouse\_move()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.mouse_move)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.mouse_move "Link to this definition")

moves mouse (not click), to element position. when an element has an hover/mouseover effect, this would trigger it

_async_ mouse\_drag(_destination_, _relative\=False_, _steps\=1_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.mouse_drag)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.mouse_drag "Link to this definition")

drag an element to another element or target coordinates. dragging of elements should be supported by the site of course

Parameters:

*   **destination** ([_Element_](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element "nodriver.Element")
     _or_ _coordinate as x__,__y tuple_) – another element where to drag to, or a tuple (x,y) of ints representing coordinate
    
*   **relative** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – when True, treats coordinate as relative. for example (-100, 200) will move left 100px and down 200px
    
*   **steps** ([_int_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – move in <steps> points, this could make it look more “natural” (default 1), but also a lot slower. for very smooth action use 50-100
    

Returns:

Return type:

_async_ scroll\_into\_view()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.scroll_into_view)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.scroll_into_view "Link to this definition")

scrolls element into view

_async_ clear\_input(_\_until\_event\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.clear_input)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.clear_input "Link to this definition")

clears an input field

_async_ send\_keys(_text_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.send_keys)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.send_keys "Link to this definition")

send text to an input field, or any other html element.

hint, if you ever get stuck where using py:meth:~click does not work, sending the keystroke n or rn or a spacebar work wonders!

Parameters:

**text** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
) – text to send

Returns:

None

_async_ send\_file(_\*file\_paths_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.send_file)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.send_file "Link to this definition")

some form input require a file (upload), a full path needs to be provided. this method sends 1 or more file(s) to the input field.

needles to say, but make sure the field accepts multiple files if you want to send more files. otherwise the browser might crash.

example : await fileinputElement.send\_file(‘c:/temp/image.png’, ‘c:/users/myuser/lol.gif’)

_async_ focus()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.focus)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.focus "Link to this definition")

focus the current element. often useful in form (select) fields

_async_ select\_option()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.select_option)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.select_option "Link to this definition")

for form (select) fields. when you have queried the options you can call this method on the option object. 02/08/2024: fixed the problem where events are not fired when programattically selecting an option.

calling `option.select_option()` will use that option as selected value. does not work in all cases.

_async_ set\_value(_value_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.set_value)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.set_value "Link to this definition")

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.set_text "Link to this definition")

_async_ get\_html()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.get_html)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.get_html "Link to this definition")

_property_ text_: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.text "Link to this definition")

gets the text contents of this element note: this includes text in the form of script content, as those are also just ‘text nodes’

Returns:

Return type:

_property_ text\_all[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.text_all "Link to this definition")

gets the text contents of this element, and it’s children in a concatenated string note: this includes text in the form of script content, as those are also just ‘text nodes’ :return: :rtype:

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.query_selector_all "Link to this definition")

like js querySelectorAll()

like js querySelector()

_async_ save\_screenshot(_filename\='auto'_, _format\='jpeg'_, _scale\=1_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.save_screenshot)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.save_screenshot "Link to this definition")

Saves a screenshot of this element (only) This is not the same as [`Tab.save_screenshot`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/tab.html#nodriver.Tab.save_screenshot "nodriver.Tab.save_screenshot")
, which saves a “regular” screenshot

When the element is hidden, or has no size, or is otherwise not capturable, a RuntimeError is raised

Parameters:

*   **filename** (_PathLike_) – uses this as the save path
    
*   **format** ([_str_](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – jpeg or png (defaults to jpeg)
    
*   **scale** ([`Union`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Union "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    , [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    , [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – the scale of the screenshot, eg: 1 = size as is, 2 = double, 0.5 is half
    

Returns:

the path/filename of saved screenshot

Return type:

[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")

_async_ flash(_duration\=0.5_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.flash)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.flash "Link to this definition")

displays for a short time a red dot on the element (only if the element itself is visible)

Parameters:

*   **coords** (_x__,__y_) – x,y
    
*   **duration** ([`Union`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Union "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    , [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – seconds (default 0.5)
    

Returns:

Return type:

_async_ highlight\_overlay()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.highlight_overlay)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.highlight_overlay "Link to this definition")

highlights the element devtools-style. To remove the highlight, call the method again. :return: :rtype:

_async_ record\_video(_filename\=None_, _folder\=None_, _duration\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.record_video)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.record_video "Link to this definition")

experimental option.

Parameters:

*   **filename** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – the desired filename
    
*   **folder** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – the download folder path
    
*   **duration** ([`Union`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Union "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    , [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    , [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – record for this many seconds and then download
    

on html5 video nodes, you can call this method to start recording of the video.

when any of the follow happens:

*   video ends
    
*   calling videoelement(‘pause’)
    
*   video stops
    

the video recorded will be downloaded.

_async_ is\_recording()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/core/element.html#Element.is_recording)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/classes/element.html#nodriver.Element.is_recording "Link to this definition")
