_class_ ManufacturerData(_key_, _data_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#ManufacturerData)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.ManufacturerData "Link to this definition")

Stores the manufacturer data

key_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.ManufacturerData.key "Link to this definition")

Company identifier [https://bitbucket.org/bluetooth-SIG/public/src/main/assigned\_numbers/company\_identifiers/company\_identifiers.yaml](https://bitbucket.org/bluetooth-SIG/public/src/main/assigned_numbers/company_identifiers/company_identifiers.yaml)
 [https://usb.org/developers](https://usb.org/developers)

data_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.ManufacturerData.data "Link to this definition")

Manufacturer-specific data (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

_class_ ScanRecord(_name\=None_, _uuids\=None_, _appearance\=None_, _tx\_power\=None_, _manufacturer\_data\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#ScanRecord)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.ScanRecord "Link to this definition")

Stores the byte data of the advertisement packet sent by a Bluetooth device.

name_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.ScanRecord.name "Link to this definition")

uuids_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.ScanRecord.uuids "Link to this definition")

appearance_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.ScanRecord.appearance "Link to this definition")

Stores the external appearance description of the device.

tx\_power_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.ScanRecord.tx_power "Link to this definition")

Stores the transmission power of a broadcasting device.

manufacturer\_data_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`ManufacturerData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.ManufacturerData "nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.ManufacturerData")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.ScanRecord.manufacturer_data "Link to this definition")

Key is the company identifier and the value is an array of bytes of manufacturer specific data.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#commands "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

add\_characteristic(_service\_id_, _characteristic\_uuid_, _properties_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#add_characteristic)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.add_characteristic "Link to this definition")

Adds a characteristic with `characteristicUuid` and `properties` to the service represented by `serviceId`.

Parameters:

*   **service\_id** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **characteristic\_uuid** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **properties** ([`CharacteristicProperties`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.CharacteristicProperties "nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.CharacteristicProperties")
    ) –
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Returns:

An identifier that uniquely represents this characteristic.

enable(_state_, _le\_supported_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#enable)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.enable "Link to this definition")

Enable the BluetoothEmulation domain.

Parameters:

*   **state** ([`CentralState`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.CentralState "nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.CentralState")
    ) – State of the simulated central.
    
*   **le\_supported** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – If the simulated central supports low-energy.

simulate\_characteristic\_operation\_response(_characteristic\_id_, _type\__, _code_, _data\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#simulate_characteristic_operation_response)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.bluetooth_emulation.simulate_characteristic_operation_response "Link to this definition")

Simulates the response from the characteristic with `characteristicId` for a characteristic operation of `type`. The `code` value follows the Error Codes from Bluetooth Core Specification Vol 3 Part F 3.4.1.1 Error Response. The `data` is expected to exist when simulating a successful read operation response.

Parameters:

*   **characteristic\_id** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **type** –
    
*   **code** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **data** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_
    

Return type:

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/bluetooth_emulation.html#events "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

# EventBreakpoints - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/event_breakpoints.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

EventBreakpoints[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/event_breakpoints.html#eventbreakpoints "Link to this heading")

===============================================================================================================================================

EventBreakpoints permits setting JavaScript breakpoints on operations and events occurring in native code invoked from JavaScript. Once breakpoint is hit, it is reported through Debugger domain, similarly to regular breakpoints being hit.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/event_breakpoints.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/event_breakpoints.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/event_breakpoints.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/event_breakpoints.html#types "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_There are no types in this module._

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/event_breakpoints.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

disable()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/event_breakpoints.html#disable)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/event_breakpoints.html#nodriver.cdp.event_breakpoints.disable "Link to this definition")

Removes all breakpoints

Return type:

DOMStorage[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#domstorage "Link to this heading")

=============================================================================================================================

Query and modify DOM storage.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#types "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ SerializedStorageKey[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#SerializedStorageKey)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_storage.SerializedStorageKey "Link to this definition")

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_storage.StorageId "Link to this definition")

DOM Storage identifier.

is\_local\_storage_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_storage.StorageId.is_local_storage "Link to this definition")

Whether the storage is local storage (not session storage).

security\_origin_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_storage.StorageId.security_origin "Link to this definition")

Security origin for the storage.

storage\_key_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`SerializedStorageKey`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_storage.SerializedStorageKey "nodriver.cdp.dom_storage.SerializedStorageKey")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_storage.StorageId.storage_key "Link to this definition")

Represents a key by which DOM Storage keys its CachedStorageAreas

_class_ Item(_iterable\=()_, _/_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#Item)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_storage.Item "Link to this definition")

DOM Storage item.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_storage.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

# Extensions - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

Extensions[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#extensions "Link to this heading")

============================================================================================================================

Defines commands and events for browser extensions.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#types "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ StorageArea(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#StorageArea)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#nodriver.cdp.extensions.StorageArea "Link to this definition")

Storage areas.

SESSION _\= 'session'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#nodriver.cdp.extensions.StorageArea.SESSION "Link to this definition")

LOCAL _\= 'local'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#nodriver.cdp.extensions.StorageArea.LOCAL "Link to this definition")

SYNC _\= 'sync'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#nodriver.cdp.extensions.StorageArea.SYNC "Link to this definition")

MANAGED _\= 'managed'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#nodriver.cdp.extensions.StorageArea.MANAGED "Link to this definition")

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#commands "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

clear\_storage\_items(_id\__, _storage\_area_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#clear_storage_items)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#nodriver.cdp.extensions.clear_storage_items "Link to this definition")

Clears extension storage in the given `storageArea`.

Parameters:

*   **id** – ID of extension.

get\_storage\_items(_id\__, _storage\_area_, _keys\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#get_storage_items)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#nodriver.cdp.extensions.get_storage_items "Link to this definition")

Gets data from extension storage in the given `storageArea`. If `keys` is specified, these are used to filter the result.

Parameters:

*   **id** – ID of extension.
    
*   **storage\_area** ([`StorageArea`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#nodriver.cdp.extensions.StorageArea "nodriver.cdp.extensions.StorageArea")
    ) – StorageArea to retrieve data from.

    \[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\

load\_unpacked(_path_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#load_unpacked)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/extensions.html#nodriver.cdp.extensions.load_unpacked "Link to this definition")

Installs an unpacked extension from the filesystem similar to –load-extension CLI flags. Returns extension ID once the extension has been installed. Available if the client is connected using the –remote-debugging-pipe flag and the –enable-unsafe-extension-debugging flag is set.

Parameters:

**path** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
) – Absolute file path.

\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

\], [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

    ) – StorageArea to set data in.

    ) – Values to set.

# DOMDebugger - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_debugger.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

DOMDebugger[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_debugger.html#domdebugger "Link to this heading")

================================================================================================================================

DOM debugging allows setting breakpoints on particular DOM operations and events. JavaScript execution will stop on these operations as if there was a regular breakpoint set.

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_debugger.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_debugger.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_debugger.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_debugger.html#types "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ DOMBreakpointType(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/dom_debugger.html#DOMBreakpointType)

DOM breakpoint type.

NODE\_REMOVED _\= 'node-removed'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_debugger.DOMBreakpointType.NODE_REMOVED "Link to this definition")

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_debugger.html#commands "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

get\_event\_listeners(_object\_id_, _depth\=None_, _pierce\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/dom_debugger.html#get_event_listeners)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_debugger.get_event_listeners "Link to this definition")

Returns event listeners of the given object.

Parameters:

*   **object\_id** ([`RemoteObjectId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId")
    ) – Identifier of the object to return listeners for.
    
*   **depth** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The maximum depth at which Node children should be retrieved, defaults to 1. Use -1 for the entire subtree or provide an integer larger than 0.
    
*   **pierce** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether or not iframes and shadow roots should be traversed when returning the subtree (default is false). Reports listeners for all contexts if pierce is enabled.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`EventListener`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_debugger.EventListener "nodriver.cdp.dom_debugger.EventListener")\
\]\]

Returns:

Array of relevant listeners.

# FileSystem - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/file_system.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

FileSystem[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/file_system.html#filesystem "Link to this heading")

=============================================================================================================================

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/file_system.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/file_system.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/file_system.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/file_system.html#types "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/file_system.html#nodriver.cdp.file_system.File.last_modified "Link to this definition")

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/file_system.html#nodriver.cdp.file_system.File.size "Link to this definition")

Size in bytes

type\__: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")

_class_ BucketFileSystemLocator(_storage\_key_, _path\_components_, _bucket\_name\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/file_system.html#BucketFileSystemLocator)

\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/file_system.html#nodriver.cdp.file_system.BucketFileSystemLocator.path_components "Link to this definition")

Path to the directory using each path component as an array item.

\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\

//developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Storage\_API#storage\_buckets)

Type:

Bucket name. Not passing a `bucketName` will retrieve the default Bucket. (https

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/file_system.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

) –

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Directory`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/file_system.html#nodriver.cdp.file_system.Directory "nodriver.cdp.file_system.Directory")\
\]

Returns:

Returns the directory object at the path.

Browser[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#browser "Link to this heading")

===================================================================================================================

The Browser domain defines methods and events for browser managing.

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#types "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ BrowserContextID[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#BrowserContextID)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.BrowserContextID "Link to this definition")

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.WindowState "Link to this definition")

The state of the browser window.

NORMAL _\= 'normal'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.WindowState.NORMAL "Link to this definition")

MINIMIZED _\= 'minimized'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.WindowState.MINIMIZED "Link to this definition")

MAXIMIZED _\= 'maximized'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.WindowState.MAXIMIZED "Link to this definition")

FULLSCREEN _\= 'fullscreen'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.WindowState.FULLSCREEN "Link to this definition")

_class_ Bounds(_left\=None_, _top\=None_, _width\=None_, _height\=None_, _window\_state\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#Bounds)

\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.Bounds.left "Link to this definition")

top_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\

\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\

\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.Bounds.window_state "Link to this definition")

_class_ PermissionDescriptor(_name_, _sysex\=None_, _user\_visible\_only\=None_, _allow\_without\_sanitization\=None_, _allow\_without\_gesture\=None_, _pan\_tilt\_zoom\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#PermissionDescriptor)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PermissionDescriptor "Link to this definition")

Definition of PermissionDescriptor defined in the Permissions API: [https://w3c.github.io/permissions/#dom-permissiondescriptor](https://w3c.github.io/permissions/#dom-permissiondescriptor)
.

name_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PermissionDescriptor.name "Link to this definition")

Name of permission. See [https://cs.chromium.org/chromium/src/third\_party/blink/renderer/modules/permissions/permission\_descriptor.idl](https://cs.chromium.org/chromium/src/third_party/blink/renderer/modules/permissions/permission_descriptor.idl)
 for valid permission names.

sysex_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PermissionDescriptor.sysex "Link to this definition")

For “midi” permission, may also specify sysex control.

user\_visible\_only_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PermissionDescriptor.user_visible_only "Link to this definition")

For “push” permission, may specify userVisibleOnly. Note that userVisibleOnly = true is the only currently supported type.

allow\_without\_sanitization_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PermissionDescriptor.allow_without_sanitization "Link to this definition")

For “clipboard” permission, may specify allowWithoutSanitization.

allow\_without\_gesture_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PermissionDescriptor.allow_without_gesture "Link to this definition")

true.

Type:

For “fullscreen” permission, must specify allowWithoutGesture

pan\_tilt\_zoom_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PermissionDescriptor.pan_tilt_zoom "Link to this definition")

For “camera” permission, may specify panTiltZoom.

_class_ PrivacySandboxAPI(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#PrivacySandboxAPI)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PrivacySandboxAPI "Link to this definition")

BIDDING\_AND\_AUCTION\_SERVICES _\= 'BiddingAndAuctionServices'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PrivacySandboxAPI.BIDDING_AND_AUCTION_SERVICES "Link to this definition")

TRUSTED\_KEY\_VALUE _\= 'TrustedKeyValue'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PrivacySandboxAPI.TRUSTED_KEY_VALUE "Link to this definition")

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#commands "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

add\_privacy\_sandbox\_coordinator\_key\_config(_api_, _coordinator\_origin_, _key\_config_, _browser\_context\_id\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#add_privacy_sandbox_coordinator_key_config)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.add_privacy_sandbox_coordinator_key_config "Link to this definition")

Configures encryption keys used with a given privacy sandbox API to talk to a trusted coordinator. Since this is intended for test automation only, coordinatorOrigin must be a .test domain. No existing coordinator configuration for the origin may exist.

Parameters:

*   **api** ([`PrivacySandboxAPI`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PrivacySandboxAPI "nodriver.cdp.browser.PrivacySandboxAPI")
    ) –
    
*   **coordinator\_origin** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **key\_config** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **browser\_context\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`BrowserContextID`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.BrowserContextID "nodriver.cdp.browser.BrowserContextID")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ BrowserContext to perform the action in. When omitted, default browser context is used.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

add\_privacy\_sandbox\_enrollment\_override(_url_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#add_privacy_sandbox_enrollment_override)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.add_privacy_sandbox_enrollment_override "Link to this definition")

Allows a site to use privacy sandbox features that require enrollment without the site actually being enrolled. Only supported on page targets.

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.get_histograms "Link to this definition")

    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Requested substring in name. Only histograms which have query as a substring in their name are extracted. An empty or absent query returns all histograms.

    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If true, retrieve delta since last delta call.

\]\]

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.get_version "Link to this definition")

Returns version information.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **protocolVersion** - Protocol version.
    
1.  **product** - Product name.
    
2.  **revision** - Product revision.
    
3.  **userAgent** - User-Agent.
    
4.  **jsVersion** - V8 version.

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.get_window_bounds "Link to this definition")

Get position and size of the browser window.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

**window\_id** ([`WindowID`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.WindowID "nodriver.cdp.browser.WindowID")
) – Browser window id.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Bounds`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.Bounds "nodriver.cdp.browser.Bounds")\
\]

get\_window\_for\_target(_target\_id\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#get_window_for_target)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.get_window_for_target "Link to this definition")

Get the browser window that contains the devtools target.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

**target\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`TargetID`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/target.html#nodriver.cdp.target.TargetID "nodriver.cdp.target.TargetID")\
\]) – _(Optional)_ Devtools agent host id. If called as a part of the session, associated targetId is used.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`WindowID`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.WindowID "nodriver.cdp.browser.WindowID")\
, [`Bounds`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.Bounds "nodriver.cdp.browser.Bounds")\
\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **windowId** - Browser window id.
    
1.  **bounds** - Bounds information of the window. When window state is ‘minimized’, the restored window position and size are returned.

set\_download\_behavior(_behavior_, _browser\_context\_id\=None_, _download\_path\=None_, _events\_enabled\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#set_download_behavior)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.set_download_behavior "Link to this definition")

Set the behavior when downloading a file.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **behavior** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Whether to allow all or deny all download requests, or use default Chrome behavior if available (otherwise deny). `allowAndName` allows download and names files according to their download guids.
    
*   **browser\_context\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`BrowserContextID`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.BrowserContextID "nodriver.cdp.browser.BrowserContextID")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ BrowserContext to set download behavior. When omitted, default browser context is used.
    
*   **download\_path** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The default path to save downloaded files to. This is required if behavior is set to ‘allow’ or ‘allowAndName’.
    
*   **events\_enabled** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether to emit download events (defaults to false).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]

set\_permission(_permission_, _setting_, _origin\=None_, _embedding\_origin\=None_, _browser\_context\_id\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#set_permission)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.set_permission "Link to this definition")

Set permission settings for given requesting and embedding origins.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **permission** ([`PermissionDescriptor`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PermissionDescriptor "nodriver.cdp.browser.PermissionDescriptor")
    ) – Descriptor of permission to override.
    
*   **setting** ([`PermissionSetting`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.PermissionSetting "nodriver.cdp.browser.PermissionSetting")
    ) – Setting of the permission.
    
*   **origin** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Requesting origin the permission applies to, all origins if not specified.
    
*   **embedding\_origin** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Embedding origin the permission applies to. It is ignored unless the requesting origin is present and valid. If the requesting origin is provided but the embedding origin isn’t, the requesting origin is used as the embedding origin.
    
*   **browser\_context\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`BrowserContextID`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.BrowserContextID "nodriver.cdp.browser.BrowserContextID")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Context to override. When omitted, default browser context is used.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

set\_window\_bounds(_window\_id_, _bounds_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#set_window_bounds)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.set_window_bounds "Link to this definition")

Set position and/or size of the browser window.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **window\_id** ([`WindowID`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.WindowID "nodriver.cdp.browser.WindowID")
    ) – Browser window id.
    
*   **bounds** ([`Bounds`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.Bounds "nodriver.cdp.browser.Bounds")
    ) – New window bounds. The ‘minimized’, ‘maximized’ and ‘fullscreen’ states cannot be combined with ‘left’, ‘top’, ‘width’ or ‘height’. Leaves unspecified fields unchanged.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

_class_ DownloadWillBegin(_frame\_id_, _guid_, _url_, _suggested\_filename_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#DownloadWillBegin)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.DownloadWillBegin "Link to this definition")

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Fired when page is about to start a download.

frame\_id_: [`FrameId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/page.html#nodriver.cdp.page.FrameId "nodriver.cdp.page.FrameId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.DownloadWillBegin.frame_id "Link to this definition")

Id of the frame that caused the download to begin.

guid_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.DownloadWillBegin.guid "Link to this definition")

Global unique identifier of the download.

url_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.DownloadWillBegin.url "Link to this definition")

URL of the resource being downloaded.

suggested\_filename_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.DownloadWillBegin.suggested_filename "Link to this definition")

Suggested file name of the resource (the actual name of the file saved on disk may differ).

_class_ DownloadProgress(_guid_, _total\_bytes_, _received\_bytes_, _state_, _file\_path_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#DownloadProgress)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.DownloadProgress "Link to this definition")

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Fired when download makes progress. Last call has `done` == true.

guid_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.DownloadProgress.guid "Link to this definition")

Global unique identifier of the download.

total\_bytes_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.DownloadProgress.total_bytes "Link to this definition")

Total expected bytes to download.

received\_bytes_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.DownloadProgress.received_bytes "Link to this definition")

Total bytes received.

state_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.DownloadProgress.state "Link to this definition")

Download status.

file\_path_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/browser.html#nodriver.cdp.browser.DownloadProgress.file_path "Link to this definition")

If download is “completed”, provides the path of the downloaded file. Depending on the platform, it is not guaranteed to be set, nor the file is guaranteed to exist.

# Inspector - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/inspector.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

Inspector[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/inspector.html#inspector "Link to this heading")

=========================================================================================================================

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/inspector.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/inspector.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/inspector.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/inspector.html#types "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_There are no types in this module._

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/inspector.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

\]

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/inspector.html#events "Link to this heading")

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_class_ Detached(_reason_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/inspector.html#Detached)

# HeadlessExperimental - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

HeadlessExperimental[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#headlessexperimental "Link to this heading")

===========================================================================================================================================================

This domain provides experimental commands only supported in headless mode.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#types "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ ScreenshotParams(_format\_\=None_, _quality\=None_, _optimize\_for\_speed\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#ScreenshotParams)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#nodriver.cdp.headless_experimental.ScreenshotParams "Link to this definition")

Encoding options for a screenshot.

format\__: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#nodriver.cdp.headless_experimental.ScreenshotParams.format_ "Link to this definition")

Image compression format (defaults to png).

quality_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#nodriver.cdp.headless_experimental.ScreenshotParams.quality "Link to this definition")

Compression quality from range \[0..100\] (jpeg and webp only).

optimize\_for\_speed_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#nodriver.cdp.headless_experimental.ScreenshotParams.optimize_for_speed "Link to this definition")

Optimize image encoding for speed, not for resulting size (defaults to false)

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

begin\_frame(_frame\_time\_ticks\=None_, _interval\=None_, _no\_display\_updates\=None_, _screenshot\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#begin_frame)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#nodriver.cdp.headless_experimental.begin_frame "Link to this definition")

Sends a BeginFrame to the target and returns when the frame was completed. Optionally captures a screenshot from the resulting frame. Requires that the target was created with enabled BeginFrameControl. Designed for use with –run-all-compositor-stages-before-draw, see also [https://goo.gle/chrome-headless-rendering](https://goo.gle/chrome-headless-rendering)
 for more background.

Parameters:

*   **frame\_time\_ticks** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Timestamp of this BeginFrame in Renderer TimeTicks (milliseconds of uptime). If not set, the current time will be used.
    
*   **interval** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The interval between BeginFrames that is reported to the compositor, in milliseconds. Defaults to a 60 frames/second interval, i.e. about 16.666 milliseconds.
    
*   **no\_display\_updates** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether updates should not be committed and drawn onto the display. False by default. If true, only side effects of the BeginFrame will be run, such as layout and animations, but any visual updates may not be visible on the display or in screenshots.
    
*   **screenshot** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`ScreenshotParams`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#nodriver.cdp.headless_experimental.ScreenshotParams "nodriver.cdp.headless_experimental.ScreenshotParams")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If set, a screenshot of the frame will be captured and returned in the response. Otherwise, no screenshot will be captured. Note that capturing a screenshot can fail, for example, during renderer initialization. In such a case, no screenshot data will be returned.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **hasDamage** - Whether the BeginFrame resulted in damage and, thus, a new frame was committed to the display. Reported for diagnostic uses, may be removed in the future.
    
1.  **screenshotData** - _(Optional)_ Base64-encoded image data of the screenshot, if one was requested and successfully taken. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)
    

disable()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#disable)

\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]

enable()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#enable)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/headless_experimental.html#nodriver.cdp.headless_experimental.enable "Link to this definition")

# IO - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

IO[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#io "Link to this heading")

====================================================================================================

Input/Output operations for streams produced by DevTools.

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#types "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ StreamHandle[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/io.html#StreamHandle)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#nodriver.cdp.io.StreamHandle "Link to this definition")

This is either obtained from another method or specified as `blob:<uuid>` where `<uuid>` is an UUID of a Blob.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#commands "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

close(_handle_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/io.html#close)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#nodriver.cdp.io.close "Link to this definition")

Close the stream, discard any temporary backing storage.

Parameters:

**handle** ([`StreamHandle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#nodriver.cdp.io.StreamHandle "nodriver.cdp.io.StreamHandle")
) – Handle of the stream to close.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

read(_handle_, _offset\=None_, _size\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/io.html#read)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#nodriver.cdp.io.read "Link to this definition")

Read a chunk of the stream

Parameters:

*   **handle** ([`StreamHandle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#nodriver.cdp.io.StreamHandle "nodriver.cdp.io.StreamHandle")
    ) – Handle of the stream to read.
    
*   **offset** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Seek to the specified offset before reading (if not specified, proceed with offset following the last read). Some types of streams may only support sequential reads.
    
*   **size** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Maximum number of bytes to read (left upon the agent discretion if not specified).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **base64Encoded** - _(Optional)_ Set if the data is base64-encoded
    
1.  **data** - Data that were read.
    
2.  **eof** - Set if the end-of-file condition occurred while reading.
    

resolve\_blob(_object\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/io.html#resolve_blob)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#nodriver.cdp.io.resolve_blob "Link to this definition")

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Returns:

UUID of the specified Blob.

Log[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/log.html#log "Link to this heading")

=======================================================================================================

Provides access to log entries.

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/log.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/log.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/log.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/log.html#types "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ LogEntry(_source_, _level_, _text_, _timestamp_, _category\=None_, _url\=None_, _line\_number\=None_, _stack\_trace\=None_, _network\_request\_id\=None_, _worker\_id\=None_, _args\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/log.html#LogEntry)

Timestamp when this entry was added.

category_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\

\[[`StackTrace`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTrace "nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTrace")\

network\_request\_id_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

# FedCm - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fed_cm.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

FedCm[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fed_cm.html#fedcm "Link to this heading")

==============================================================================================================

This domain allows interacting with the FedCM dialog.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fed_cm.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fed_cm.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fed_cm.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fed_cm.html#types "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ LoginState(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fed_cm.html#LoginState)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fed_cm.html#nodriver.cdp.fed_cm.LoginState "Link to this definition")

Whether this is a sign-up or sign-in action for this account, i.e. whether this account has ever been used to sign in to this RP before.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fed_cm.html#commands "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fed_cm.html#events "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

Performance[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance.html#module-nodriver.cdp.performance "Link to this heading")

===================================================================================================================================================

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance.html#types)
