Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance.html#types "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ Metric(_name_, _value_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/performance.html#Metric)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance.html#nodriver.cdp.performance.Metric "Link to this definition")

Run-time execution metric.

name_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance.html#nodriver.cdp.performance.Metric.name "Link to this definition")

Metric name.

value_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance.html#nodriver.cdp.performance.Metric.value "Link to this definition")

Metric value.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

set\_time\_domain(_time\_domain_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/performance.html#set_time_domain)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance.html#nodriver.cdp.performance.set_time_domain "Link to this definition")

Sets time domain to use for collecting and reporting duration metrics. Note that this must be called before enabling metrics collection. Calling this method while metrics collection is enabled returns an error.

Deprecated since version 1.3.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

**time\_domain** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
) – Time domain

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\

\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Schema[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/schema.html#schema "Link to this heading")

================================================================================================================

This domain is deprecated.

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/schema.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/schema.html#commands)

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/schema.html#types "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ Domain(_name_, _version_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/schema.html#Domain)

Domain name.

version_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/schema.html#nodriver.cdp.schema.Domain.version "Link to this definition")

Domain version.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/schema.html#commands "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ HeapSnapshotObjectId[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#HeapSnapshotObjectId)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.HeapSnapshotObjectId "Link to this definition")

Heap snapshot object id.

_class_ SamplingHeapProfileNode(_call\_frame_, _self\_size_, _id\__, _children_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#SamplingHeapProfileNode)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.SamplingHeapProfileNode "Link to this definition")

Sampling Heap Profile node. Holds callsite information, allocation statistics and child nodes.

call\_frame_: [`CallFrame`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.CallFrame "nodriver.cdp.runtime.CallFrame")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.SamplingHeapProfileNode.call_frame "Link to this definition")

Function location.

self\_size_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.SamplingHeapProfileNode.self_size "Link to this definition")

Allocations size in bytes for the node excluding children.

id\__: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.SamplingHeapProfileNode.id_ "Link to this definition")

Node id. Ids are unique across all profiles collected between startSampling and stopSampling.

children_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`SamplingHeapProfileNode`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.SamplingHeapProfileNode "nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.SamplingHeapProfileNode")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.SamplingHeapProfileNode.children "Link to this definition")

Child nodes.

_class_ SamplingHeapProfileSample(_size_, _node\_id_, _ordinal_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#SamplingHeapProfileSample)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.SamplingHeapProfileSample "Link to this definition")

A single sample from a sampling profile.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.SamplingHeapProfileSample.size "Link to this definition")

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.SamplingHeapProfileSample.node_id "Link to this definition")

ordinal_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.SamplingHeapProfileSample.ordinal "Link to this definition")

Time-ordered sample ordinal number. It is unique across all profiles retrieved between startSampling and stopSampling.

start\_sampling(_sampling\_interval\=None_, _include\_objects\_collected\_by\_major\_gc\=None_, _include\_objects\_collected\_by\_minor\_gc\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#start_sampling)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.start_sampling "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

*   **sampling\_interval** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Average sample interval in bytes. Poisson distribution is used for the intervals. The default value is 32768 bytes.
    
*   **include\_objects\_collected\_by\_major\_gc** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ By default, the sampling heap profiler reports only objects which are still alive when the profile is returned via getSamplingProfile or stopSampling, which is useful for determining what functions contribute the most to steady-state memory usage. This flag instructs the sampling heap profiler to also include information about objects discarded by major GC, which will show which functions cause large temporary memory usage or long GC pauses.
    
*   **include\_objects\_collected\_by\_minor\_gc** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ By default, the sampling heap profiler reports only objects which are still alive when the profile is returned via getSamplingProfile or stopSampling, which is useful for determining what functions contribute the most to steady-state memory usage. This flag instructs the sampling heap profiler to also include information about objects discarded by minor GC, which is useful when tuning a latency-sensitive application for minimal GC activity.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

stop\_tracking\_heap\_objects(_report\_progress\=None_, _treat\_global\_objects\_as\_roots\=None_, _capture\_numeric\_value\=None_, _expose\_internals\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#stop_tracking_heap_objects)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.stop_tracking_heap_objects "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

*   **report\_progress** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If true ‘reportHeapSnapshotProgress’ events will be generated while snapshot is being taken when the tracking is stopped.
    
*   **treat\_global\_objects\_as\_roots** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(DEPRECATED)** _(Optional)_ Deprecated in favor of `` `exposeInternals` ``.
    
*   **capture\_numeric\_value** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If true, numerical values are included in the snapshot
    
*   **expose\_internals** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ If true, exposes internals of the snapshot.
    

Return type:

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#events "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_class_ AddHeapSnapshotChunk(_chunk_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#AddHeapSnapshotChunk)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.AddHeapSnapshotChunk "Link to this definition")

chunk_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.AddHeapSnapshotChunk.chunk "Link to this definition")

_class_ HeapStatsUpdate(_stats\_update_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#HeapStatsUpdate)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.HeapStatsUpdate "Link to this definition")

If heap objects tracking has been started then backend may send update for one or more fragments

stats\_update_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.HeapStatsUpdate.stats_update "Link to this definition")

An array of triplets. Each triplet describes a fragment. The first integer is the fragment index, the second integer is a total count of objects for the fragment, the third integer is a total size of the objects for the fragment.

_class_ LastSeenObjectId(_last\_seen\_object\_id_, _timestamp_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#LastSeenObjectId)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.LastSeenObjectId "Link to this definition")

If heap objects tracking has been started then backend regularly sends a current value for last seen object id and corresponding timestamp. If the were changes in the heap since last event then one or more heapStatsUpdate events will be sent before a new lastSeenObjectId event.

last\_seen\_object\_id_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.LastSeenObjectId.last_seen_object_id "Link to this definition")

timestamp_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/heap_profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.heap_profiler.LastSeenObjectId.timestamp "Link to this definition")

Memory[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#memory "Link to this heading")

================================================================================================================

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#types "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ PressureLevel(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#PressureLevel)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#nodriver.cdp.memory.PressureLevel "Link to this definition")

Memory pressure level.

MODERATE _\= 'moderate'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#nodriver.cdp.memory.PressureLevel.MODERATE "Link to this definition")

CRITICAL _\= 'critical'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#nodriver.cdp.memory.PressureLevel.CRITICAL "Link to this definition")

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#nodriver.cdp.memory.SamplingProfileNode.size "Link to this definition")

Size of the sampled allocation.

total_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#nodriver.cdp.memory.SamplingProfileNode.total "Link to this definition")

Total bytes attributed to this sample.

stack_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#nodriver.cdp.memory.SamplingProfileNode.stack "Link to this definition")

Execution stack at the point of allocation.

Object count.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/memory.html#commands "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

# Media - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

Media[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#media "Link to this heading")

=============================================================================================================

This domain allows detailed inspection of media elements.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#types "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ PlayerId[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/media.html#PlayerId)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerId "Link to this definition")

Players will get an ID that is unique within the agent context.

_class_ Timestamp(_x\=0_, _/_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/media.html#Timestamp)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.Timestamp "Link to this definition")

_class_ PlayerMessage(_level_, _message_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/media.html#PlayerMessage)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerMessage "Link to this definition")

Have one type per entry in MediaLogRecord::Type Corresponds to kMessage

level_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerMessage.level "Link to this definition")

Keep in sync with MediaLogMessageLevel We are currently keeping the message level ‘error’ separate from the PlayerError type because right now they represent different things, this one being a DVLOG(ERROR) style log message that gets printed based on what log level is selected in the UI, and the other is a representation of a media::PipelineStatus object. Soon however we’re going to be moving away from using PipelineStatus for errors and introducing a new error type which should hopefully let us integrate the error log level into the PlayerError type.

message_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerMessage.message "Link to this definition")

_class_ PlayerProperty(_name_, _value_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/media.html#PlayerProperty)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerProperty "Link to this definition")

Corresponds to kMediaPropertyChange

name_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerProperty.name "Link to this definition")

value_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerProperty.value "Link to this definition")

_class_ PlayerEvent(_timestamp_, _value_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/media.html#PlayerEvent)

Corresponds to kMediaEventTriggered

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerEvent.timestamp "Link to this definition")

_class_ PlayerErrorSourceLocation(_file_, _line_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/media.html#PlayerErrorSourceLocation)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerErrorSourceLocation "Link to this definition")

Represents logged source line numbers reported in an error. NOTE: file and line are from chromium c++ implementation code, not js.

file_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerErrorSourceLocation.file "Link to this definition")

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerErrorSourceLocation.line "Link to this definition")

_class_ PlayerError(_error\_type_, _code_, _stack_, _cause_, _data_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/media.html#PlayerError)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerError "Link to this definition")

Corresponds to kMediaError

error\_type_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerError.error_type "Link to this definition")

code_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerError.code "Link to this definition")

Code is the numeric enum entry for a specific set of error codes, such as PipelineStatusCodes in media/base/pipeline\_status.h

stack_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`PlayerErrorSourceLocation`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerErrorSourceLocation "nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerErrorSourceLocation")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerError.stack "Link to this definition")

A trace of where this error was caused / where it passed through.

cause_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`PlayerError`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerError "nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerError")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerError.cause "Link to this definition")

Errors potentially have a root cause error, ie, a DecoderError might be caused by an WindowsError

data_: [`dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerError.data "Link to this definition")

Extra data attached to an error, such as an HRESULT, Video Codec, etc.

_class_ Player(_player\_id_, _dom\_node\_id\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/media.html#Player)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.Player "Link to this definition")

player\_id_: [`PlayerId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerId "nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.Player.player_id "Link to this definition")

dom\_node\_id_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`BackendNodeId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.BackendNodeId "nodriver.cdp.dom.BackendNodeId")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.Player.dom_node_id "Link to this definition")

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

disable()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/media.html#disable)

, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#events "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_class_ PlayerPropertiesChanged(_player\_id_, _properties_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/media.html#PlayerPropertiesChanged)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerPropertiesChanged "Link to this definition")

This can be called multiple times, and can be used to set / override / remove player properties. A null propValue indicates removal.

_class_ PlayerEventsAdded(_player\_id_, _events_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/media.html#PlayerEventsAdded)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerEventsAdded "Link to this definition")

Send events as a list, allowing them to be batched on the browser for less congestion. If batched, events must ALWAYS be in chronological order.

\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerEventsAdded.events "Link to this definition")

_class_ PlayerCreated(_player_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/media.html#PlayerCreated)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/media.html#nodriver.cdp.media.PlayerCreated "Link to this definition")

Called whenever a player is created, or when a new agent joins and receives a list of active players. If an agent is restored, it will receive one event for each active player.

# PerformanceTimeline - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

PerformanceTimeline[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#performancetimeline "Link to this heading")

========================================================================================================================================================

Reporting of performance timeline events, as specified in [https://w3c.github.io/performance-timeline/#dom-performanceobserver](https://w3c.github.io/performance-timeline/#dom-performanceobserver)
.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#types "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ LargestContentfulPaint(_render\_time_, _load\_time_, _size_, _element\_id\=None_, _url\=None_, _node\_id\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#LargestContentfulPaint)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.LargestContentfulPaint "Link to this definition")

See [https://github.com/WICG/LargestContentfulPaint](https://github.com/WICG/LargestContentfulPaint)
 and largest\_contentful\_paint.idl

render\_time_: [`TimeSinceEpoch`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.TimeSinceEpoch "nodriver.cdp.network.TimeSinceEpoch")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.LargestContentfulPaint.render_time "Link to this definition")

load\_time_: [`TimeSinceEpoch`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.TimeSinceEpoch "nodriver.cdp.network.TimeSinceEpoch")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.LargestContentfulPaint.load_time "Link to this definition")

size_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.LargestContentfulPaint.size "Link to this definition")

The number of pixels being painted.

element\_id_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.LargestContentfulPaint.element_id "Link to this definition")

The id attribute of the element, if available.

url_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.LargestContentfulPaint.url "Link to this definition")

The URL of the image (may be trimmed).

node\_id_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`BackendNodeId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.BackendNodeId "nodriver.cdp.dom.BackendNodeId")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.LargestContentfulPaint.node_id "Link to this definition")

_class_ LayoutShift(_value_, _had\_recent\_input_, _last\_input\_time_, _sources_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#LayoutShift)

_class_ TimelineEvent(_frame\_id_, _type\__, _name_, _time_, _duration\=None_, _lcp\_details\=None_, _layout\_shift\_details\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#TimelineEvent)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.TimelineEvent "Link to this definition")

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.TimelineEvent.frame_id "Link to this definition")

Identifies the frame that this event is related to. Empty for non-frame targets.

type\__: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.TimelineEvent.type_ "Link to this definition")

//w3c.github.io/performance-timeline/#dom-performanceentry-entrytype This determines which of the optional “details” fields is present.

Type:

The event type, as specified in https

name_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.TimelineEvent.name "Link to this definition")

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.TimelineEvent.time "Link to this definition")

Time in seconds since Epoch, monotonically increasing within document lifetime.

duration_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.TimelineEvent.duration "Link to this definition")

Event duration, if applicable.

lcp\_details_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`LargestContentfulPaint`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.LargestContentfulPaint "nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.LargestContentfulPaint")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.TimelineEvent.lcp_details "Link to this definition")

\[[`LayoutShift`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.LayoutShift "nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.LayoutShift")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.TimelineEvent.layout_shift_details "Link to this definition")

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#commands "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

enable(_event\_types_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#enable)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.enable "Link to this definition")

Previously buffered events would be reported before method returns. See also: timelineEventAdded

Parameters:

**event\_types** ([`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]) – The types of event to report, as specified in [https://w3c.github.io/performance-timeline/#dom-performanceentry-entrytype](https://w3c.github.io/performance-timeline/#dom-performanceentry-entrytype)
 The specified filter overrides any previous filters, passing empty filter disables recording. Note that not all types exposed to the web platform are currently supported.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#events "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_class_ TimelineEventAdded(_event_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#TimelineEventAdded)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.TimelineEventAdded "Link to this definition")

Sent when a performance timeline event is added. See reportPerformanceTimeline method.

event_: [`TimelineEvent`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.TimelineEvent "nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.TimelineEvent")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/performance_timeline.html#nodriver.cdp.performance_timeline.TimelineEventAdded.event "Link to this definition")

# Fetch - nodriver documentation

Toggle table of contents sidebar

Fetch[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#fetch "Link to this heading")

=============================================================================================================

A domain for letting clients substitute browser’s network layer with client code.

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#types "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ RequestId[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#RequestId)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId "Link to this definition")

Unique request identifier. Note that this does not identify individual HTTP requests that are part of a network request.

_class_ RequestStage(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#RequestStage)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestStage "Link to this definition")

Stages of the request to handle. Request will intercept before the request is sent. Response will intercept after the response is received (but before response body is received).

REQUEST _\= 'Request'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestStage.REQUEST "Link to this definition")

RESPONSE _\= 'Response'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestStage.RESPONSE "Link to this definition")

_class_ RequestPattern(_url\_pattern\=None_, _resource\_type\=None_, _request\_stage\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#RequestPattern)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPattern "Link to this definition")

url\_pattern_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPattern.url_pattern "Link to this definition")

Wildcards (`'*'` -> zero or more, `'?'` -> exactly one) are allowed. Escape character is backslash. Omitting is equivalent to `"*"`.

resource\_type_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`ResourceType`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.ResourceType "nodriver.cdp.network.ResourceType")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPattern.resource_type "Link to this definition")

If set, only requests for matching resource types will be intercepted.

request\_stage_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RequestStage`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestStage "nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestStage")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPattern.request_stage "Link to this definition")

Stage at which to begin intercepting requests. Default is Request.

_class_ HeaderEntry(_name_, _value_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#HeaderEntry)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.HeaderEntry "Link to this definition")

Response HTTP header entry

name_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.HeaderEntry.name "Link to this definition")

value_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.HeaderEntry.value "Link to this definition")

_class_ AuthChallenge(_origin_, _scheme_, _realm_, _source\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#AuthChallenge)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthChallenge "Link to this definition")

Authorization challenge for HTTP status code 401 or 407.

origin_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthChallenge.origin "Link to this definition")

Origin of the challenger.

scheme_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthChallenge.scheme "Link to this definition")

The authentication scheme used, such as basic or digest

realm_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthChallenge.realm "Link to this definition")

The realm of the challenge. May be empty.

source_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthChallenge.source "Link to this definition")

Source of the authentication challenge.

_class_ AuthChallengeResponse(_response_, _username\=None_, _password\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#AuthChallengeResponse)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthChallengeResponse "Link to this definition")

Response to an AuthChallenge.

response_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthChallengeResponse.response "Link to this definition")

The decision on what to do in response to the authorization challenge. Default means deferring to the default behavior of the net stack, which will likely either the Cancel authentication or display a popup dialog box.

username_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthChallengeResponse.username "Link to this definition")

The username to provide, possibly empty. Should only be set if response is ProvideCredentials.

password_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthChallengeResponse.password "Link to this definition")

The password to provide, possibly empty. Should only be set if response is ProvideCredentials.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

continue\_request(_request\_id_, _url\=None_, _method\=None_, _post\_data\=None_, _headers\=None_, _intercept\_response\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#continue_request)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.continue_request "Link to this definition")

Continues the request, optionally modifying some of its parameters.

Parameters:

*   **request\_id** ([`RequestId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId "nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId")
    ) – An id the client received in requestPaused event.
    
*   **url** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If set, the request url will be modified in a way that’s not observable by page.
    
*   **method** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If set, the request method is overridden.
    
*   **post\_data** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If set, overrides the post data in the request. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)
    
*   **headers** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \[[`HeaderEntry`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.HeaderEntry "nodriver.cdp.fetch.HeaderEntry")\
    \]\]) – _(Optional)_ If set, overrides the request headers. Note that the overrides do not extend to subsequent redirect hops, if a redirect happens. Another override may be applied to a different request produced by a redirect.
    
*   **intercept\_response** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ If set, overrides response interception behavior for this request.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

continue\_response(_request\_id_, _response\_code\=None_, _response\_phrase\=None_, _response\_headers\=None_, _binary\_response\_headers\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#continue_response)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.continue_response "Link to this definition")

Continues loading of the paused response, optionally modifying the response headers. If either responseCode or headers are modified, all of them must be present.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **request\_id** ([`RequestId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId "nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId")
    ) – An id the client received in requestPaused event.
    
*   **response\_code** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ An HTTP response code. If absent, original response code will be used.
    
*   **response\_phrase** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ A textual representation of responseCode. If absent, a standard phrase matching responseCode is used.
    
*   **response\_headers** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \[[`HeaderEntry`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.HeaderEntry "nodriver.cdp.fetch.HeaderEntry")\
    \]\]) – _(Optional)_ Response headers. If absent, original response headers will be used.
    
*   **binary\_response\_headers** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Alternative way of specifying response headers as a 0-separated series of name: value pairs. Prefer the above method unless you need to represent some non-UTF8 values that can’t be transmitted over the protocol as text. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

enable(_patterns\=None_, _handle\_auth\_requests\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#enable)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.enable "Link to this definition")

Enables issuing of requestPaused events. A request will be paused until client calls one of failRequest, fulfillRequest or continueRequest/continueWithAuth.

Parameters:

*   **patterns** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \[[`RequestPattern`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPattern "nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPattern")\
    \]\]) – _(Optional)_ If specified, only requests matching any of these patterns will produce fetchRequested event and will be paused until clients response. If not set, all requests will be affected.
    
*   **handle\_auth\_requests** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If true, authRequired events will be issued and requests will be paused expecting a call to continueWithAuth.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

fail\_request(_request\_id_, _error\_reason_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#fail_request)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.fail_request "Link to this definition")

Causes the request to fail with specified reason.

Parameters:

*   **request\_id** ([`RequestId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId "nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId")
    ) – An id the client received in requestPaused event.
    
*   **error\_reason** ([`ErrorReason`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.ErrorReason "nodriver.cdp.network.ErrorReason")
    ) – Causes the request to fail with the given reason.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

fulfill\_request(_request\_id_, _response\_code_, _response\_headers\=None_, _binary\_response\_headers\=None_, _body\=None_, _response\_phrase\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#fulfill_request)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.fulfill_request "Link to this definition")

Provides response to the request.

Parameters:

*   **request\_id** ([`RequestId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId "nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId")
    ) – An id the client received in requestPaused event.
    
*   **response\_code** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – An HTTP response code.
    
*   **response\_headers** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \[[`HeaderEntry`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.HeaderEntry "nodriver.cdp.fetch.HeaderEntry")\
    \]\]) – _(Optional)_ Response headers.
    
*   **binary\_response\_headers** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Alternative way of specifying response headers as a 0-separated series of name: value pairs. Prefer the above method unless you need to represent some non-UTF8 values that can’t be transmitted over the protocol as text. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)
    
*   **body** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ A response body. If absent, original response body will be used if the request is intercepted at the response stage and empty body will be used if the request is intercepted at the request stage. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)
    
*   **response\_phrase** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ A textual representation of responseCode. If absent, a standard phrase matching responseCode is used.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

get\_response\_body(_request\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#get_response_body)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.get_response_body "Link to this definition")

Causes the body of the response to be received from the server and returned as a single string. May only be issued for a request that is paused in the Response stage and is mutually exclusive with takeResponseBodyForInterceptionAsStream. Calling other methods that affect the request or disabling fetch domain before body is received results in an undefined behavior. Note that the response body is not available for redirects. Requests paused in the \_redirect [received\_](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#id1)
 state may be differentiated by `responseCode` and presence of `location` response header, see comments to `requestPaused` for details.

Parameters:

**request\_id** ([`RequestId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId "nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId")
) – Identifier for the intercepted request to get body for.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **body** - Response body.
    
1.  **base64Encoded** - True, if content was sent as base64.
    

take\_response\_body\_as\_stream(_request\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#take_response_body_as_stream)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.take_response_body_as_stream "Link to this definition")

Returns a handle to the stream representing the response body. The request must be paused in the HeadersReceived stage. Note that after this command the request can’t be continued as is – client either needs to cancel it or to provide the response body. The stream only supports sequential read, IO.read will fail if the position is specified. This method is mutually exclusive with getResponseBody. Calling other methods that affect the request or disabling fetch domain before body is received results in an undefined behavior.

Parameters:

**request\_id** ([`RequestId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId "nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId")
) –

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`StreamHandle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#nodriver.cdp.io.StreamHandle "nodriver.cdp.io.StreamHandle")\
\]

Returns:

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#events "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_class_ RequestPaused(_request\_id_, _request_, _frame\_id_, _resource\_type_, _response\_error\_reason_, _response\_status\_code_, _response\_status\_text_, _response\_headers_, _network\_id_, _redirected\_request\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#RequestPaused)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPaused "Link to this definition")

Issued when the domain is enabled and the request URL matches the specified filter. The request is paused until the client responds with one of continueRequest, failRequest or fulfillRequest. The stage of the request can be determined by presence of responseErrorReason and responseStatusCode – the request is at the response stage if either of these fields is present and in the request stage otherwise. Redirect responses and subsequent requests are reported similarly to regular responses and requests. Redirect responses may be distinguished by the value of `responseStatusCode` (which is one of 301, 302, 303, 307, 308) along with presence of the `location` header. Requests resulting from a redirect will have `redirectedRequestId` field set.

request\_id_: [`RequestId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId "nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPaused.request_id "Link to this definition")

Each request the page makes will have a unique id.

request_: [`Request`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.Request "nodriver.cdp.network.Request")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPaused.request "Link to this definition")

The details of the request.

frame\_id_: [`FrameId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/page.html#nodriver.cdp.page.FrameId "nodriver.cdp.page.FrameId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPaused.frame_id "Link to this definition")

The id of the frame that initiated the request.

resource\_type_: [`ResourceType`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.ResourceType "nodriver.cdp.network.ResourceType")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPaused.resource_type "Link to this definition")

How the requested resource will be used.

response\_error\_reason_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`ErrorReason`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.ErrorReason "nodriver.cdp.network.ErrorReason")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPaused.response_error_reason "Link to this definition")

Response error if intercepted at response stage.

response\_status\_code_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPaused.response_status_code "Link to this definition")

Response code if intercepted at response stage.

response\_status\_text_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPaused.response_status_text "Link to this definition")

Response status text if intercepted at response stage.

response\_headers_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`HeaderEntry`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.HeaderEntry "nodriver.cdp.fetch.HeaderEntry")\
\]\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPaused.response_headers "Link to this definition")

Response headers if intercepted at the response stage.

network\_id_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RequestId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.RequestId "nodriver.cdp.network.RequestId")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPaused.network_id "Link to this definition")

If the intercepted request had a corresponding Network.requestWillBeSent event fired for it, then this networkId will be the same as the requestId present in the requestWillBeSent event.

redirected\_request\_id_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RequestId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId "nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestPaused.redirected_request_id "Link to this definition")

If the request is due to a redirect response from the server, the id of the request that has caused the redirect.

_class_ AuthRequired(_request\_id_, _request_, _frame\_id_, _resource\_type_, _auth\_challenge_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#AuthRequired)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthRequired "Link to this definition")

Issued when the domain is enabled with handleAuthRequests set to true. The request is paused until client responds with continueWithAuth.

request\_id_: [`RequestId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId "nodriver.cdp.fetch.RequestId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthRequired.request_id "Link to this definition")

Each request the page makes will have a unique id.

request_: [`Request`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.Request "nodriver.cdp.network.Request")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthRequired.request "Link to this definition")

The details of the request.

frame\_id_: [`FrameId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/page.html#nodriver.cdp.page.FrameId "nodriver.cdp.page.FrameId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthRequired.frame_id "Link to this definition")

The id of the frame that initiated the request.

resource\_type_: [`ResourceType`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.ResourceType "nodriver.cdp.network.ResourceType")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthRequired.resource_type "Link to this definition")

How the requested resource will be used.

auth\_challenge_: [`AuthChallenge`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthChallenge "nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthChallenge")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/fetch.html#nodriver.cdp.fetch.AuthRequired.auth_challenge "Link to this definition")

Details of the Authorization Challenge encountered. If this is set, client should respond with continueRequest that contains AuthChallengeResponse.

# Tethering - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tethering.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

Tethering[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tethering.html#tethering "Link to this heading")

=========================================================================================================================

The Tethering domain defines methods and events for browser port binding.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tethering.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tethering.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tethering.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tethering.html#types "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_There are no types in this module._

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tethering.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.
