IndexedDB[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#indexeddb "Link to this heading")

==========================================================================================================================

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#types "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ DatabaseWithObjectStores(_name_, _version_, _object\_stores_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#DatabaseWithObjectStores)

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#nodriver.cdp.indexed_db.DatabaseWithObjectStores.version "Link to this definition")

Database version (type is not ‘integer’, as the standard requires the version number to be ‘unsigned long long’)

object\_stores_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")

\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#nodriver.cdp.indexed_db.DatabaseWithObjectStores.object_stores "Link to this definition")

Object stores in this database.

_class_ ObjectStore(_name_, _key\_path_, _auto\_increment_, _indexes_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#ObjectStore)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#nodriver.cdp.indexed_db.ObjectStore "Link to this definition")

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#commands "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

clear\_object\_store(_database\_name_, _object\_store\_name_, _security\_origin\=None_, _storage\_key\=None_, _storage\_bucket\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#clear_object_store)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#nodriver.cdp.indexed_db.clear_object_store "Link to this definition")

Clears all entries from an object store.

Parameters:

*   **security\_origin** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ At least and at most one of securityOrigin, storageKey, or storageBucket must be specified. Security origin.
    
*   **storage\_key** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Storage key.
    
*   **storage\_bucket** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`StorageBucket`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/storage.html#nodriver.cdp.storage.StorageBucket "nodriver.cdp.storage.StorageBucket")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Storage bucket. If not specified, it uses the default bucket.
    
*   **database\_name** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Database name.
    
*   **object\_store\_name** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Object store name.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")

\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **entriesCount** - the entries count
    
1.  **keyGeneratorValue** - the current value of key generator, to become the next inserted key into the object store. Valid if objectStore.autoIncrement is true.
    

request\_data(_database\_name_, _object\_store\_name_, _index\_name_, _skip\_count_, _page\_size_, _security\_origin\=None_, _storage\_key\=None_, _storage\_bucket\=None_, _key\_range\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#request_data)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#nodriver.cdp.indexed_db.request_data "Link to this definition")

Requests data from object store or index.

Parameters:

*   **security\_origin** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ At least and at most one of securityOrigin, storageKey, or storageBucket must be specified. Security origin.
    
*   **storage\_key** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Storage key.
    
*   **storage\_bucket** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`StorageBucket`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/storage.html#nodriver.cdp.storage.StorageBucket "nodriver.cdp.storage.StorageBucket")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Storage bucket. If not specified, it uses the default bucket.
    
*   **database\_name** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Database name.
    
*   **object\_store\_name** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Object store name.
    
*   **index\_name** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Index name, empty string for object store data requests.
    
*   **skip\_count** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Number of records to skip.
    
*   **page\_size** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Number of records to fetch.
    
*   **key\_range** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`KeyRange`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#nodriver.cdp.indexed_db.KeyRange "nodriver.cdp.indexed_db.KeyRange")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Key range.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`DataEntry`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#nodriver.cdp.indexed_db.DataEntry "nodriver.cdp.indexed_db.DataEntry")\
\], [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **objectStoreDataEntries** - Array of object store data entries.
    
1.  **hasMore** - If true, there are more entries to fetch in the given range.
    

request\_database(_database\_name_, _security\_origin\=None_, _storage\_key\=None_, _storage\_bucket\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/indexed_db.html#request_database)

LayerTree[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#layertree "Link to this heading")

==========================================================================================================================

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#types "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

Unique snapshot identifier.

_class_ PaintProfile(_iterable\=()_, _/_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#PaintProfile)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#nodriver.cdp.layer_tree.PaintProfile "Link to this definition")

Array of timings, one per paint step.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#commands "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

compositing\_reasons(_layer\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#compositing_reasons)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#nodriver.cdp.layer_tree.compositing_reasons "Link to this definition")

Provides the reasons why the given layer was composited.

Parameters:

**layer\_id** ([`LayerId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#nodriver.cdp.layer_tree.LayerId "nodriver.cdp.layer_tree.LayerId")
) – The id of the layer for which we want to get the reasons it was composited.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **compositingReasons** - A list of strings specifying reasons for the given layer to become composited.
    
1.  **compositingReasonIds** - A list of strings specifying reason IDs for the given layer to become composited.

profile\_snapshot(_snapshot\_id_, _min\_repeat\_count\=None_, _min\_duration\=None_, _clip\_rect\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#profile_snapshot)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#nodriver.cdp.layer_tree.profile_snapshot "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

*   **snapshot\_id** ([`SnapshotId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#nodriver.cdp.layer_tree.SnapshotId "nodriver.cdp.layer_tree.SnapshotId")
    ) – The id of the layer snapshot.
    
*   **min\_repeat\_count** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The maximum number of times to replay the snapshot (1, if not specified).
    
*   **min\_duration** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The minimum duration (in seconds) to replay the snapshot.
    
*   **clip\_rect** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`Rect`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.Rect "nodriver.cdp.dom.Rect")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The clip rectangle to apply when replaying the snapshot.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`PaintProfile`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#nodriver.cdp.layer_tree.PaintProfile "nodriver.cdp.layer_tree.PaintProfile")\
\]\]

Returns:

The array of paint profiles, one per run.

replay\_snapshot(_snapshot\_id_, _from\_step\=None_, _to\_step\=None_, _scale\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#replay_snapshot)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#nodriver.cdp.layer_tree.replay_snapshot "Link to this definition")

Replays the layer snapshot and returns the resulting bitmap.

Parameters:

*   **snapshot\_id** ([`SnapshotId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#nodriver.cdp.layer_tree.SnapshotId "nodriver.cdp.layer_tree.SnapshotId")
    ) – The id of the layer snapshot.
    
*   **from\_step** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The first step to replay from (replay from the very start if not specified).
    
*   **to\_step** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The last step to replay to (replay till the end if not specified).
    
*   **scale** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The scale to apply while replaying (defaults to 1).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Returns:

A data: URL for resulting image.

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/layer_tree.html#events "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

# PWA - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

PWA[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#pwa "Link to this heading")

=======================================================================================================

This domain allows interacting with the browser to control PWAs.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#types "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ FileHandlerAccept(_media\_type_, _file\_extensions_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#FileHandlerAccept)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.FileHandlerAccept "Link to this definition")

The following types are the replica of [https://crsrc.org/c/chrome/browser/web\_applications/proto/web\_app\_os\_integration\_state.proto;drc=9910d3be894c8f142c977ba1023f30a656bc13fc;l=67](https://crsrc.org/c/chrome/browser/web_applications/proto/web_app_os_integration_state.proto;drc=9910d3be894c8f142c977ba1023f30a656bc13fc;l=67)

media\_type_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.FileHandlerAccept.media_type "Link to this definition")

New name of the mimetype according to [https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml](https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml)

file\_extensions_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.FileHandlerAccept.file_extensions "Link to this definition")

_class_ FileHandler(_action_, _accepts_, _display\_name_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#FileHandler)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.FileHandler "Link to this definition")

action_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.FileHandler.action "Link to this definition")

accepts_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`FileHandlerAccept`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.FileHandlerAccept "nodriver.cdp.pwa.FileHandlerAccept")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.FileHandler.accepts "Link to this definition")

display\_name_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.FileHandler.display_name "Link to this definition")

_class_ DisplayMode(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#DisplayMode)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.DisplayMode "Link to this definition")

If user prefers opening the app in browser or an app window.

STANDALONE _\= 'standalone'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.DisplayMode.STANDALONE "Link to this definition")

BROWSER _\= 'browser'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.DisplayMode.BROWSER "Link to this definition")

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

change\_app\_user\_settings(_manifest\_id_, _link\_capturing\=None_, _display\_mode\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#change_app_user_settings)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.change_app_user_settings "Link to this definition")

Changes user settings of the web app identified by its manifestId. If the app was not installed, this command returns an error. Unset parameters will be ignored; unrecognized values will cause an error.

Unlike the ones defined in the manifest files of the web apps, these settings are provided by the browser and controlled by the users, they impact the way the browser handling the web apps.

See the comment of each parameter.

Parameters:

*   **manifest\_id** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **link\_capturing** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If user allows the links clicked on by the user in the app’s scope, or extended scope if the manifest has scope extensions and the flags ``` `DesktopPWAsLinkCapturingWithScopeExtensions`` ``` and ``` ``WebAppEnableScopeExtensions` ``` are enabled. Note, the API does not support resetting the linkCapturing to the initial value, uninstalling and installing the web app again will reset it. TODO(crbug.com/339453269): Setting this value on ChromeOS is not supported yet.
    
*   **display\_mode** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`DisplayMode`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.DisplayMode "nodriver.cdp.pwa.DisplayMode")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

\[[`FileHandler`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.FileHandler "nodriver.cdp.pwa.FileHandler")\

install(_manifest\_id_, _install\_url\_or\_bundle\_url\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#install)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.install "Link to this definition")

Installs the given manifest identity, optionally using the given installUrlOrBundleUrl

IWA-specific install description: manifestId corresponds to isolated-app:// + web\_package::SignedWebBundleId

File installation mode: The installUrlOrBundleUrl can be either [file://](file:///)
 or http(s):// pointing to a signed web bundle (.swbn). In this case SignedWebBundleId must correspond to The .swbn file’s signing key.

Dev proxy installation mode: installUrlOrBundleUrl must be http(s):// that serves dev mode IWA. web\_package::SignedWebBundleId must be of type dev proxy.

The advantage of dev proxy mode is that all changes to IWA automatically will be reflected in the running app without reinstallation.

To generate bundle id for proxy mode: 1. Generate 32 random bytes. 2. Add a specific suffix at the end following the documentation

> [https://github.com/WICG/isolated-web-apps/blob/main/Scheme.md#suffix](https://github.com/WICG/isolated-web-apps/blob/main/Scheme.md#suffix)

3.  Encode the entire sequence using Base32 without padding.
    

If Chrome is not in IWA dev mode, the installation will fail, regardless of the state of the allowlist.

Parameters:

*   **manifest\_id** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **install\_url\_or\_bundle\_url** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The location of the app or bundle overriding the one derived from the manifestId.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]

launch\_files\_in\_app(_manifest\_id_, _files_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#launch_files_in_app)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/pwa.html#nodriver.cdp.pwa.launch_files_in_app "Link to this definition")

Opens one or more local files from an installed web app identified by its manifestId. The web app needs to have file handlers registered to process the files. The API returns one or more page Target.TargetIDs which can be used to attach to via Target.attachToTarget or similar APIs. If some files in the parameters cannot be handled by the web app, they will be ignored. If none of the files can be handled, this API returns an error. If no files are provided as the parameter, this API also returns an error.

According to the definition of the file handlers in the manifest file, one Target.TargetID may represent a page handling one or more files. The order of the returned Target.TargetIDs is not guaranteed.

TODO(crbug.com/339454034): Check the existences of the input files.

Parameters:

*   **manifest\_id** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **files** ([`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) –
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\

\], [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`TargetID`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/target.html#nodriver.cdp.target.TargetID "nodriver.cdp.target.TargetID")\
\]\]

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#types "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ ProfileNode(_id\__, _call\_frame_, _hit\_count\=None_, _children\=None_, _deopt\_reason\=None_, _position\_ticks\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#ProfileNode)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.ProfileNode "Link to this definition")

Profile node. Holds callsite information, execution statistics and child nodes.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.ProfileNode.id_ "Link to this definition")

Unique id of the node.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.ProfileNode.call_frame "Link to this definition")

Function location.

hit\_count_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.ProfileNode.hit_count "Link to this definition")

Number of samples where this node was on top of the call stack.

\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\

Child node ids.

deopt\_reason_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.ProfileNode.deopt_reason "Link to this definition")

An array of source position ticks.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#commands "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

start\_precise\_coverage(_call\_count\=None_, _detailed\=None_, _allow\_triggered\_updates\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#start_precise_coverage)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.start_precise_coverage "Link to this definition")

Enable precise code coverage. Coverage data for JavaScript executed before enabling precise code coverage may be incomplete. Enabling prevents running optimized code and resets execution counters.

Parameters:

*   **call\_count** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Collect accurate call counts beyond simple ‘covered’ or ‘not covered’.
    
*   **detailed** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Collect block-based coverage.
    
*   **allow\_triggered\_updates** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Allow the backend to send updates on its own initiative
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Returns:

Monotonically increasing time (in seconds) when the coverage update was taken in the backend.

\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\

\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]

take\_precise\_coverage()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#take_precise_coverage)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.take_precise_coverage "Link to this definition")

Collect coverage data for the current isolate, and resets execution counters. Precise code coverage needs to have started.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`ScriptCoverage`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.ScriptCoverage "nodriver.cdp.profiler.ScriptCoverage")\
\], [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **result** - Coverage data for the current isolate.
    
1.  **timestamp** - Monotonically increasing time (in seconds) when the coverage update was taken in the backend.
    

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#events "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_class_ PreciseCoverageDeltaUpdate(_timestamp_, _occasion_, _result_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#PreciseCoverageDeltaUpdate)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.PreciseCoverageDeltaUpdate "Link to this definition")

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Reports coverage delta since the last poll (either from an event like this, or from `takePreciseCoverage` for the current isolate. May only be sent if precise code coverage has been started. This event can be trigged by the embedder to, for example, trigger collection of coverage data immediately at a certain point in time.

timestamp_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.PreciseCoverageDeltaUpdate.timestamp "Link to this definition")

Monotonically increasing time (in seconds) when the coverage update was taken in the backend.

occasion_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.PreciseCoverageDeltaUpdate.occasion "Link to this definition")

Identifier for distinguishing coverage events.

result_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`ScriptCoverage`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.ScriptCoverage "nodriver.cdp.profiler.ScriptCoverage")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/profiler.html#nodriver.cdp.profiler.PreciseCoverageDeltaUpdate.result "Link to this definition")

Coverage data for the current isolate.

# DOMSnapshot - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

DOMSnapshot[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#domsnapshot "Link to this heading")

================================================================================================================================

This domain facilitates obtaining document snapshots with DOM, layout, and style information.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#types "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ DOMNode(_node\_type_, _node\_name_, _node\_value_, _backend\_node\_id_, _text\_value\=None_, _input\_value\=None_, _input\_checked\=None_, _option\_selected\=None_, _child\_node\_indexes\=None_, _attributes\=None_, _pseudo\_element\_indexes\=None_, _layout\_node\_index\=None_, _document\_url\=None_, _base\_url\=None_, _content\_language\=None_, _document\_encoding\=None_, _public\_id\=None_, _system\_id\=None_, _frame\_id\=None_, _content\_document\_index\=None_, _pseudo\_type\=None_, _shadow\_root\_type\=None_, _is\_clickable\=None_, _event\_listeners\=None_, _current\_source\_url\=None_, _origin\_url\=None_, _scroll\_offset\_x\=None_, _scroll\_offset\_y\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#DOMNode)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.DOMNode "Link to this definition")

A Node in the DOM tree.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.DOMNode.node_type "Link to this definition")

`Node`’s nodeType.

node\_name_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.DOMNode.node_name "Link to this definition")

_class_ InlineTextBox(_bounding\_box_, _start\_character\_index_, _num\_characters_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#InlineTextBox)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.InlineTextBox "Link to this definition")

Details of post layout rendered text positions. The exact layout should not be regarded as stable and may change between versions.

bounding\_box_: [`Rect`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.Rect "nodriver.cdp.dom.Rect")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.InlineTextBox.bounding_box "Link to this definition")

The bounding box in document coordinates. Note that scroll offset of the document is ignored.

start\_character\_index_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.InlineTextBox.start_character_index "Link to this definition")

The starting index in characters, for this post layout textbox substring. Characters that would be represented as a surrogate pair in UTF-16 have length 2.

num\_characters_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.InlineTextBox.num_characters "Link to this definition")

The number of characters in this post layout textbox substring. Characters that would be represented as a surrogate pair in UTF-16 have length 2.

\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeNode.layout_text "Link to this definition")

\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeNode.style_index "Link to this definition")

Index into the `computedStyles` array returned by `getSnapshot`.

paint\_order_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeNode.paint_order "Link to this definition")

Global paint order index, which is determined by the stacking order of the nodes. Nodes that are painted together will have the same index. Only provided if includePaintOrder in getSnapshot was true.

\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\

Only set for textarea elements, contains the text value.

input\_value_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RareStringData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareStringData "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareStringData")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.NodeTreeSnapshot.input_value "Link to this definition")

Only set for input elements, contains the input’s associated text value.

input\_checked_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RareBooleanData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareBooleanData "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareBooleanData")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.NodeTreeSnapshot.input_checked "Link to this definition")

Only set for radio and checkbox input elements, indicates if the element has been checked

option\_selected_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RareBooleanData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareBooleanData "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareBooleanData")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.NodeTreeSnapshot.option_selected "Link to this definition")

Only set for option elements, indicates if the element has been selected

\[[`RareIntegerData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareIntegerData "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareIntegerData")\

The index of the document in the list of the snapshot documents.

pseudo\_type_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RareStringData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareStringData "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareStringData")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.NodeTreeSnapshot.pseudo_type "Link to this definition")

Type of a pseudo element node.

pseudo\_identifier_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RareStringData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareStringData "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareStringData")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.NodeTreeSnapshot.pseudo_identifier "Link to this definition")

Pseudo element identifier for this node. Only present if there is a valid pseudoType.

is\_clickable_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RareBooleanData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareBooleanData "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareBooleanData")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.NodeTreeSnapshot.is_clickable "Link to this definition")

Whether this DOM node responds to mouse clicks. This includes nodes that have had click event listeners attached via JavaScript as well as anchor tags that naturally navigate when clicked.

current\_source\_url_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RareStringData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareStringData "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareStringData")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.NodeTreeSnapshot.current_source_url "Link to this definition")

The selected url for nodes with a srcset attribute.

origin\_url_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RareStringData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareStringData "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareStringData")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.NodeTreeSnapshot.origin_url "Link to this definition")

The url of the script (if any) that generates this node.

\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeSnapshot.node_index "Link to this definition")

Index of the corresponding node in the `NodeTreeSnapshot` array returned by `captureSnapshot`.

\[[`ArrayOfStrings`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.ArrayOfStrings "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.ArrayOfStrings")\

Array of indexes specifying computed style strings, filtered according to the `computedStyles` parameter passed to `captureSnapshot`.

bounds_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Rectangle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.Rectangle "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.Rectangle")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeSnapshot.bounds "Link to this definition")

The absolute position bounding box.

text_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`StringIndex`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.StringIndex "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.StringIndex")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeSnapshot.text "Link to this definition")

Contents of the LayoutText, if any.

stacking\_contexts_: [`RareBooleanData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareBooleanData "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.RareBooleanData")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeSnapshot.stacking_contexts "Link to this definition")

Stacking context information.

paint\_orders_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeSnapshot.paint_orders "Link to this definition")

Global paint order index, which is determined by the stacking order of the nodes. Nodes that are painted together will have the same index. Only provided if includePaintOrder in captureSnapshot was true.

\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`Rectangle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.Rectangle "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.Rectangle")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeSnapshot.offset_rects "Link to this definition")

The offset rect of nodes. Only available when includeDOMRects is set to true

scroll\_rects_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`Rectangle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.Rectangle "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.Rectangle")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeSnapshot.scroll_rects "Link to this definition")

The scroll rect of nodes. Only available when includeDOMRects is set to true

client\_rects_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`Rectangle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.Rectangle "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.Rectangle")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeSnapshot.client_rects "Link to this definition")

The client rect of nodes. Only available when includeDOMRects is set to true

blended\_background\_colors_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`StringIndex`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.StringIndex "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.StringIndex")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeSnapshot.blended_background_colors "Link to this definition")

The list of background colors that are blended with colors of overlapping elements.

text\_color\_opacities_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeSnapshot.text_color_opacities "Link to this definition")

The list of computed text opacities.

_class_ TextBoxSnapshot(_layout\_index_, _bounds_, _start_, _length_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#TextBoxSnapshot)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.TextBoxSnapshot "Link to this definition")

Table of details of the post layout rendered text positions. The exact layout should not be regarded as stable and may change between versions.

layout\_index_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.TextBoxSnapshot.layout_index "Link to this definition")

Index of the layout tree node that owns this box collection.

bounds_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Rectangle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.Rectangle "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.Rectangle")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.TextBoxSnapshot.bounds "Link to this definition")

The absolute position bounding box.

start_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.TextBoxSnapshot.start "Link to this definition")

The starting index in characters, for this post layout textbox substring. Characters that would be represented as a surrogate pair in UTF-16 have length 2.

length_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.TextBoxSnapshot.length "Link to this definition")

The number of characters in this post layout textbox substring. Characters that would be represented as a surrogate pair in UTF-16 have length 2.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#commands "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

capture\_snapshot(_computed\_styles_, _include\_paint\_order\=None_, _include\_dom\_rects\=None_, _include\_blended\_background\_colors\=None_, _include\_text\_color\_opacities\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#capture_snapshot)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.capture_snapshot "Link to this definition")

Returns a document snapshot, including the full DOM tree of the root node (including iframes, template contents, and imported documents) in a flattened array, as well as layout and white-listed computed style information for the nodes. Shadow DOM in the returned DOM tree is flattened.

Parameters:

*   **computed\_styles** ([`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – Whitelist of computed styles to return.
    
*   **include\_paint\_order** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether to include layout object paint orders into the snapshot.
    
*   **include\_dom\_rects** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether to include DOM rectangles (offsetRects, clientRects, scrollRects) into the snapshot
    
*   **include\_blended\_background\_colors** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Whether to include blended background colors in the snapshot (default: false). Blended background color is achieved by blending background colors of all elements that overlap with the current element.
    
*   **include\_text\_color\_opacities** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Whether to include text color opacity in the snapshot (default: false). An element might have the opacity property set that affects the text color of the element. The final text color opacity is computed based on the opacity of all overlapping elements.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`DocumentSnapshot`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.DocumentSnapshot "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.DocumentSnapshot")\
\], [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **documents** - The nodes in the DOM tree. The DOMNode at index 0 corresponds to the root document.
    
1.  **strings** - Shared string table that all string properties refer to with indexes.

Enables DOM snapshot agent for the given page.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]

get\_snapshot(_computed\_style\_whitelist_, _include\_event\_listeners\=None_, _include\_paint\_order\=None_, _include\_user\_agent\_shadow\_tree\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#get_snapshot)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.get_snapshot "Link to this definition")

Returns a document snapshot, including the full DOM tree of the root node (including iframes, template contents, and imported documents) in a flattened array, as well as layout and white-listed computed style information for the nodes. Shadow DOM in the returned DOM tree is flattened.

Deprecated since version 1.3.

Parameters:

*   **computed\_style\_whitelist** ([`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – Whitelist of computed styles to return.
    
*   **include\_event\_listeners** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether or not to retrieve details of DOM listeners (default false).
    
*   **include\_paint\_order** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether to determine and include the paint order index of LayoutTreeNodes (default false).
    
*   **include\_user\_agent\_shadow\_tree** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether to include UA shadow tree in the snapshot (default false).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`DOMNode`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.DOMNode "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.DOMNode")\
\], [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`LayoutTreeNode`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeNode "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.LayoutTreeNode")\
\], [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`ComputedStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom_snapshot.html#nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.ComputedStyle "nodriver.cdp.dom_snapshot.ComputedStyle")\
\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **domNodes** - The nodes in the DOM tree. The DOMNode at index 0 corresponds to the root document.
    
1.  **layoutTreeNodes** - The nodes in the layout tree.
    
2.  **computedStyles** - Whitelisted ComputedStyle properties for each node in the layout tree.
    

Deprecated since version 1.3.

_There are no events in this module._

# SystemInfo - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

SystemInfo[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#systeminfo "Link to this heading")

=============================================================================================================================

The SystemInfo domain defines methods and events for querying low-level system information.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#types "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ GPUDevice(_vendor\_id_, _device\_id_, _vendor\_string_, _device\_string_, _driver\_vendor_, _driver\_version_, _sub\_sys\_id\=None_, _revision\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#GPUDevice)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.GPUDevice "Link to this definition")

Describes a single graphics processor (GPU).

vendor\_id_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.GPUDevice.vendor_id "Link to this definition")

PCI ID of the GPU vendor, if available; 0 otherwise.

device\_id_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.GPUDevice.device_id "Link to this definition")

PCI ID of the GPU device, if available; 0 otherwise.

vendor\_string_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.GPUDevice.vendor_string "Link to this definition")

String description of the GPU vendor, if the PCI ID is not available.

device\_string_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.GPUDevice.device_string "Link to this definition")

String description of the GPU device, if the PCI ID is not available.

driver\_vendor_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.GPUDevice.driver_vendor "Link to this definition")

String description of the GPU driver vendor.

driver\_version_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.GPUDevice.driver_version "Link to this definition")

String description of the GPU driver version.

sub\_sys\_id_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.GPUDevice.sub_sys_id "Link to this definition")

Sub sys ID of the GPU, only available on Windows.

revision_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.GPUDevice.revision "Link to this definition")

Revision of the GPU, only available on Windows.

_class_ Size(_width_, _height_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#Size)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.Size "Link to this definition")

Describes the width and height dimensions of an entity.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.Size.width "Link to this definition")

Width in pixels.

height_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.Size.height "Link to this definition")

Height in pixels.

_class_ VideoDecodeAcceleratorCapability(_profile_, _max\_resolution_, _min\_resolution_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#VideoDecodeAcceleratorCapability)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.VideoDecodeAcceleratorCapability "Link to this definition")

Describes a supported video decoding profile with its associated minimum and maximum resolutions.

profile_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.VideoDecodeAcceleratorCapability.profile "Link to this definition")

Video codec profile that is supported, e.g. VP9 Profile 2.

max\_resolution_: [`Size`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.Size "nodriver.cdp.system_info.Size")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.VideoDecodeAcceleratorCapability.max_resolution "Link to this definition")

Maximum video dimensions in pixels supported for this `profile`.

min\_resolution_: [`Size`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.Size "nodriver.cdp.system_info.Size")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.VideoDecodeAcceleratorCapability.min_resolution "Link to this definition")

Minimum video dimensions in pixels supported for this `profile`.

_class_ VideoEncodeAcceleratorCapability(_profile_, _max\_resolution_, _max\_framerate\_numerator_, _max\_framerate\_denominator_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#VideoEncodeAcceleratorCapability)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.VideoEncodeAcceleratorCapability "Link to this definition")

Describes a supported video encoding profile with its associated maximum resolution and maximum framerate.

profile_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.VideoEncodeAcceleratorCapability.profile "Link to this definition")

Video codec profile that is supported, e.g H264 Main.

max\_resolution_: [`Size`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.Size "nodriver.cdp.system_info.Size")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.VideoEncodeAcceleratorCapability.max_resolution "Link to this definition")

Maximum video dimensions in pixels supported for this `profile`.

max\_framerate\_numerator_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.VideoEncodeAcceleratorCapability.max_framerate_numerator "Link to this definition")

Maximum encoding framerate in frames per second supported for this `profile`, as fraction’s numerator and denominator, e.g. 24/1 fps, 24000/1001 fps, etc.

max\_framerate\_denominator_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.VideoEncodeAcceleratorCapability.max_framerate_denominator "Link to this definition")

_class_ SubsamplingFormat(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#SubsamplingFormat)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.SubsamplingFormat "Link to this definition")

YUV subsampling type of the pixels of a given image.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.ProcessInfo.cpu_time "Link to this definition")

Specifies cumulative CPU usage in seconds across all threads of the process since the process start.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
