\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Returns:

get\_info()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#get_info)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.get_info "Link to this definition")

Returns information about the system.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`GPUInfo`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.GPUInfo "nodriver.cdp.system_info.GPUInfo")\
, [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **gpu** - Information about the GPUs on the system.
    
1.  **modelName** - A platform-dependent description of the model of the machine. On Mac OS, this is, for example, ‘MacBookPro’. Will be the empty string if not supported.
    
2.  **modelVersion** - A platform-dependent description of the version of the machine. On Mac OS, this is, for example, ‘10.1’. Will be the empty string if not supported.
    
3.  **commandLine** - The command line string used to launch the browser. Will be the empty string if not supported.

\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`ProcessInfo`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/system_info.html#nodriver.cdp.system_info.ProcessInfo "nodriver.cdp.system_info.ProcessInfo")\
\]\]

Returns:

An array of process info blocks.

ServiceWorker[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/service_worker.html#serviceworker "Link to this heading")

======================================================================================================================================

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/service_worker.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/service_worker.html#commands)

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/service_worker.html#types "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/service_worker.html#commands "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/service_worker.html#events "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

Input[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#module-nodriver.cdp.input_ "Link to this heading")

===================================================================================================================================

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#types "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ TouchPoint(_x_, _y_, _radius\_x\=None_, _radius\_y\=None_, _rotation\_angle\=None_, _force\=None_, _tangential\_pressure\=None_, _tilt\_x\=None_, _tilt\_y\=None_, _twist\=None_, _id\_\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#TouchPoint)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint "Link to this definition")

x_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint.x "Link to this definition")

X coordinate of the event relative to the main frame’s viewport in CSS pixels.

y_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint.y "Link to this definition")

Y coordinate of the event relative to the main frame’s viewport in CSS pixels. 0 refers to the top of the viewport and Y increases as it proceeds towards the bottom of the viewport.

radius\_x_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint.radius_x "Link to this definition")

1.0).

Type:

X radius of the touch area (default

radius\_y_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint.radius_y "Link to this definition")

1.0).

Type:

Y radius of the touch area (default

rotation\_angle_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint.rotation_angle "Link to this definition")

0.0).

Type:

Rotation angle (default

force_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint.force "Link to this definition")

1.0).

Type:

Force (default

tangential\_pressure_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint.tangential_pressure "Link to this definition")

0).

Type:

The normalized tangential pressure, which has a range of \[-1,1\] (default

tilt\_x_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint.tilt_x "Link to this definition")

Type:

The plane angle between the Y-Z plane and the plane containing both the stylus axis and the Y axis, in degrees of the range \[-90,90\], a positive tiltX is to the right (default

tilt\_y_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint.tilt_y "Link to this definition")

0).

Type:

The plane angle between the X-Z plane and the plane containing both the stylus axis and the X axis, in degrees of the range \[-90,90\], a positive tiltY is towards the user (default

twist_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint.twist "Link to this definition")

0).

Type:

The clockwise rotation of a pen stylus around its own major axis, in degrees in the range \[0,359\] (default

id\__: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint.id_ "Link to this definition")

Identifier used to track touch sources between events, must be unique within an event.

_class_ DragDataItem(_mime\_type_, _data_, _title\=None_, _base\_url\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#DragDataItem)

mime\_type_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")

Mime type of the dragged data.

data_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.DragDataItem.data "Link to this definition")

Depending of the value of `mimeType`, it contains the dragged link, text, HTML markup or any other data.

title_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.DragDataItem.title "Link to this definition")

Title associated with a link. Only valid when `mimeType` == “text/uri-list”.

base\_url_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.DragDataItem.base_url "Link to this definition")

Stores the base URL for the contained markup. Only valid when `mimeType` == “text/html”.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.DragData.drag_operations_mask "Link to this definition")

Bit field representing allowed drag operations. Copy = 1, Link = 2, Move = 16

\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.DragData.files "Link to this definition")

List of filenames that should be included when dropping

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#commands "Link to this heading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

dispatch\_drag\_event(_type\__, _x_, _y_, _data_, _modifiers\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#dispatch_drag_event)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.dispatch_drag_event "Link to this definition")

Dispatches a drag event into the page.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **type** – Type of the drag event.
    
*   **x** ([`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – X coordinate of the event relative to the main frame’s viewport in CSS pixels.
    
*   **y** ([`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Y coordinate of the event relative to the main frame’s viewport in CSS pixels. 0 refers to the top of the viewport and Y increases as it proceeds towards the bottom of the viewport.
    
*   **data** ([`DragData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.DragData "nodriver.cdp.input_.DragData")
    ) –
    
*   **modifiers** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Bit field representing pressed modifier keys. Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8 (default: 0).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

dispatch\_key\_event(_type\__, _modifiers\=None_, _timestamp\=None_, _text\=None_, _unmodified\_text\=None_, _key\_identifier\=None_, _code\=None_, _key\=None_, _windows\_virtual\_key\_code\=None_, _native\_virtual\_key\_code\=None_, _auto\_repeat\=None_, _is\_keypad\=None_, _is\_system\_key\=None_, _location\=None_, _commands\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#dispatch_key_event)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.dispatch_key_event "Link to this definition")

Dispatches a key event to the page.

Parameters:

*   **type** – Type of the key event.
    
*   **modifiers** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Bit field representing pressed modifier keys. Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8 (default: 0).
    
*   **timestamp** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`TimeSinceEpoch`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TimeSinceEpoch "nodriver.cdp.input_.TimeSinceEpoch")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Time at which the event occurred.
    
*   **text** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Text as generated by processing a virtual key code with a keyboard layout. Not needed for for ``` `keyUp`` ``` and ` ``rawKeyDown`` ` events (default: “”)
    
*   **unmodified\_text** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Text that would have been generated by the keyboard if no modifiers were pressed (except for shift). Useful for shortcut (accelerator) key handling (default: “”).
    
*   **key\_identifier** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Unique key identifier (e.g., ‘U+0041’) (default: “”).
    
*   **code** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Unique DOM defined string value for each physical key (e.g., ‘KeyA’) (default: “”).
    
*   **key** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Unique DOM defined string value describing the meaning of the key in the context of active modifiers, keyboard layout, etc (e.g., ‘AltGr’) (default: “”).
    
*   **windows\_virtual\_key\_code** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Windows virtual key code (default: 0).
    
*   **native\_virtual\_key\_code** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Native virtual key code (default: 0).
    
*   **auto\_repeat** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether the event was generated from auto repeat (default: false).
    
*   **is\_keypad** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether the event was generated from the keypad (default: false).
    
*   **is\_system\_key** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether the event was a system key event (default: false).
    
*   **location** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether the event was from the left or right side of the keyboard. 1=Left, 2=Right (default: 0).
    
*   **commands** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]\]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Editing commands to send with the key event (e.g., ‘selectAll’) (default: \[\]). These are related to but not equal the command names used in ``` ``document.execCommand` ``` and NSStandardKeyBindingResponding. See [https://source.chromium.org/chromium/chromium/src/+/main:third\_party/blink/renderer/core/editing/commands/editor\_command\_names.h](https://source.chromium.org/chromium/chromium/src/+/main:third_party/blink/renderer/core/editing/commands/editor_command_names.h)
     for valid command names.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

dispatch\_mouse\_event(_type\__, _x_, _y_, _modifiers\=None_, _timestamp\=None_, _button\=None_, _buttons\=None_, _click\_count\=None_, _force\=None_, _tangential\_pressure\=None_, _tilt\_x\=None_, _tilt\_y\=None_, _twist\=None_, _delta\_x\=None_, _delta\_y\=None_, _pointer\_type\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#dispatch_mouse_event)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.dispatch_mouse_event "Link to this definition")

Dispatches a mouse event to the page.

Parameters:

*   **type** – Type of the mouse event.
    
*   **x** ([`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – X coordinate of the event relative to the main frame’s viewport in CSS pixels.
    
*   **y** ([`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Y coordinate of the event relative to the main frame’s viewport in CSS pixels. 0 refers to the top of the viewport and Y increases as it proceeds towards the bottom of the viewport.
    
*   **modifiers** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Bit field representing pressed modifier keys. Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8 (default: 0).
    
*   **timestamp** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`TimeSinceEpoch`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TimeSinceEpoch "nodriver.cdp.input_.TimeSinceEpoch")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Time at which the event occurred.
    
*   **button** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`MouseButton`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.MouseButton "nodriver.cdp.input_.MouseButton")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Mouse button (default: “none”).
    
*   **buttons** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ A number indicating which buttons are pressed on the mouse when a mouse event is triggered. Left=1, Right=2, Middle=4, Back=8, Forward=16, None=0.
    
*   **click\_count** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Number of times the mouse button was clicked (default: 0).
    
*   **force** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ The normalized pressure, which has a range of \[0,1\] (default: 0).
    
*   **tangential\_pressure** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ The normalized tangential pressure, which has a range of \[-1,1\] (default: 0).
    
*   **tilt\_x** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The plane angle between the Y-Z plane and the plane containing both the stylus axis and the Y axis, in degrees of the range \[-90,90\], a positive tiltX is to the right (default: 0).
    
*   **tilt\_y** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The plane angle between the X-Z plane and the plane containing both the stylus axis and the X axis, in degrees of the range \[-90,90\], a positive tiltY is towards the user (default: 0).
    
*   **twist** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ The clockwise rotation of a pen stylus around its own major axis, in degrees in the range \[0,359\] (default: 0).
    
*   **delta\_x** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ X delta in CSS pixels for mouse wheel event (default: 0).
    
*   **delta\_y** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Y delta in CSS pixels for mouse wheel event (default: 0).
    
*   **pointer\_type** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Pointer type (default: “mouse”).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

dispatch\_touch\_event(_type\__, _touch\_points_, _modifiers\=None_, _timestamp\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#dispatch_touch_event)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.dispatch_touch_event "Link to this definition")

Dispatches a touch event to the page.

Parameters:

*   **type** – Type of the touch event. TouchEnd and TouchCancel must not contain any touch points, while TouchStart and TouchMove must contains at least one.
    
*   **touch\_points** ([`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`TouchPoint`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint "nodriver.cdp.input_.TouchPoint")\
    \]) – Active touch points on the touch device. One event per any changed point (compared to previous touch event in a sequence) is generated, emulating pressing/moving/releasing points one by one.
    
*   **modifiers** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Bit field representing pressed modifier keys. Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8 (default: 0).
    
*   **timestamp** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`TimeSinceEpoch`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TimeSinceEpoch "nodriver.cdp.input_.TimeSinceEpoch")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Time at which the event occurred.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

emulate\_touch\_from\_mouse\_event(_type\__, _x_, _y_, _button_, _timestamp\=None_, _delta\_x\=None_, _delta\_y\=None_, _modifiers\=None_, _click\_count\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#emulate_touch_from_mouse_event)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.emulate_touch_from_mouse_event "Link to this definition")

Emulates touch event from the mouse event parameters.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **type** – Type of the mouse event.
    
*   **x** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – X coordinate of the mouse pointer in DIP.
    
*   **y** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Y coordinate of the mouse pointer in DIP.
    
*   **button** ([`MouseButton`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.MouseButton "nodriver.cdp.input_.MouseButton")
    ) – Mouse button. Only “none”, “left”, “right” are supported.
    
*   **timestamp** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`TimeSinceEpoch`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.TimeSinceEpoch "nodriver.cdp.input_.TimeSinceEpoch")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Time at which the event occurred (default: current time).
    
*   **delta\_x** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ X delta in DIP for mouse wheel event (default: 0).
    
*   **delta\_y** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Y delta in DIP for mouse wheel event (default: 0).
    
*   **modifiers** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Bit field representing pressed modifier keys. Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8 (default: 0).
    
*   **click\_count** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Number of times the mouse button was clicked (default: 0).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

ime\_set\_composition(_text_, _selection\_start_, _selection\_end_, _replacement\_start\=None_, _replacement\_end\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#ime_set_composition)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.ime_set_composition "Link to this definition")

This method sets the current candidate text for IME. Use imeCommitComposition to commit the final text. Use imeSetComposition with empty string as text to cancel composition.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **text** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – The text to insert
    
*   **selection\_start** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – selection start
    
*   **selection\_end** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – selection end
    
*   **replacement\_start** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ replacement start
    
*   **replacement\_end** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ replacement end
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.insert_text "Link to this definition")

This method emulates inserting text that doesn’t come from a key press, for example an emoji keyboard or an IME.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

**text** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
) – The text to insert.

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.set_ignore_input_events "Link to this definition")

Ignores input events (useful while auditing page).

Parameters:

**ignore** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
) – Ignores input events processing when set to true.

set\_intercept\_drags(_enabled_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#set_intercept_drags)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.set_intercept_drags "Link to this definition")

Prevents default drag and drop behavior and instead emits `Input.dragIntercepted` events. Drag and drop behavior can be directly controlled via `Input.dispatchDragEvent`.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

**enabled** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
) –

synthesize\_pinch\_gesture(_x_, _y_, _scale\_factor_, _relative\_speed\=None_, _gesture\_source\_type\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#synthesize_pinch_gesture)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.synthesize_pinch_gesture "Link to this definition")

Synthesizes a pinch gesture over a time period by issuing appropriate touch events.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **x** ([`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – X coordinate of the start of the gesture in CSS pixels.
    
*   **y** ([`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Y coordinate of the start of the gesture in CSS pixels.
    
*   **scale\_factor** ([`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Relative scale factor after zooming (>1.0 zooms in, <1.0 zooms out).
    
*   **relative\_speed** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Relative pointer speed in pixels per second (default: 800).
    
*   **gesture\_source\_type** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`GestureSourceType`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.GestureSourceType "nodriver.cdp.input_.GestureSourceType")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Which type of input events to be generated (default: ‘default’, which queries the platform for the preferred input type).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

synthesize\_scroll\_gesture(_x_, _y_, _x\_distance\=None_, _y\_distance\=None_, _x\_overscroll\=None_, _y\_overscroll\=None_, _prevent\_fling\=None_, _speed\=None_, _gesture\_source\_type\=None_, _repeat\_count\=None_, _repeat\_delay\_ms\=None_, _interaction\_marker\_name\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#synthesize_scroll_gesture)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.synthesize_scroll_gesture "Link to this definition")

Synthesizes a scroll gesture over a time period by issuing appropriate touch events.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **x** ([`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – X coordinate of the start of the gesture in CSS pixels.
    
*   **y** ([`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Y coordinate of the start of the gesture in CSS pixels.
    
*   **x\_distance** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The distance to scroll along the X axis (positive to scroll left).
    
*   **y\_distance** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The distance to scroll along the Y axis (positive to scroll up).
    
*   **x\_overscroll** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The number of additional pixels to scroll back along the X axis, in addition to the given distance.
    
*   **y\_overscroll** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The number of additional pixels to scroll back along the Y axis, in addition to the given distance.
    
*   **prevent\_fling** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Prevent fling (default: true).
    
*   **speed** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Swipe speed in pixels per second (default: 800).
    
*   **gesture\_source\_type** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`GestureSourceType`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.GestureSourceType "nodriver.cdp.input_.GestureSourceType")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Which type of input events to be generated (default: ‘default’, which queries the platform for the preferred input type).
    
*   **repeat\_count** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The number of times to repeat the gesture (default: 0).
    
*   **repeat\_delay\_ms** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The number of milliseconds delay between each repeat. (default: 250).
    
*   **interaction\_marker\_name** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The name of the interaction markers to generate, if not empty (default: “”).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

synthesize\_tap\_gesture(_x_, _y_, _duration\=None_, _tap\_count\=None_, _gesture\_source\_type\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#synthesize_tap_gesture)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.synthesize_tap_gesture "Link to this definition")

Synthesizes a tap gesture over a time period by issuing appropriate touch events.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **x** ([`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – X coordinate of the start of the gesture in CSS pixels.
    
*   **y** ([`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Y coordinate of the start of the gesture in CSS pixels.
    
*   **duration** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Duration between touchdown and touchup events in ms (default: 50).
    
*   **tap\_count** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Number of times to perform the tap (e.g. 2 for double tap, default: 1).
    
*   **gesture\_source\_type** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`GestureSourceType`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.GestureSourceType "nodriver.cdp.input_.GestureSourceType")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Which type of input events to be generated (default: ‘default’, which queries the platform for the preferred input type).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#events "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_class_ DragIntercepted(_data_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#DragIntercepted)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.DragIntercepted "Link to this definition")

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Emitted only when `Input.setInterceptDrags` is enabled. Use this data with `Input.dispatchDragEvent` to restore normal drag and drop behavior.

data_: [`DragData`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.DragData "nodriver.cdp.input_.DragData")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/input_.html#nodriver.cdp.input_.DragIntercepted.data "Link to this definition")

Security[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#module-nodriver.cdp.security "Link to this heading")

==========================================================================================================================================

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#commands)

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#types "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ CertificateId[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/security.html#CertificateId)

An internal certificate ID value.

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.MixedContentType "Link to this definition")

A description of mixed content (HTTP resources on HTTPS pages), as defined by [https://www.w3.org/TR/mixed-content/#categories](https://www.w3.org/TR/mixed-content/#categories)

BLOCKABLE _\= 'blockable'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.MixedContentType.BLOCKABLE "Link to this definition")

OPTIONALLY\_BLOCKABLE _\= 'optionally-blockable'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.MixedContentType.OPTIONALLY_BLOCKABLE "Link to this definition")

NONE _\= 'none'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.MixedContentType.NONE "Link to this definition")

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.SecurityState "Link to this definition")

The security level of a page or resource.

NEUTRAL _\= 'neutral'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.SecurityState.NEUTRAL "Link to this definition")

INSECURE _\= 'insecure'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.SecurityState.INSECURE "Link to this definition")

SECURE _\= 'secure'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.SecurityState.SECURE "Link to this definition")

INFO _\= 'info'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.SecurityState.INFO "Link to this definition")

INSECURE\_BROKEN _\= 'insecure-broken'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.SecurityState.INSECURE_BROKEN "Link to this definition")

_class_ CertificateSecurityState(_protocol_, _key\_exchange_, _cipher_, _certificate_, _subject\_name_, _issuer_, _valid\_from_, _valid\_to_, _certificate\_has\_weak\_signature_, _certificate\_has\_sha1\_signature_, _modern\_ssl_, _obsolete\_ssl\_protocol_, _obsolete\_ssl\_key\_exchange_, _obsolete\_ssl\_cipher_, _obsolete\_ssl\_signature_, _key\_exchange\_group\=None_, _mac\=None_, _certificate\_network\_error\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/security.html#CertificateSecurityState)

Name of the issuing CA.

valid\_from_: [`TimeSinceEpoch`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.TimeSinceEpoch "nodriver.cdp.network.TimeSinceEpoch")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateSecurityState.valid_from "Link to this definition")

Certificate valid from date.

valid\_to_: [`TimeSinceEpoch`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.TimeSinceEpoch "nodriver.cdp.network.TimeSinceEpoch")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateSecurityState.valid_to "Link to this definition")

Certificate valid to (expiration) date

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateSecurityState.certificate_has_weak_signature "Link to this definition")

True if the certificate uses a weak signature algorithm.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateSecurityState.certificate_has_sha1_signature "Link to this definition")

True if the certificate has a SHA1 signature in the chain.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateSecurityState.modern_ssl "Link to this definition")

True if modern SSL

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateSecurityState.obsolete_ssl_protocol "Link to this definition")

True if the connection is using an obsolete SSL protocol.

obsolete\_ssl\_key\_exchange_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateSecurityState.obsolete_ssl_key_exchange "Link to this definition")

True if the connection is using an obsolete SSL key exchange.

obsolete\_ssl\_cipher_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateSecurityState.obsolete_ssl_cipher "Link to this definition")

True if the connection is using an obsolete SSL cipher.

obsolete\_ssl\_signature_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateSecurityState.obsolete_ssl_signature "Link to this definition")

True if the connection is using an obsolete SSL signature.

\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateSecurityState.key_exchange_group "Link to this definition")

(EC)DH group used by the connection, if applicable.

mac_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateSecurityState.mac "Link to this definition")

TLS MAC. Note that AEAD ciphers do not have separate MACs.

\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateSecurityState.certificate_network_error "Link to this definition")

The highest priority network error code, if the certificate has an error.

\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.VisibleSecurityState.safety_tip_info "Link to this definition")

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.SecurityStateExplanation.summary "Link to this definition")

\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.SecurityStateExplanation.certificate "Link to this definition")

_class_ InsecureContentStatus(_ran\_mixed\_content_, _displayed\_mixed\_content_, _contained\_mixed\_form_, _ran\_content\_with\_cert\_errors_, _displayed\_content\_with\_cert\_errors_, _ran\_insecure\_content\_style_, _displayed\_insecure\_content\_style_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/security.html#InsecureContentStatus)

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.InsecureContentStatus.ran_insecure_content_style "Link to this definition")

_class_ CertificateErrorAction(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/security.html#CertificateErrorAction)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateErrorAction "Link to this definition")

The action to take when a certificate error occurs. continue will continue processing the request and cancel will cancel the request.

CONTINUE _\= 'continue'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateErrorAction.CONTINUE "Link to this definition")

CANCEL _\= 'cancel'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateErrorAction.CANCEL "Link to this definition")

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#commands "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

handle\_certificate\_error(_event\_id_, _action_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/security.html#handle_certificate_error)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.handle_certificate_error "Link to this definition")

Handles a certificate error that fired a certificateError event.

Deprecated since version 1.3.

Parameters:

*   **event\_id** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – The ID of the event.

    ) – The action to take on the certificate error.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")

set\_override\_certificate\_errors(_override_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/security.html#set_override_certificate_errors)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.set_override_certificate_errors "Link to this definition")

Enable/disable overriding certificate errors. If enabled, all certificate error events need to be handled by the DevTools client and should be answered with `handleCertificateError` commands.

Deprecated since version 1.3.

Parameters:

**override** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
) – If true, certificate errors will be overridden.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#events "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_class_ CertificateError(_event\_id_, _error\_type_, _request\_url_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/security.html#CertificateError)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateError "Link to this definition")

There is a certificate error. If overriding certificate errors is enabled, then it should be handled with the `handleCertificateError` command. Note: this event does not fire if the certificate error has been allowed internally. Only one client per target should override certificate errors at the same time.

Deprecated since version 1.3.

event\_id_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateError.event_id "Link to this definition")

The ID of the event.

error\_type_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateError.error_type "Link to this definition")

The type of the error.

request\_url_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.CertificateError.request_url "Link to this definition")

The url that was requested.

\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/security.html#nodriver.cdp.security.SecurityStateChanged.summary "Link to this definition")

# Tracing - nodriver documentation

Toggle table of contents sidebar

Tracing[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#module-nodriver.cdp.tracing "Link to this heading")

=======================================================================================================================================

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#types "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ MemoryDumpConfig[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#MemoryDumpConfig)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.MemoryDumpConfig "Link to this definition")

Configuration for memory dump. Used only when “memory-infra” category is enabled.

_class_ TraceConfig(_record\_mode\=None_, _trace\_buffer\_size\_in\_kb\=None_, _enable\_sampling\=None_, _enable\_systrace\=None_, _enable\_argument\_filter\=None_, _included\_categories\=None_, _excluded\_categories\=None_, _synthetic\_delays\=None_, _memory\_dump\_config\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#TraceConfig)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TraceConfig "Link to this definition")

record\_mode_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TraceConfig.record_mode "Link to this definition")

Controls how the trace buffer stores data. The default is `recordUntilFull`.

trace\_buffer\_size\_in\_kb_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TraceConfig.trace_buffer_size_in_kb "Link to this definition")

Size of the trace buffer in kilobytes. If not specified or zero is passed, a default value of 200 MB would be used.

enable\_sampling_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TraceConfig.enable_sampling "Link to this definition")

Turns on JavaScript stack sampling.

enable\_systrace_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TraceConfig.enable_systrace "Link to this definition")

Turns on system tracing.

enable\_argument\_filter_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TraceConfig.enable_argument_filter "Link to this definition")

Turns on argument filter.

included\_categories_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TraceConfig.included_categories "Link to this definition")

Included category filters.

excluded\_categories_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TraceConfig.excluded_categories "Link to this definition")

Excluded category filters.

synthetic\_delays_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TraceConfig.synthetic_delays "Link to this definition")

Configuration to synthesize the delays in tracing.

memory\_dump\_config_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`MemoryDumpConfig`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.MemoryDumpConfig "nodriver.cdp.tracing.MemoryDumpConfig")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TraceConfig.memory_dump_config "Link to this definition")

Configuration for memory dump triggers. Used only when “memory-infra” category is enabled.

_class_ StreamFormat(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#StreamFormat)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamFormat "Link to this definition")

Data format of a trace. Can be either the legacy JSON format or the protocol buffer format. Note that the JSON format will be deprecated soon.

JSON _\= 'json'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamFormat.JSON "Link to this definition")

PROTO _\= 'proto'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamFormat.PROTO "Link to this definition")

_class_ StreamCompression(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#StreamCompression)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamCompression "Link to this definition")

Compression type to use for traces returned via streams.

NONE _\= 'none'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamCompression.NONE "Link to this definition")

GZIP _\= 'gzip'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamCompression.GZIP "Link to this definition")

_class_ MemoryDumpLevelOfDetail(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#MemoryDumpLevelOfDetail)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.MemoryDumpLevelOfDetail "Link to this definition")

Details exposed when memory request explicitly declared. Keep consistent with memory\_dump\_request\_args.h and memory\_instrumentation.mojom

BACKGROUND _\= 'background'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.MemoryDumpLevelOfDetail.BACKGROUND "Link to this definition")

LIGHT _\= 'light'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.MemoryDumpLevelOfDetail.LIGHT "Link to this definition")

DETAILED _\= 'detailed'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.MemoryDumpLevelOfDetail.DETAILED "Link to this definition")

_class_ TracingBackend(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#TracingBackend)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TracingBackend "Link to this definition")

Backend type to use for tracing. `chrome` uses the Chrome-integrated tracing service and is supported on all platforms. `system` is only supported on Chrome OS and uses the Perfetto system tracing service. `auto` chooses `system` when the perfettoConfig provided to Tracing.start specifies at least one non-Chrome data source; otherwise uses `chrome`.

AUTO _\= 'auto'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TracingBackend.AUTO "Link to this definition")

CHROME _\= 'chrome'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TracingBackend.CHROME "Link to this definition")

SYSTEM _\= 'system'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TracingBackend.SYSTEM "Link to this definition")

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#commands "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.
