request\_memory\_dump(_deterministic\=None_, _level\_of\_detail\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#request_memory_dump)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.request_memory_dump "Link to this definition")

Request a global memory dump.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **deterministic** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Enables more deterministic results by forcing garbage collection
    
*   **level\_of\_detail** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`MemoryDumpLevelOfDetail`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.MemoryDumpLevelOfDetail "nodriver.cdp.tracing.MemoryDumpLevelOfDetail")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Specifies level of details in memory dump. Defaults to “detailed”.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **dumpGuid** - GUID of the resulting global memory dump.
    
1.  **success** - True iff the global memory dump succeeded.
    

start(_categories\=None_, _options\=None_, _buffer\_usage\_reporting\_interval\=None_, _transfer\_mode\=None_, _stream\_format\=None_, _stream\_compression\=None_, _trace\_config\=None_, _perfetto\_config\=None_, _tracing\_backend\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#start)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.start "Link to this definition")

Start trace events collection.

Parameters:

*   **categories** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(DEPRECATED)** **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Category/tag filter
    
*   **options** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(DEPRECATED)** **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Tracing options
    
*   **buffer\_usage\_reporting\_interval** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ If set, the agent will issue bufferUsage events at this interval, specified in milliseconds
    
*   **transfer\_mode** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether to report trace events as series of dataCollected events or to save trace to a stream (defaults to ``` `ReportEvents`` ```).
    
*   **stream\_format** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`StreamFormat`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamFormat "nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamFormat")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Trace data format to use. This only applies when using ` ``ReturnAsStream`` ` transfer mode (defaults to ` ``json`` `).
    
*   **stream\_compression** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`StreamCompression`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamCompression "nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamCompression")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Compression format to use. This only applies when using ` ``ReturnAsStream`` ` transfer mode (defaults to ` ``none`` `)
    
*   **trace\_config** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`TraceConfig`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TraceConfig "nodriver.cdp.tracing.TraceConfig")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_
    
*   **perfetto\_config** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Base64-encoded serialized perfetto.protos.TraceConfig protobuf message When specified, the parameters ` ``categories`` `, ` ``options`` `, ` ``traceConfig`` ` are ignored. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)
    
*   **tracing\_backend** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`TracingBackend`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TracingBackend "nodriver.cdp.tracing.TracingBackend")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Backend type (defaults to ``` ``auto` ```)
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#events "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_class_ BufferUsage(_percent\_full_, _event\_count_, _value_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#BufferUsage)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.BufferUsage "Link to this definition")

**EXPERIMENTAL**

percent\_full_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.BufferUsage.percent_full "Link to this definition")

A number in range \[0..1\] that indicates the used size of event buffer as a fraction of its total size.

event\_count_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.BufferUsage.event_count "Link to this definition")

An approximate number of events in the trace log.

value_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.BufferUsage.value "Link to this definition")

A number in range \[0..1\] that indicates the used size of event buffer as a fraction of its total size.

_class_ DataCollected(_value_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#DataCollected)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.DataCollected "Link to this definition")

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Contains a bucket of collected trace events. When tracing is stopped collected events will be sent as a sequence of dataCollected events followed by tracingComplete event.

value_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.DataCollected.value "Link to this definition")

_class_ TracingComplete(_data\_loss\_occurred_, _stream_, _trace\_format_, _stream\_compression_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#TracingComplete)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TracingComplete "Link to this definition")

Signals that tracing is stopped and there is no trace buffers pending flush, all data were delivered via dataCollected events.

data\_loss\_occurred_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TracingComplete.data_loss_occurred "Link to this definition")

Indicates whether some trace data is known to have been lost, e.g. because the trace ring buffer wrapped around.

stream_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`StreamHandle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/io.html#nodriver.cdp.io.StreamHandle "nodriver.cdp.io.StreamHandle")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TracingComplete.stream "Link to this definition")

A handle of the stream that holds resulting trace data.

trace\_format_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`StreamFormat`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamFormat "nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamFormat")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TracingComplete.trace_format "Link to this definition")

Trace data format of returned stream.

stream\_compression_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`StreamCompression`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamCompression "nodriver.cdp.tracing.StreamCompression")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/tracing.html#nodriver.cdp.tracing.TracingComplete.stream_compression "Link to this definition")

Compression format of returned stream.

# Debugger - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

Debugger[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#debugger "Link to this heading")

======================================================================================================================

Debugger domain exposes JavaScript debugging capabilities. It allows setting and removing breakpoints, stepping through execution, exploring stack traces, etc.

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#types "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ BreakpointId[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#BreakpointId)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.BreakpointId "Link to this definition")

Breakpoint identifier.

_class_ CallFrameId[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#CallFrameId)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrameId "Link to this definition")

Call frame identifier.

_class_ Location(_script\_id_, _line\_number_, _column\_number\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#Location)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location "Link to this definition")

Location in the source code.

script\_id_: [`ScriptId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location.script_id "Link to this definition")

Script identifier as reported in the `Debugger.scriptParsed`.

line\_number_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location.line_number "Link to this definition")

Line number in the script (0-based).

column\_number_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location.column_number "Link to this definition")

Column number in the script (0-based).

_class_ ScriptPosition(_line\_number_, _column\_number_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#ScriptPosition)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptPosition "Link to this definition")

Location in the source code.

line\_number_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptPosition.line_number "Link to this definition")

column\_number_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptPosition.column_number "Link to this definition")

_class_ LocationRange(_script\_id_, _start_, _end_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#LocationRange)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.LocationRange "Link to this definition")

Location range within one script.

script\_id_: [`ScriptId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.LocationRange.script_id "Link to this definition")

start_: [`ScriptPosition`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptPosition "nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptPosition")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.LocationRange.start "Link to this definition")

end_: [`ScriptPosition`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptPosition "nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptPosition")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.LocationRange.end "Link to this definition")

_class_ CallFrame(_call\_frame\_id_, _function\_name_, _location_, _url_, _scope\_chain_, _this_, _function\_location\=None_, _return\_value\=None_, _can\_be\_restarted\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#CallFrame)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame "Link to this definition")

JavaScript call frame. Array of call frames form the call stack.

call\_frame\_id_: [`CallFrameId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrameId "nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrameId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame.call_frame_id "Link to this definition")

Call frame identifier. This identifier is only valid while the virtual machine is paused.

function\_name_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame.function_name "Link to this definition")

Name of the JavaScript function called on this call frame.

location_: [`Location`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location "nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame.location "Link to this definition")

Location in the source code.

url_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame.url "Link to this definition")

JavaScript script name or url. Deprecated in favor of using the `location.scriptId` to resolve the URL via a previously sent `Debugger.scriptParsed` event.

scope\_chain_: [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Scope`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Scope "nodriver.cdp.debugger.Scope")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame.scope_chain "Link to this definition")

Scope chain for this call frame.

this_: [`RemoteObject`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame.this "Link to this definition")

`this` object for this call frame.

function\_location_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Location`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location "nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame.function_location "Link to this definition")

Location in the source code.

return\_value_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RemoteObject`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame.return_value "Link to this definition")

The value being returned, if the function is at return point.

can\_be\_restarted_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame.can_be_restarted "Link to this definition")

Valid only while the VM is paused and indicates whether this frame can be restarted or not. Note that a `true` value here does not guarantee that Debugger#restartFrame with this CallFrameId will be successful, but it is very likely.

_class_ Scope(_type\__, _object\__, _name\=None_, _start\_location\=None_, _end\_location\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#Scope)

end\_location_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#commands "Link to this heading")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

continue\_to\_location(_location_, _target\_call\_frames\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#continue_to_location)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.continue_to_location "Link to this definition")

Continues execution until specific location is reached.

Parameters:

*   **location** ([`Location`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location "nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location")
    ) – Location to continue to.
    
*   **target\_call\_frames** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

disassemble\_wasm\_module(_script\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#disassemble_wasm_module)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.disassemble_wasm_module "Link to this definition")

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

**script\_id** ([`ScriptId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId")
) – Id of the script to disassemble

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`WasmDisassemblyChunk`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.WasmDisassemblyChunk "nodriver.cdp.debugger.WasmDisassemblyChunk")\
\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **streamId** - _(Optional)_ For large modules, return a stream from which additional chunks of disassembly can be read successively.
    
1.  **totalNumberOfLines** - The total number of lines in the disassembly text.
    
2.  **functionBodyOffsets** - The offsets of all function bodies, in the format \[start1, end1, start2, end2, …\] where all ends are exclusive.
    
3.  **chunk** - The first chunk of disassembly.
    

enable(_max\_scripts\_cache\_size\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#enable)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.enable "Link to this definition")

Enables debugger for the given page. Clients should not assume that the debugging has been enabled until the result for this command is received.

Parameters:

**max\_scripts\_cache\_size** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ The maximum size in bytes of collected scripts (not referenced by other heap objects) the debugger can hold. Puts no limit if parameter is omitted.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`UniqueDebuggerId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.UniqueDebuggerId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.UniqueDebuggerId")\
\]

Returns:

Unique identifier of the debugger.

evaluate\_on\_call\_frame(_call\_frame\_id_, _expression_, _object\_group\=None_, _include\_command\_line\_api\=None_, _silent\=None_, _return\_by\_value\=None_, _generate\_preview\=None_, _throw\_on\_side\_effect\=None_, _timeout\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#evaluate_on_call_frame)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.evaluate_on_call_frame "Link to this definition")

Evaluates expression on a given call frame.

Parameters:

*   **call\_frame\_id** ([`CallFrameId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrameId "nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrameId")
    ) – Call frame identifier to evaluate on.
    
*   **expression** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Expression to evaluate.
    
*   **object\_group** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ String object group name to put result into (allows rapid releasing resulting object handles using ``` `releaseObjectGroup`` ```).
    
*   **include\_command\_line\_api** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Specifies whether command line API should be available to the evaluated expression, defaults to false.
    
*   **silent** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ In silent mode exceptions thrown during evaluation are not reported and do not pause execution. Overrides ``` ``setPauseOnException` ``` state.
    
*   **return\_by\_value** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether the result is expected to be a JSON object that should be sent by value.
    
*   **generate\_preview** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Whether preview should be generated for the result.
    
*   **throw\_on\_side\_effect** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether to throw an exception if side effect cannot be ruled out during evaluation.
    
*   **timeout** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`TimeDelta`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.TimeDelta "nodriver.cdp.runtime.TimeDelta")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Terminate execution after timing out (number of milliseconds).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`RemoteObject`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject")\
, [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **result** - Object wrapper for the evaluation result.
    
1.  **exceptionDetails** - _(Optional)_ Exception details.
    

get\_possible\_breakpoints(_start_, _end\=None_, _restrict\_to\_function\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#get_possible_breakpoints)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.get_possible_breakpoints "Link to this definition")

Returns possible locations for breakpoint. scriptId in start and end range locations should be the same.

Parameters:

*   **start** ([`Location`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location "nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location")
    ) – Start of range to search possible breakpoint locations in.
    
*   **end** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`Location`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location "nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ End of range to search possible breakpoint locations in (excluding). When not specified, end of scripts is used as end of range.
    
*   **restrict\_to\_function** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Only consider locations which are in the same (non-nested) function as start.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`BreakLocation`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.BreakLocation "nodriver.cdp.debugger.BreakLocation")\
\]\]

Returns:

List of the possible breakpoint locations.

next\_wasm\_disassembly\_chunk(_stream\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#next_wasm_disassembly_chunk)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.next_wasm_disassembly_chunk "Link to this definition")

Disassemble the next chunk of lines for the module corresponding to the stream. If disassembly is complete, this API will invalidate the streamId and return an empty chunk. Any subsequent calls for the now invalid stream will return errors.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

**stream\_id** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
) –

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`WasmDisassemblyChunk`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.WasmDisassemblyChunk "nodriver.cdp.debugger.WasmDisassemblyChunk")\
\]

Returns:

The next chunk of disassembly.

restart\_frame(_call\_frame\_id_, _mode\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#restart_frame)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.restart_frame "Link to this definition")

Restarts particular call frame from the beginning. The old, deprecated behavior of `restartFrame` is to stay paused and allow further CDP commands after a restart was scheduled. This can cause problems with restarting, so we now continue execution immediatly after it has been scheduled until we reach the beginning of the restarted frame.

To stay back-wards compatible, `restartFrame` now expects a `mode` parameter to be present. If the `mode` parameter is missing, `restartFrame` errors out.

The various return values are deprecated and `callFrames` is always empty. Use the call frames from the `Debugger#paused` events instead, that fires once V8 pauses at the beginning of the restarted function.

Parameters:

*   **call\_frame\_id** ([`CallFrameId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrameId "nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrameId")
    ) – Call frame identifier to evaluate on.
    
*   **mode** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ The ``` `mode`` ``` parameter must be present and set to ‘StepInto’, otherwise ``` ``restartFrame` ``` will error out.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`CallFrame`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame "nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame")\
\], [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`StackTrace`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTrace "nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTrace")\
\], [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`StackTraceId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTraceId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTraceId")\
\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **callFrames** - New stack trace.
    
1.  **asyncStackTrace** - _(Optional)_ Async stack trace, if any.
    
2.  **asyncStackTraceId** - _(Optional)_ Async stack trace, if any.
    

resume(_terminate\_on\_resume\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#resume)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.resume "Link to this definition")

Resumes JavaScript execution.

Parameters:

**terminate\_on\_resume** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]) – _(Optional)_ Set to true to terminate execution upon resuming execution. In contrast to Runtime.terminateExecution, this will allows to execute further JavaScript (i.e. via evaluation) until execution of the paused code is actually resumed, at which point termination is triggered. If execution is currently not paused, this parameter has no effect.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

    ) – Id of the script to search in.

    \]) – _(Optional)_ If true, search is case sensitive.

    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If true, treats string parameter as regex.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\

\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

set\_blackbox\_execution\_contexts(_unique\_ids_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#set_blackbox_execution_contexts)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.set_blackbox_execution_contexts "Link to this definition")

Replace previous blackbox execution contexts with passed ones. Forces backend to skip stepping/pausing in scripts in these execution contexts. VM will try to leave blackboxed script by performing ‘step in’ several times, finally resorting to ‘step out’ if unsuccessful.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

**unique\_ids** ([`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]) – Array of execution context unique ids for the debugger to ignore.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

set\_blackbox\_patterns(_patterns_, _skip\_anonymous\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#set_blackbox_patterns)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.set_blackbox_patterns "Link to this definition")

Replace previous blackbox patterns with passed ones. Forces backend to skip stepping/pausing in scripts with url matching one of the patterns. VM will try to leave blackboxed script by performing ‘step in’ several times, finally resorting to ‘step out’ if unsuccessful.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **patterns** ([`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – Array of regexps that will be used to check script url for blackbox state.
    
*   **skip\_anonymous** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If true, also ignore scripts with no source url.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

set\_blackboxed\_ranges(_script\_id_, _positions_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#set_blackboxed_ranges)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.set_blackboxed_ranges "Link to this definition")

Makes backend skip steps in the script in blackboxed ranges. VM will try leave blacklisted scripts by performing ‘step in’ several times, finally resorting to ‘step out’ if unsuccessful. Positions array contains positions where blackbox state is changed. First interval isn’t blackboxed. Array should be sorted.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **script\_id** ([`ScriptId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId")
    ) – Id of the script.
    
*   **positions** ([`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`ScriptPosition`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptPosition "nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptPosition")\
    \]) –
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

set\_breakpoint(_location_, _condition\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#set_breakpoint)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.set_breakpoint "Link to this definition")

Sets JavaScript breakpoint at a given location.

Parameters:

*   **location** ([`Location`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location "nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location")
    ) – Location to set breakpoint in.
    
*   **condition** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Expression to use as a breakpoint condition. When specified, debugger will only stop on the breakpoint if this expression evaluates to true.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`BreakpointId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.BreakpointId "nodriver.cdp.debugger.BreakpointId")\
, [`Location`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location "nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location")\
\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **breakpointId** - Id of the created breakpoint for further reference.
    
1.  **actualLocation** - Location this breakpoint resolved into.
    

set\_breakpoint\_by\_url(_line\_number_, _url\=None_, _url\_regex\=None_, _script\_hash\=None_, _column\_number\=None_, _condition\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#set_breakpoint_by_url)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.set_breakpoint_by_url "Link to this definition")

Sets JavaScript breakpoint at given location specified either by URL or URL regex. Once this command is issued, all existing parsed scripts will have breakpoints resolved and returned in `locations` property. Further matching script parsing will result in subsequent `breakpointResolved` events issued. This logical breakpoint will survive page reloads.

Parameters:

*   **line\_number** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Line number to set breakpoint at.
    
*   **url** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ URL of the resources to set breakpoint on.
    
*   **url\_regex** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Regex pattern for the URLs of the resources to set breakpoints on. Either ``` `url`` ``` or ``` ``urlRegex` ``` must be specified.
    
*   **script\_hash** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Script hash of the resources to set breakpoint on.
    
*   **column\_number** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Offset in the line to set breakpoint at.
    
*   **condition** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Expression to use as a breakpoint condition. When specified, debugger will only stop on the breakpoint if this expression evaluates to true.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`BreakpointId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.BreakpointId "nodriver.cdp.debugger.BreakpointId")\
, [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`Location`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location "nodriver.cdp.debugger.Location")\
\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **breakpointId** - Id of the created breakpoint for further reference.
    
1.  **locations** - List of the locations this breakpoint resolved into upon addition.
    

set\_breakpoint\_on\_function\_call(_object\_id_, _condition\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#set_breakpoint_on_function_call)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.set_breakpoint_on_function_call "Link to this definition")

Sets JavaScript breakpoint before each call to the given function. If another function was created from the same source as a given one, calling it will also trigger the breakpoint.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **object\_id** ([`RemoteObjectId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId")
    ) – Function object id.
    
*   **condition** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Expression to use as a breakpoint condition. When specified, debugger will stop on the breakpoint if this expression evaluates to true.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`BreakpointId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.BreakpointId "nodriver.cdp.debugger.BreakpointId")\
\]

Returns:

Id of the created breakpoint for further reference.

set\_pause\_on\_exceptions(_state_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#set_pause_on_exceptions)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.set_pause_on_exceptions "Link to this definition")

Defines pause on exceptions state. Can be set to stop on all exceptions, uncaught exceptions, or caught exceptions, no exceptions. Initial pause on exceptions state is `none`.

\]

set\_script\_source(_script\_id_, _script\_source_, _dry\_run\=None_, _allow\_top\_frame\_editing\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#set_script_source)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.set_script_source "Link to this definition")

Edits JavaScript source live.

In general, functions that are currently on the stack can not be edited with a single exception: If the edited function is the top-most stack frame and that is the only activation of that function on the stack. In this case the live edit will be successful and a `Debugger.restartFrame` for the top-most function is automatically triggered.

Parameters:

*   **script\_id** ([`ScriptId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId")
    ) – Id of the script to edit.
    
*   **script\_source** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – New content of the script.
    
*   **dry\_run** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If true the change will not actually be applied. Dry run may be used to get result description without actually modifying the code.
    
*   **allow\_top\_frame\_editing** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ If true, then ``` `scriptSource`` ``` is allowed to change the function on top of the stack as long as the top-most stack frame is the only activation of that function.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`CallFrame`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame "nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrame")\
\]\], [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`StackTrace`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTrace "nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTrace")\
\], [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`StackTraceId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTraceId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTraceId")\
\], [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`ExceptionDetails`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails")\
\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **callFrames** - _(Optional)_ New stack trace in case editing has happened while VM was stopped.
    
1.  **stackChanged** - _(Optional)_ Whether current call stack was modified after applying the changes.
    
2.  **asyncStackTrace** - _(Optional)_ Async stack trace, if any.
    
3.  **asyncStackTraceId** - _(Optional)_ Async stack trace, if any.
    
4.  **status** - Whether the operation was successful or not. Only \`\` Ok\`\` denotes a successful live edit while the other enum variants denote why the live edit failed.
    
5.  **exceptionDetails** - _(Optional)_ Exception details if any. Only present when \`\` status\`\` is \`\` CompileError\`.
    

set\_skip\_all\_pauses(_skip_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#set_skip_all_pauses)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.set_skip_all_pauses "Link to this definition")

Makes page not interrupt on any pauses (breakpoint, exception, dom exception etc).

**skip** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
) – New value for skip pauses state.

\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\

set\_variable\_value(_scope\_number_, _variable\_name_, _new\_value_, _call\_frame\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#set_variable_value)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.set_variable_value "Link to this definition")

Changes value of variable in a callframe. Object-based scopes are not supported and must be mutated manually.

Parameters:

*   **scope\_number** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – 0-based number of scope as was listed in scope chain. Only ‘local’, ‘closure’ and ‘catch’ scope types are allowed. Other scopes could be manipulated manually.
    
*   **variable\_name** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Variable name.
    
*   **new\_value** ([`CallArgument`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.CallArgument "nodriver.cdp.runtime.CallArgument")
    ) – New variable value.
    
*   **call\_frame\_id** ([`CallFrameId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrameId "nodriver.cdp.debugger.CallFrameId")
    ) – Id of callframe that holds variable.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#events "Link to this heading")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_class_ BreakpointResolved(_breakpoint\_id_, _location_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#BreakpointResolved)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.BreakpointResolved "Link to this definition")

Fired when breakpoint is resolved to an actual script and location. Deprecated in favor of `resolvedBreakpoints` in the `scriptParsed` event.

Deprecated since version 1.3.

Breakpoint unique identifier.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.BreakpointResolved.location "Link to this definition")

Actual breakpoint location.

For Wasm modules, the content of the `build_id` custom section. For JavaScript the `debugId` magic comment.

\[[`dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

Type:

boolean, [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.13)")

\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptFailedToParse.is_module "Link to this definition")

\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptFailedToParse.length "Link to this definition")

stack\_trace_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`StackTrace`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTrace "nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTrace")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptFailedToParse.stack_trace "Link to this definition")

\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptFailedToParse.code_offset "Link to this definition")

\[[`ScriptLanguage`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptLanguage "nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptLanguage")\

_class_ ScriptParsed(_script\_id_, _url_, _start\_line_, _start\_column_, _end\_line_, _end\_column_, _execution\_context\_id_, _hash\__, _build\_id_, _execution\_context\_aux\_data_, _is\_live\_edit_, _source\_map\_url_, _has\_source\_url_, _is\_module_, _length_, _stack\_trace_, _code\_offset_, _script\_language_, _debug\_symbols_, _embedder\_name_, _resolved\_breakpoints_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#ScriptParsed)

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.script_id "Link to this definition")

Identifier of the script parsed.

url_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.url "Link to this definition")

URL or name of the script parsed (if any).

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.start_line "Link to this definition")

Line offset of the script within the resource with given URL (for script tags).

start\_column_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.start_column "Link to this definition")

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.end_line "Link to this definition")

Last line of the script.

end\_column_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.end_column "Link to this definition")

Length of the last line of the script.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.execution_context_id "Link to this definition")

Specifies script creation context.

hash\__: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.hash_ "Link to this definition")

Content hash of the script, SHA-256.

build\_id_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.build_id "Link to this definition")

For Wasm modules, the content of the `build_id` custom section. For JavaScript the `debugId` magic comment.

\[[`dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.execution_context_aux_data "Link to this definition")

‘default’\`\`’isolated’\`\`’worker’, frameId: string}

Type:

Embedder-specific auxiliary data likely matching {isDefault

Type:

boolean, [type](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#type "(in Python v3.13)")

is\_live\_edit_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.is_live_edit "Link to this definition")

True, if this script is generated as a result of the live edit operation.

\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.source_map_url "Link to this definition")

URL of source map associated with script (if any).

\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.has_source_url "Link to this definition")

True, if this script has sourceURL.

is\_module_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.is_module "Link to this definition")

True, if this script is ES6 module.

length_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.length "Link to this definition")

This script length.

stack\_trace_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`StackTrace`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTrace "nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTrace")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.stack_trace "Link to this definition")

JavaScript top stack frame of where the script parsed event was triggered if available.

code\_offset_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.code_offset "Link to this definition")

If the scriptLanguage is WebAssembly, the code section offset in the module.

script\_language_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`ScriptLanguage`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptLanguage "nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptLanguage")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.script_language "Link to this definition")

The language of the script.

debug\_symbols_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`DebugSymbols`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.DebugSymbols "nodriver.cdp.debugger.DebugSymbols")\
\]\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.debug_symbols "Link to this definition")

If the scriptLanguage is WebAssembly, the source of debug symbols for the module.

embedder\_name_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.embedder_name "Link to this definition")

The name the embedder supplied for this script.

resolved\_breakpoints_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`ResolvedBreakpoint`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ResolvedBreakpoint "nodriver.cdp.debugger.ResolvedBreakpoint")\
\]\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/debugger.html#nodriver.cdp.debugger.ScriptParsed.resolved_breakpoints "Link to this definition")

The list of set breakpoints in this script if calls to `setBreakpointByUrl` matches this script’s URL or hash. Clients that use this list can ignore the `breakpointResolved` event. They are equivalent.

# WebAudio - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

WebAudio[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#webaudio "Link to this heading")

=======================================================================================================================

This domain allows inspection of Web Audio API. [https://webaudio.github.io/web-audio-api/](https://webaudio.github.io/web-audio-api/)

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#types "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ GraphObjectId[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#GraphObjectId)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#nodriver.cdp.web_audio.GraphObjectId "Link to this definition")

An unique ID for a graph object (AudioContext, AudioNode, AudioParam) in Web Audio API

_class_ ContextRealtimeData(_current\_time_, _render\_capacity_, _callback\_interval\_mean_, _callback\_interval\_variance_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#ContextRealtimeData)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#nodriver.cdp.web_audio.ContextRealtimeData "Link to this definition")

Fields in AudioContext that change in real-time.

current\_time_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#nodriver.cdp.web_audio.ContextRealtimeData.current_time "Link to this definition")

The current context time in second in BaseAudioContext.

render\_capacity_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#nodriver.cdp.web_audio.ContextRealtimeData.render_capacity "Link to this definition")

The time spent on rendering graph divided by render quantum duration, and multiplied by 100. 100 means the audio renderer reached the full capacity and glitch may occur.

callback\_interval\_mean_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#nodriver.cdp.web_audio.ContextRealtimeData.callback_interval_mean "Link to this definition")

A running mean of callback interval.

callback\_interval\_variance_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#nodriver.cdp.web_audio.ContextRealtimeData.callback_interval_variance "Link to this definition")

A running variance of callback interval.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/web_audio.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.
