_class_ ScreenInfo(_left_, _top_, _width_, _height_, _avail\_left_, _avail\_top_, _avail\_width_, _avail\_height_, _device\_pixel\_ratio_, _orientation_, _color\_depth_, _is\_extended_, _is\_internal_, _is\_primary_, _label_, _id\__)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#ScreenInfo)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo "Link to this definition")

Screen information similar to the one returned by window.getScreenDetails() method, see [https://w3c.github.io/window-management/#screendetailed](https://w3c.github.io/window-management/#screendetailed)
.

left_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.left "Link to this definition")

Offset of the left edge of the screen.

top_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.top "Link to this definition")

Offset of the top edge of the screen.

width_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.width "Link to this definition")

Width of the screen.

height_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.height "Link to this definition")

Height of the screen.

avail\_left_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.avail_left "Link to this definition")

Offset of the left edge of the available screen area.

avail\_top_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.avail_top "Link to this definition")

Offset of the top edge of the available screen area.

avail\_width_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.avail_width "Link to this definition")

Width of the available screen area.

avail\_height_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.avail_height "Link to this definition")

Height of the available screen area.

device\_pixel\_ratio_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.device_pixel_ratio "Link to this definition")

Specifies the screen’s device pixel ratio.

orientation_: [`ScreenOrientation`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenOrientation "nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenOrientation")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.orientation "Link to this definition")

Specifies the screen’s orientation.

color\_depth_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.color_depth "Link to this definition")

Specifies the screen’s color depth in bits.

is\_extended_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.is_extended "Link to this definition")

Indicates whether the device has multiple screens.

is\_internal_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.is_internal "Link to this definition")

Indicates whether the screen is internal to the device or external, attached to the device.

is\_primary_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.is_primary "Link to this definition")

Indicates whether the screen is set as the the operating system primary screen.

label_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.label "Link to this definition")

Specifies the descriptive label for the screen.

id\__: [`ScreenId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenId "nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo.id_ "Link to this definition")

Specifies the unique identifier of the screen.

_class_ DisabledImageType(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#DisabledImageType)

Enum of image types that can be disabled.

WEBP _\= 'webp'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.DisabledImageType.WEBP "Link to this definition")

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

add\_screen(_left_, _top_, _width_, _height_, _work\_area\_insets\=None_, _device\_pixel\_ratio\=None_, _rotation\=None_, _color\_depth\=None_, _label\=None_, _is\_internal\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#add_screen)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.add_screen "Link to this definition")

Add a new screen to the device. Only supported in headless mode.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **left** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Offset of the left edge of the screen in pixels.
    
*   **top** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Offset of the top edge of the screen in pixels.
    
*   **width** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – The width of the screen in pixels.
    
*   **height** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – The height of the screen in pixels.
    
*   **work\_area\_insets** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`WorkAreaInsets`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.WorkAreaInsets "nodriver.cdp.emulation.WorkAreaInsets")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Specifies the screen’s work area. Default is entire screen.
    
*   **device\_pixel\_ratio** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Specifies the screen’s device pixel ratio. Default is 1.
    
*   **rotation** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Specifies the screen’s rotation angle. Available values are 0, 90, 180 and 270. Default is 0.
    
*   **color\_depth** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Specifies the screen’s color depth in bits. Default is 24.
    
*   **label** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Specifies the descriptive label for the screen. Default is none.
    
*   **is\_internal** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Indicates whether the screen is internal to the device or external, attached to the device. Default is false.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`ScreenInfo`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo "nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenInfo")\
\]

clear\_device\_posture\_override()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#clear_device_posture_override)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.clear_device_posture_override "Link to this definition")

Clears a device posture override set with either setDeviceMetricsOverride() or setDevicePostureOverride() and starts using posture information from the platform again. Does nothing if no override is set.

remove\_screen(_screen\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#remove_screen)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.remove_screen "Link to this definition")

Remove screen from the device. Only supported in headless mode.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

set\_device\_metrics\_override(_width_, _height_, _device\_scale\_factor_, _mobile_, _scale\=None_, _screen\_width\=None_, _screen\_height\=None_, _position\_x\=None_, _position\_y\=None_, _dont\_set\_visible\_size\=None_, _screen\_orientation\=None_, _viewport\=None_, _display\_feature\=None_, _device\_posture\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#set_device_metrics_override)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.set_device_metrics_override "Link to this definition")

Overrides the values of device screen dimensions (window.screen.width, window.screen.height, window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight, and “device-width”/”device-height”-related CSS media query results).

Parameters:

*   **width** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Overriding width value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000). 0 disables the override.
    
*   **height** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Overriding height value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000). 0 disables the override.
    
*   **device\_scale\_factor** ([`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Overriding device scale factor value. 0 disables the override.
    
*   **mobile** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Whether to emulate mobile device. This includes viewport meta tag, overlay scrollbars, text autosizing and more.
    
*   **scale** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Scale to apply to resulting view image.
    
*   **screen\_width** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Overriding screen width value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).
    
*   **screen\_height** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Overriding screen height value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).
    
*   **position\_x** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Overriding view X position on screen in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).
    
*   **position\_y** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Overriding view Y position on screen in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).
    
*   **dont\_set\_visible\_size** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Do not set visible view size, rely upon explicit setVisibleSize call.
    
*   **screen\_orientation** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`ScreenOrientation`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenOrientation "nodriver.cdp.emulation.ScreenOrientation")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Screen orientation override.
    
*   **viewport** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`Viewport`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/page.html#nodriver.cdp.page.Viewport "nodriver.cdp.page.Viewport")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ If set, the visible area of the page will be overridden to this viewport. This viewport change is not observed by the page, e.g. viewport-relative elements do not change positions.
    
*   **display\_feature** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`DisplayFeature`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.DisplayFeature "nodriver.cdp.emulation.DisplayFeature")\
    \]) – **(DEPRECATED)** **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ If set, the display feature of a multi-segment screen. If not set, multi-segment support is turned-off. Deprecated, use Emulation.setDisplayFeaturesOverride.
    
*   **device\_posture** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`DevicePosture`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.DevicePosture "nodriver.cdp.emulation.DevicePosture")\
    \]) – **(DEPRECATED)** **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ If set, the posture of a foldable device. If not set the posture is set to continuous. Deprecated, use Emulation.setDevicePostureOverride.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Overrides the values for env(safe-area-inset-_) and env(safe-area-max-inset-_). Unset values will cause the respective variables to be undefined, even if previously overridden.

set\_sensor\_override\_enabled(_enabled_, _type\__, _metadata\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#set_sensor_override_enabled)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.set_sensor_override_enabled "Link to this definition")

Overrides a platform sensor of a given type. If `enabled` is true, calls to Sensor.start() will use a virtual sensor as backend rather than fetching data from a real hardware sensor. Otherwise, existing virtual sensor-backend Sensor objects will fire an error event and new calls to Sensor.start() will attempt to use a real sensor instead.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **enabled** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **type** –
    
*   **metadata** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`SensorMetadata`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.SensorMetadata "nodriver.cdp.emulation.SensorMetadata")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.set_small_viewport_height_difference_override "Link to this definition")

Allows overriding the difference between the small and large viewport sizes, which determine the value of the `svh` and `lvh` unit, respectively. Only supported for top-level frames.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

**difference** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
) – This will cause an element of size 100svh to be `` `difference` `` pixels smaller than an element of size 100lvh.

set\_user\_agent\_override(_user\_agent_, _accept\_language\=None_, _platform\=None_, _user\_agent\_metadata\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#set_user_agent_override)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.set_user_agent_override "Link to this definition")

Allows overriding user agent with the given string. `userAgentMetadata` must be set for Client Hint headers to be sent.

Parameters:

    ) – User agent to use.

    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Browser language to emulate.
    
*   **platform** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The platform navigator.platform should return.
    
*   **user\_agent\_metadata** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`UserAgentMetadata`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.UserAgentMetadata "nodriver.cdp.emulation.UserAgentMetadata")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ To be sent in Sec-CH-UA-\* headers and returned in navigator.userAgentData
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")

set\_virtual\_time\_policy(_policy_, _budget\=None_, _max\_virtual\_time\_task\_starvation\_count\=None_, _initial\_virtual\_time\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#set_virtual_time_policy)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.set_virtual_time_policy "Link to this definition")

Turns on virtual time for all frames (replacing real-time with a synthetic time source) and sets the current virtual time policy. Note this supersedes any previous time budget.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **policy** ([`VirtualTimePolicy`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.VirtualTimePolicy "nodriver.cdp.emulation.VirtualTimePolicy")
    ) –
    
*   **budget** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If set, after this many virtual milliseconds have elapsed virtual time will be paused and a virtualTimeBudgetExpired event is sent.
    
*   **max\_virtual\_time\_task\_starvation\_count** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If set this specifies the maximum number of tasks that can be run before virtual is forced forwards to prevent deadlock.
    
*   **initial\_virtual\_time** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`TimeSinceEpoch`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/network.html#nodriver.cdp.network.TimeSinceEpoch "nodriver.cdp.network.TimeSinceEpoch")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If set, base::Time::Now will be overridden to initially return this value.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Returns:

Absolute timestamp at which virtual time was first enabled (up time in milliseconds).

set\_visible\_size(_width_, _height_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#set_visible_size)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.set_visible_size "Link to this definition")

Resizes the frame/viewport of the page. Note that this does not affect the frame’s container (e.g. browser window). Can be used to produce screenshots of the specified size. Not supported on Android.

Deprecated since version 1.3.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

*   **width** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Frame width (DIP).
    
*   **height** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Frame height (DIP).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Deprecated since version 1.3.

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#events "Link to this heading")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_class_ VirtualTimeBudgetExpired[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#VirtualTimeBudgetExpired)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/emulation.html#nodriver.cdp.emulation.VirtualTimeBudgetExpired "Link to this definition")

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Notification sent after the virtual time budget for the current VirtualTimePolicy has run out.

# Overlay - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

Overlay[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#overlay "Link to this heading")

===================================================================================================================

This domain provides various functionality related to drawing atop the inspected page.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#types "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ SourceOrderConfig(_parent\_outline\_color_, _child\_outline\_color_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#SourceOrderConfig)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.SourceOrderConfig "Link to this definition")

Configuration data for drawing the source order of an elements children.

parent\_outline\_color_: [`RGBA`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.RGBA "nodriver.cdp.dom.RGBA")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.SourceOrderConfig.parent_outline_color "Link to this definition")

the color to outline the given element in.

child\_outline\_color_: [`RGBA`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.RGBA "nodriver.cdp.dom.RGBA")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.SourceOrderConfig.child_outline_color "Link to this definition")

the color to outline the child elements in.

_class_ GridHighlightConfig(_show\_grid\_extension\_lines\=None_, _show\_positive\_line\_numbers\=None_, _show\_negative\_line\_numbers\=None_, _show\_area\_names\=None_, _show\_line\_names\=None_, _show\_track\_sizes\=None_, _grid\_border\_color\=None_, _cell\_border\_color\=None_, _row\_line\_color\=None_, _column\_line\_color\=None_, _grid\_border\_dash\=None_, _cell\_border\_dash\=None_, _row\_line\_dash\=None_, _column\_line\_dash\=None_, _row\_gap\_color\=None_, _row\_hatch\_color\=None_, _column\_gap\_color\=None_, _column\_hatch\_color\=None_, _area\_border\_color\=None_, _grid\_background\_color\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#GridHighlightConfig)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.GridHighlightConfig "Link to this definition")

Configuration data for the highlighting of Grid elements.

show\_grid\_extension\_lines_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.GridHighlightConfig.show_grid_extension_lines "Link to this definition")

false).

Type:

Whether the extension lines from grid cells to the rulers should be shown (default

_class_ FlexContainerHighlightConfig(_container\_border\=None_, _line\_separator\=None_, _item\_separator\=None_, _main\_distributed\_space\=None_, _cross\_distributed\_space\=None_, _row\_gap\_space\=None_, _column\_gap\_space\=None_, _cross\_alignment\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#FlexContainerHighlightConfig)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.FlexContainerHighlightConfig "Link to this definition")

Configuration data for the highlighting of Flex container elements.

container\_border_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`LineStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.LineStyle "nodriver.cdp.overlay.LineStyle")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.FlexContainerHighlightConfig.container_border "Link to this definition")

The style of the container border

line\_separator_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`LineStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.LineStyle "nodriver.cdp.overlay.LineStyle")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.FlexContainerHighlightConfig.line_separator "Link to this definition")

The style of the separator between lines

item\_separator_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`LineStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.LineStyle "nodriver.cdp.overlay.LineStyle")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.FlexContainerHighlightConfig.item_separator "Link to this definition")

The style of the separator between items

main\_distributed\_space_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`BoxStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.BoxStyle "nodriver.cdp.overlay.BoxStyle")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.FlexContainerHighlightConfig.main_distributed_space "Link to this definition")

Style of content-distribution space on the main axis (justify-content).

cross\_distributed\_space_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`BoxStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.BoxStyle "nodriver.cdp.overlay.BoxStyle")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.FlexContainerHighlightConfig.cross_distributed_space "Link to this definition")

Style of content-distribution space on the cross axis (align-content).

\[[`BoxStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.BoxStyle "nodriver.cdp.overlay.BoxStyle")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.FlexContainerHighlightConfig.row_gap_space "Link to this definition")

Style of empty space caused by row gaps (gap/row-gap).

column\_gap\_space_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`BoxStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.BoxStyle "nodriver.cdp.overlay.BoxStyle")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.FlexContainerHighlightConfig.column_gap_space "Link to this definition")

\[[`LineStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.LineStyle "nodriver.cdp.overlay.LineStyle")\

Style of the self-alignment line (align-items).

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#commands "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

disable()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#disable)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.disable "Link to this definition")

Disables domain notifications.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

get\_highlight\_object\_for\_test(_node\_id_, _include\_distance\=None_, _include\_style\=None_, _color\_format\=None_, _show\_accessibility\_info\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#get_highlight_object_for_test)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.get_highlight_object_for_test "Link to this definition")

For testing.

    \]) – _(Optional)_ The color format to get config with (default: hex).

    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether to show accessibility info (default: true).

highlight\_frame(_frame\_id_, _content\_color\=None_, _content\_outline\_color\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#highlight_frame)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.highlight_frame "Link to this definition")

Highlights owner element of the frame with given id. Deprecated: Doesn’t work reliably and cannot be fixed due to process separation (the owner node might be in a different process). Determine the owner node in the client and use highlightNode.

Deprecated since version 1.3.

Parameters:

*   **frame\_id** ([`FrameId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/page.html#nodriver.cdp.page.FrameId "nodriver.cdp.page.FrameId")
    ) – Identifier of the frame to highlight.
    
*   **content\_color** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`RGBA`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.RGBA "nodriver.cdp.dom.RGBA")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The content box highlight fill color (default: transparent).
    
*   **content\_outline\_color** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`RGBA`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.RGBA "nodriver.cdp.dom.RGBA")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The content box highlight outline color (default: transparent).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Deprecated since version 1.3.

highlight\_node(_highlight\_config_, _node\_id\=None_, _backend\_node\_id\=None_, _object\_id\=None_, _selector\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#highlight_node)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.highlight_node "Link to this definition")

Highlights DOM node with given id or with the given JavaScript object wrapper. Either nodeId or objectId must be specified.

Parameters:

*   **highlight\_config** ([`HighlightConfig`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.HighlightConfig "nodriver.cdp.overlay.HighlightConfig")
    ) – A descriptor for the highlight appearance.
    
*   **node\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`NodeId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.NodeId "nodriver.cdp.dom.NodeId")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Identifier of the node to highlight.
    
*   **backend\_node\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`BackendNodeId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.BackendNodeId "nodriver.cdp.dom.BackendNodeId")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Identifier of the backend node to highlight.
    
*   **object\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`RemoteObjectId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ JavaScript object id of the node to be highlighted.
    
*   **selector** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Selectors to highlight relevant nodes.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

highlight\_rect(_x_, _y_, _width_, _height_, _color\=None_, _outline\_color\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#highlight_rect)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.highlight_rect "Link to this definition")

Highlights given rectangle. Coordinates are absolute with respect to the main frame viewport. Issue: the method does not handle device pixel ratio (DPR) correctly. The coordinates currently have to be adjusted by the client if DPR is not 1 (see crbug.com/437807128).

Parameters:

*   **x** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – X coordinate
    
*   **y** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Y coordinate
    
*   **width** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Rectangle width
    
*   **height** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Rectangle height
    
*   **color** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`RGBA`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.RGBA "nodriver.cdp.dom.RGBA")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The highlight fill color (default: transparent).
    
*   **outline\_color** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`RGBA`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.RGBA "nodriver.cdp.dom.RGBA")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ The highlight outline color (default: transparent).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

highlight\_source\_order(_source\_order\_config_, _node\_id\=None_, _backend\_node\_id\=None_, _object\_id\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#highlight_source_order)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.highlight_source_order "Link to this definition")

Highlights the source order of the children of the DOM node with given id or with the given JavaScript object wrapper. Either nodeId or objectId must be specified.

Parameters:

*   **source\_order\_config** ([`SourceOrderConfig`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.SourceOrderConfig "nodriver.cdp.overlay.SourceOrderConfig")
    ) – A descriptor for the appearance of the overlay drawing.
    
*   **node\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`NodeId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.NodeId "nodriver.cdp.dom.NodeId")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Identifier of the node to highlight.
    
*   **backend\_node\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`BackendNodeId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.BackendNodeId "nodriver.cdp.dom.BackendNodeId")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Identifier of the backend node to highlight.
    
*   **object\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`RemoteObjectId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ JavaScript object id of the node to be highlighted.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

set\_inspect\_mode(_mode_, _highlight\_config\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#set_inspect_mode)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.set_inspect_mode "Link to this definition")

Enters the ‘inspect’ mode. In this mode, elements that user is hovering over are highlighted. Backend then generates ‘inspectNodeRequested’ event upon element selection.

Parameters:

*   **mode** ([`InspectMode`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.InspectMode "nodriver.cdp.overlay.InspectMode")
    ) – Set an inspection mode.
    
*   **highlight\_config** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`HighlightConfig`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.HighlightConfig "nodriver.cdp.overlay.HighlightConfig")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ A descriptor for the highlight appearance of hovered-over nodes. May be omitted if `` `enabled == false` ``.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\

**window\_controls\_overlay\_config** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`WindowControlsOverlayConfig`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.WindowControlsOverlayConfig "nodriver.cdp.overlay.WindowControlsOverlayConfig")\
\]) – _(Optional)_ Window Controls Overlay data, null means hide Window Controls Overlay

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#events "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_class_ InspectNodeRequested(_backend\_node\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#InspectNodeRequested)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.InspectNodeRequested "Link to this definition")

Fired when the node should be inspected. This happens after call to `setInspectMode` or when user manually inspects an element.

backend\_node\_id_: [`BackendNodeId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.BackendNodeId "nodriver.cdp.dom.BackendNodeId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.InspectNodeRequested.backend_node_id "Link to this definition")

Id of the node to inspect.

_class_ NodeHighlightRequested(_node\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#NodeHighlightRequested)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.NodeHighlightRequested "Link to this definition")

Fired when the node should be highlighted. This happens after call to `setInspectMode`.

node\_id_: [`NodeId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.NodeId "nodriver.cdp.dom.NodeId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.NodeHighlightRequested.node_id "Link to this definition")

_class_ ScreenshotRequested(_viewport_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#ScreenshotRequested)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.ScreenshotRequested "Link to this definition")

Fired when user asks to capture screenshot of some area on the page.

viewport_: [`Viewport`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/page.html#nodriver.cdp.page.Viewport "nodriver.cdp.page.Viewport")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.ScreenshotRequested.viewport "Link to this definition")

Viewport to capture, in device independent pixels (dip).

_class_ InspectModeCanceled[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#InspectModeCanceled)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/overlay.html#nodriver.cdp.overlay.InspectModeCanceled "Link to this definition")

Fired when user cancels the inspect mode.

# Runtime - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

Runtime[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#runtime "Link to this heading")

===================================================================================================================

Runtime domain exposes JavaScript runtime by means of remote evaluation and mirror objects. Evaluation results are returned as mirror object that expose object type, string representation and unique identifier that can be used for further object reference. Original objects are maintained in memory unless they are either explicitly released or are released along with the other objects in their object group.

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#types "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ ScriptId[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#ScriptId)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId "Link to this definition")

Unique script identifier.

_class_ SerializationOptions(_serialization_, _max\_depth\=None_, _additional\_parameters\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#SerializationOptions)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.SerializationOptions "Link to this definition")

Represents options for serialization. Overrides `generatePreview` and `returnByValue`.

serialization_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.SerializationOptions.serialization "Link to this definition")

max\_depth_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.SerializationOptions.max_depth "Link to this definition")

Deep serialization depth. Default is full depth. Respected only in `deep` serialization mode.

additional\_parameters_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.SerializationOptions.additional_parameters "Link to this definition")

Embedder-specific parameters. For example if connected to V8 in Chrome these control DOM serialization via `maxNodeDepth: integer` and `includeShadowTree: "none" `` "open" `` "all"`. Values can be only of type string or integer.

_class_ DeepSerializedValue(_type\__, _value\=None_, _object\_id\=None_, _weak\_local\_object\_reference\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#DeepSerializedValue)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.DeepSerializedValue "Link to this definition")

Represents deep serialized value.

type\__: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.DeepSerializedValue.type_ "Link to this definition")

value_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.DeepSerializedValue.value "Link to this definition")

object\_id_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.DeepSerializedValue.object_id "Link to this definition")

weak\_local\_object\_reference_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.DeepSerializedValue.weak_local_object_reference "Link to this definition")

Set if value reference met more then once during serialization. In such case, value is provided only to one of the serialized values. Unique per value in the scope of one CDP call.

_class_ RemoteObjectId[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#RemoteObjectId)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId "Link to this definition")

Unique object identifier.

_class_ UnserializableValue[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#UnserializableValue)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.UnserializableValue "Link to this definition")

Primitive value which cannot be JSON-stringified. Includes values `-0`, `NaN`, `Infinity`, `-Infinity`, and bigint literals.

_class_ RemoteObject(_type\__, _subtype\=None_, _class\_name\=None_, _value\=None_, _unserializable\_value\=None_, _description\=None_, _deep\_serialized\_value\=None_, _object\_id\=None_, _preview\=None_, _custom\_preview\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#RemoteObject)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject "Link to this definition")

Mirror object referencing original JavaScript object.

type\__: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject.type_ "Link to this definition")

Object type.

subtype_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject.subtype "Link to this definition")

Object subtype hint. Specified for `object` type values only. NOTE: If you change anything here, make sure to also update `subtype` in `ObjectPreview` and `PropertyPreview` below.

class\_name_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject.class_name "Link to this definition")

Object class (constructor) name. Specified for `object` type values only.

value_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject.value "Link to this definition")

Remote object value in case of primitive values or JSON values (if it was requested).

unserializable\_value_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`UnserializableValue`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.UnserializableValue "nodriver.cdp.runtime.UnserializableValue")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject.unserializable_value "Link to this definition")

Primitive value which can not be JSON-stringified does not have `value`, but gets this property.

description_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject.description "Link to this definition")

String representation of the object.

deep\_serialized\_value_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`DeepSerializedValue`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.DeepSerializedValue "nodriver.cdp.runtime.DeepSerializedValue")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject.deep_serialized_value "Link to this definition")

Deep serialized value.

object\_id_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RemoteObjectId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject.object_id "Link to this definition")

Unique object identifier (for non-primitive values).

preview_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`ObjectPreview`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ObjectPreview "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ObjectPreview")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject.preview "Link to this definition")

Preview containing abbreviated property values. Specified for `object` type values only.

custom\_preview_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`CustomPreview`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.CustomPreview "nodriver.cdp.runtime.CustomPreview")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject.custom_preview "Link to this definition")

_class_ CustomPreview(_header_, _body\_getter\_id\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#CustomPreview)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.CustomPreview "Link to this definition")

header_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.CustomPreview.header "Link to this definition")

The JSON-stringified result of formatter.header(object, config) call. It contains json ML array that represents RemoteObject.

body\_getter\_id_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`RemoteObjectId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.CustomPreview.body_getter_id "Link to this definition")

If formatter returns true as a result of formatter.hasBody call then bodyGetterId will contain RemoteObjectId for the function that returns result of formatter.body(object, config) call. The result value is json ML array.

_class_ ObjectPreview(_type\__, _overflow_, _properties_, _subtype\=None_, _description\=None_, _entries\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#ObjectPreview)

True iff some of the properties or entries of the original object did not fit.

\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ObjectPreview.subtype "Link to this definition")

description_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ObjectPreview.description "Link to this definition")

\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`EntryPreview`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.EntryPreview "nodriver.cdp.runtime.EntryPreview")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ObjectPreview.entries "Link to this definition")

List of the entries. Specified for `map` and `set` subtype values only.

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.EntryPreview "Link to this definition")

_class_ PropertyDescriptor(_name_, _configurable_, _enumerable_, _value\=None_, _writable\=None_, _get\=None_, _set\_\=None_, _was\_thrown\=None_, _is\_own\=None_, _symbol\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#PropertyDescriptor)

name_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.PropertyDescriptor.name "Link to this definition")

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.PropertyDescriptor.configurable "Link to this definition")

True if the type of this property descriptor may be changed and if the property may be deleted from the corresponding object.

enumerable_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.PropertyDescriptor.enumerable "Link to this definition")

True if this property shows up during enumeration of the properties on the corresponding object.

Embedder-specific auxiliary data likely matching {isDefault

_class_ StackTraceId(_id\__, _debugger\_id\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#StackTraceId)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTraceId "Link to this definition")

If `debuggerId` is set stack trace comes from another debugger and can be resolved there. This allows to track cross-debugger calls. See `Runtime.StackTrace` and `Debugger.paused` for usages.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTraceId.id_ "Link to this definition")
