\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTraceId.debugger_id "Link to this definition")

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#commands "Link to this heading")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

add\_binding(_name_, _execution\_context\_id\=None_, _execution\_context\_name\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#add_binding)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.add_binding "Link to this definition")

If executionContextId is empty, adds binding with the given name on the global objects of all inspected contexts, including those created later, bindings survive reloads. Binding function takes exactly one argument, this argument should be string, in case of any other input, function throws an exception. Each binding function call produces Runtime.bindingCalled notification.

Parameters:

*   **name** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) –
    
*   **execution\_context\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`ExecutionContextId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExecutionContextId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExecutionContextId")\
    \]) – **(DEPRECATED)** **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ If specified, the binding would only be exposed to the specified execution context. If omitted and ``` `executionContextName`` ``` is not set, the binding is exposed to all execution contexts of the target. This parameter is mutually exclusive with ` ``executionContextName`` `. Deprecated in favor of ` ``executionContextName`` ` due to an unclear use case and bugs in implementation (crbug.com/1169639). ` ``executionContextId`` ` will be removed in the future.
    
*   **execution\_context\_name** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If specified, the binding is exposed to the executionContext with matching name, even for contexts created after the binding is added. See also ` ``ExecutionContext.name`` ` and ` ``worldName`` ` parameter to ` ``Page.addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument`` `. This parameter is mutually exclusive with ``` ``executionContextId` ```.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

await\_promise(_promise\_object\_id_, _return\_by\_value\=None_, _generate\_preview\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#await_promise)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.await_promise "Link to this definition")

Add handler to promise with given promise object id.

Parameters:

*   **promise\_object\_id** ([`RemoteObjectId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId")
    ) – Identifier of the promise.
    
*   **return\_by\_value** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether the result is expected to be a JSON object that should be sent by value.
    
*   **generate\_preview** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether preview should be generated for the result.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`RemoteObject`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject")\
, [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`ExceptionDetails`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails")\
\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **result** - Promise result. Will contain rejected value if promise was rejected.
    
1.  **exceptionDetails** - _(Optional)_ Exception details if stack strace is available.
    

call\_function\_on(_function\_declaration_, _object\_id\=None_, _arguments\=None_, _silent\=None_, _return\_by\_value\=None_, _generate\_preview\=None_, _user\_gesture\=None_, _await\_promise\=None_, _execution\_context\_id\=None_, _object\_group\=None_, _throw\_on\_side\_effect\=None_, _unique\_context\_id\=None_, _serialization\_options\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#call_function_on)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.call_function_on "Link to this definition")

Calls function with given declaration on the given object. Object group of the result is inherited from the target object.

Parameters:

*   **function\_declaration** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Declaration of the function to call.
    
*   **object\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`RemoteObjectId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Identifier of the object to call function on. Either objectId or executionContextId should be specified.
    
*   **arguments** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \[[`CallArgument`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.CallArgument "nodriver.cdp.runtime.CallArgument")\
    \]\]) – _(Optional)_ Call arguments. All call arguments must belong to the same JavaScript world as the target object.
    
*   **silent** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ In silent mode exceptions thrown during evaluation are not reported and do not pause execution. Overrides ``` `setPauseOnException`` ``` state.
    
*   **return\_by\_value** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether the result is expected to be a JSON object which should be sent by value. Can be overriden by ` ``serializationOptions`` `.
    
*   **generate\_preview** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Whether preview should be generated for the result.
    
*   **user\_gesture** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether execution should be treated as initiated by user in the UI.
    
*   **await\_promise** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether execution should ` ``await`` ` for resulting value and return once awaited promise is resolved.
    
*   **execution\_context\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`ExecutionContextId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExecutionContextId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExecutionContextId")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Specifies execution context which global object will be used to call function on. Either executionContextId or objectId should be specified.
    
*   **object\_group** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Symbolic group name that can be used to release multiple objects. If objectGroup is not specified and objectId is, objectGroup will be inherited from object.
    
*   **throw\_on\_side\_effect** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Whether to throw an exception if side effect cannot be ruled out during evaluation.
    
*   **unique\_context\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ An alternative way to specify the execution context to call function on. Compared to contextId that may be reused across processes, this is guaranteed to be system-unique, so it can be used to prevent accidental function call in context different than intended (e.g. as a result of navigation across process boundaries). This is mutually exclusive with ` ``executionContextId`` `.
    
*   **serialization\_options** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`SerializationOptions`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.SerializationOptions "nodriver.cdp.runtime.SerializationOptions")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Specifies the result serialization. If provided, overrides ` ``generatePreview`` ` and ``` ``returnByValue` ```.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`RemoteObject`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject")\
, [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`ExceptionDetails`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails")\
\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **result** - Call result.
    
1.  **exceptionDetails** - _(Optional)_ Exception details.
    

compile\_script(_expression_, _source\_url_, _persist\_script_, _execution\_context\_id\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#compile_script)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.compile_script "Link to this definition")

Compiles expression.

Parameters:

*   **expression** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Expression to compile.
    
*   **source\_url** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Source url to be set for the script.
    
*   **persist\_script** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Specifies whether the compiled script should be persisted.
    
*   **execution\_context\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`ExecutionContextId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExecutionContextId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExecutionContextId")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Specifies in which execution context to perform script run. If the parameter is omitted the evaluation will be performed in the context of the inspected page.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`ScriptId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId")\
\], [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`ExceptionDetails`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails")\
\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **scriptId** - _(Optional)_ Id of the script.
    
1.  **exceptionDetails** - _(Optional)_ Exception details.
    

disable()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#disable)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.disable "Link to this definition")

Disables reporting of execution contexts creation.

\]

enable()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#enable)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.enable "Link to this definition")

Enables reporting of execution contexts creation by means of `executionContextCreated` event. When the reporting gets enabled the event will be sent immediately for each existing execution context.

\]

evaluate(_expression_, _object\_group\=None_, _include\_command\_line\_api\=None_, _silent\=None_, _context\_id\=None_, _return\_by\_value\=None_, _generate\_preview\=None_, _user\_gesture\=None_, _await\_promise\=None_, _throw\_on\_side\_effect\=None_, _timeout\=None_, _disable\_breaks\=None_, _repl\_mode\=None_, _allow\_unsafe\_eval\_blocked\_by\_csp\=None_, _unique\_context\_id\=None_, _serialization\_options\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#evaluate)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.evaluate "Link to this definition")

Evaluates expression on global object.

Parameters:

*   **expression** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Expression to evaluate.
    
*   **object\_group** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Symbolic group name that can be used to release multiple objects.
    
*   **include\_command\_line\_api** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Determines whether Command Line API should be available during the evaluation.
    
*   **silent** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ In silent mode exceptions thrown during evaluation are not reported and do not pause execution. Overrides ``` `setPauseOnException`` ``` state.
    
*   **context\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`ExecutionContextId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExecutionContextId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExecutionContextId")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Specifies in which execution context to perform evaluation. If the parameter is omitted the evaluation will be performed in the context of the inspected page. This is mutually exclusive with ` ``uniqueContextId`` `, which offers an alternative way to identify the execution context that is more reliable in a multi-process environment.
    
*   **return\_by\_value** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether the result is expected to be a JSON object that should be sent by value.
    
*   **generate\_preview** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Whether preview should be generated for the result.
    
*   **user\_gesture** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether execution should be treated as initiated by user in the UI.
    
*   **await\_promise** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether execution should ` ``await`` ` for resulting value and return once awaited promise is resolved.
    
*   **throw\_on\_side\_effect** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Whether to throw an exception if side effect cannot be ruled out during evaluation. This implies ` ``disableBreaks`` ` below.
    
*   **timeout** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`TimeDelta`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.TimeDelta "nodriver.cdp.runtime.TimeDelta")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Terminate execution after timing out (number of milliseconds).
    
*   **disable\_breaks** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Disable breakpoints during execution.
    
*   **repl\_mode** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Setting this flag to true enables ` ``let`` ` re-declaration and top-level ` ``await`` `. Note that ` ``let`` ` variables can only be re-declared if they originate from ` ``replMode`` ` themselves.
    
*   **allow\_unsafe\_eval\_blocked\_by\_csp** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ The Content Security Policy (CSP) for the target might block ‘unsafe-eval’ which includes eval(), Function(), setTimeout() and setInterval() when called with non-callable arguments. This flag bypasses CSP for this evaluation and allows unsafe-eval. Defaults to true.
    
*   **unique\_context\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ An alternative way to specify the execution context to evaluate in. Compared to contextId that may be reused across processes, this is guaranteed to be system-unique, so it can be used to prevent accidental evaluation of the expression in context different than intended (e.g. as a result of navigation across process boundaries). This is mutually exclusive with ` ``contextId`` `.
    
*   **serialization\_options** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`SerializationOptions`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.SerializationOptions "nodriver.cdp.runtime.SerializationOptions")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Specifies the result serialization. If provided, overrides ` ``generatePreview`` ` and ``` ``returnByValue` ```.
    

Return type:

get\_exception\_details(_error\_object\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#get_exception_details)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.get_exception_details "Link to this definition")

This method tries to lookup and populate exception details for a JavaScript Error object. Note that the stackTrace portion of the resulting exceptionDetails will only be populated if the Runtime domain was enabled at the time when the Error was thrown.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Parameters:

**error\_object\_id** ([`RemoteObjectId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId")
) – The error object for which to resolve the exception details.

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]\]

get\_heap\_usage()[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#get_heap_usage)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.get_heap_usage "Link to this definition")

Returns the JavaScript heap usage. It is the total usage of the corresponding isolate not scoped to a particular Runtime.

**EXPERIMENTAL**

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **usedSize** - Used JavaScript heap size in bytes.
    
1.  **totalSize** - Allocated JavaScript heap size in bytes.
    
2.  **embedderHeapUsedSize** - Used size in bytes in the embedder’s garbage-collected heap.
    
3.  **backingStorageSize** - Size in bytes of backing storage for array buffers and external strings.

get\_properties(_object\_id_, _own\_properties\=None_, _accessor\_properties\_only\=None_, _generate\_preview\=None_, _non\_indexed\_properties\_only\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#get_properties)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.get_properties "Link to this definition")

Returns properties of a given object. Object group of the result is inherited from the target object.

Parameters:

*   **object\_id** ([`RemoteObjectId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObjectId")
    ) – Identifier of the object to return properties for.
    
*   **own\_properties** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If true, returns properties belonging only to the element itself, not to its prototype chain.
    
*   **accessor\_properties\_only** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ If true, returns accessor properties (with getter/setter) only; internal properties are not returned either.
    
*   **generate\_preview** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ Whether preview should be generated for the results.
    
*   **non\_indexed\_properties\_only** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ If true, returns non-indexed properties only.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`PropertyDescriptor`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.PropertyDescriptor "nodriver.cdp.runtime.PropertyDescriptor")\
\], [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`InternalPropertyDescriptor`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.InternalPropertyDescriptor "nodriver.cdp.runtime.InternalPropertyDescriptor")\
\]\], [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`PrivatePropertyDescriptor`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.PrivatePropertyDescriptor "nodriver.cdp.runtime.PrivatePropertyDescriptor")\
\]\], [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`ExceptionDetails`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails")\
\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **result** - Object properties.
    
1.  **internalProperties** - _(Optional)_ Internal object properties (only of the element itself).
    
2.  **privateProperties** - _(Optional)_ Object private properties.
    
3.  **exceptionDetails** - _(Optional)_ Exception details.

run\_script(_script\_id_, _execution\_context\_id\=None_, _object\_group\=None_, _silent\=None_, _include\_command\_line\_api\=None_, _return\_by\_value\=None_, _generate\_preview\=None_, _await\_promise\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#run_script)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.run_script "Link to this definition")

Runs script with given id in a given context.

Parameters:

*   **script\_id** ([`ScriptId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ScriptId")
    ) – Id of the script to run.
    
*   **execution\_context\_id** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`ExecutionContextId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExecutionContextId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExecutionContextId")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Specifies in which execution context to perform script run. If the parameter is omitted the evaluation will be performed in the context of the inspected page.
    
*   **object\_group** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Symbolic group name that can be used to release multiple objects.
    
*   **silent** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ In silent mode exceptions thrown during evaluation are not reported and do not pause execution. Overrides ``` `setPauseOnException`` ``` state.
    
*   **include\_command\_line\_api** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Determines whether Command Line API should be available during the evaluation.
    
*   **return\_by\_value** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether the result is expected to be a JSON object which should be sent by value.
    
*   **generate\_preview** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether preview should be generated for the result.
    
*   **await\_promise** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ Whether execution should ``` ``await` ``` for resulting value and return once awaited promise is resolved.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Tuple`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Tuple "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`RemoteObject`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject "nodriver.cdp.runtime.RemoteObject")\
, [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`ExceptionDetails`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExceptionDetails")\
\]\]\]

Returns:

A tuple with the following items:

0.  **result** - Run result.
    
1.  **exceptionDetails** - _(Optional)_ Exception details.

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.set_async_call_stack_depth "Link to this definition")

Parameters:

**max\_depth** ([`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")

Events[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#events "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP events yourself. Instead, the API creates events for you and then you use the event’s attributes.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ConsoleAPICalled.type_ "Link to this definition")

\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ConsoleAPICalled.args "Link to this definition")

Call arguments.

execution\_context\_id_: [`ExecutionContextId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExecutionContextId "nodriver.cdp.runtime.ExecutionContextId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ConsoleAPICalled.execution_context_id "Link to this definition")

Identifier of the context where the call was made.

timestamp_: [`Timestamp`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.Timestamp "nodriver.cdp.runtime.Timestamp")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ConsoleAPICalled.timestamp "Link to this definition")

Call timestamp.

stack\_trace_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`StackTrace`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTrace "nodriver.cdp.runtime.StackTrace")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ConsoleAPICalled.stack_trace "Link to this definition")

Stack trace captured when the call was made. The async stack chain is automatically reported for the following call types: `assert`, `error`, `trace`, `warning`. For other types the async call chain can be retrieved using `Debugger.getStackTrace` and `stackTrace.parentId` field.

context_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/runtime.html#nodriver.cdp.runtime.ConsoleAPICalled.context "Link to this definition")

Console context descriptor for calls on non-default console context (not console.\*): ‘anonymous#unique-logger-id’ for call on unnamed context, ‘name#unique-logger-id’ for call on named context.

# CSS - nodriver documentation

Contents Menu Expand Light mode Dark mode Auto light/dark mode  

[Back to top](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#)

Toggle Light / Dark / Auto color theme

Toggle table of contents sidebar

CSS[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#css "Link to this heading")

=======================================================================================================

This domain exposes CSS read/write operations. All CSS objects (stylesheets, rules, and styles) have an associated [`id`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#id "(in Python v3.13)")
 used in subsequent operations on the related object. Each object type has a specific [`id`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#id "(in Python v3.13)")
 structure, and those are not interchangeable between objects of different kinds. CSS objects can be loaded using the `get*ForNode()` calls (which accept a DOM node id). A client can also keep track of stylesheets via the `styleSheetAdded`/`styleSheetRemoved` events and subsequently load the required stylesheet contents using the `getStyleSheet[Text]()` methods.

_This CDP domain is experimental._

*   [Types](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#types)
    
*   [Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#commands)
    
*   [Events](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#events)
    

Types[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#types "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Generally, you do not need to instantiate CDP types yourself. Instead, the API creates objects for you as return values from commands, and then you can use those objects as arguments to other commands.

_class_ StyleSheetId[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/css.html#StyleSheetId)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId "Link to this definition")

_class_ StyleSheetOrigin(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/css.html#StyleSheetOrigin)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetOrigin "Link to this definition")

Stylesheet type: “injected” for stylesheets injected via extension, “user-agent” for user-agent stylesheets, “inspector” for stylesheets created by the inspector (i.e. those holding the “via inspector” rules), “regular” for regular stylesheets.

INJECTED _\= 'injected'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetOrigin.INJECTED "Link to this definition")

USER\_AGENT _\= 'user-agent'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetOrigin.USER_AGENT "Link to this definition")

INSPECTOR _\= 'inspector'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetOrigin.INSPECTOR "Link to this definition")

REGULAR _\= 'regular'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetOrigin.REGULAR "Link to this definition")

\[[`CSSAnimationStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSAnimationStyle "nodriver.cdp.css.CSSAnimationStyle")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.InheritedAnimatedStyleEntry.animation_styles "Link to this definition")

Styles coming from the animations of the ancestor, if any, in the style inheritance chain.

transitions\_style_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`CSSStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyle "nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyle")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.InheritedAnimatedStyleEntry.transitions_style "Link to this definition")

The style coming from the transitions of the ancestor, if any, in the style inheritance chain.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.Specificity.a "Link to this definition")

The a component, which represents the number of ID selectors.

b_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.Specificity.b "Link to this definition")

The b component, which represents the number of class selectors, attributes selectors, and pseudo-classes.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.Specificity.c "Link to this definition")

The c component, which represents the number of type selectors and pseudo-elements.

_class_ CSSStyleSheetHeader(_style\_sheet\_id_, _frame\_id_, _source\_url_, _origin_, _title_, _disabled_, _is\_inline_, _is\_mutable_, _is\_constructed_, _start\_line_, _start\_column_, _length_, _end\_line_, _end\_column_, _source\_map\_url\=None_, _owner\_node\=None_, _has\_source\_url\=None_, _loading\_failed\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/css.html#CSSStyleSheetHeader)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader "Link to this definition")

CSS stylesheet metainformation.

style\_sheet\_id_: [`StyleSheetId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId "nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.style_sheet_id "Link to this definition")

The stylesheet identifier.

frame\_id_: [`FrameId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/page.html#nodriver.cdp.page.FrameId "nodriver.cdp.page.FrameId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.frame_id "Link to this definition")

Owner frame identifier.

source\_url_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.source_url "Link to this definition")

Stylesheet resource URL. Empty if this is a constructed stylesheet created using new CSSStyleSheet() (but non-empty if this is a constructed stylesheet imported as a CSS module script).

origin_: [`StyleSheetOrigin`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetOrigin "nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetOrigin")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.origin "Link to this definition")

Stylesheet origin.

title_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.title "Link to this definition")

Stylesheet title.

disabled_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.disabled "Link to this definition")

Denotes whether the stylesheet is disabled.

is\_inline_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.is_inline "Link to this definition")

Whether this stylesheet is created for STYLE tag by parser. This flag is not set for document.written STYLE tags.

is\_mutable_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.is_mutable "Link to this definition")

Whether this stylesheet is mutable. Inline stylesheets become mutable after they have been modified via CSSOM API. `<link>` element’s stylesheets become mutable only if DevTools modifies them. Constructed stylesheets (new CSSStyleSheet()) are mutable immediately after creation.

is\_constructed_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.is_constructed "Link to this definition")

True if this stylesheet is created through new CSSStyleSheet() or imported as a CSS module script.

start\_line_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.start_line "Link to this definition")

Line offset of the stylesheet within the resource (zero based).

start\_column_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.start_column "Link to this definition")

Column offset of the stylesheet within the resource (zero based).

length_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.length "Link to this definition")

Size of the content (in characters).

end\_line_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.end_line "Link to this definition")

Line offset of the end of the stylesheet within the resource (zero based).

end\_column_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.end_column "Link to this definition")

Column offset of the end of the stylesheet within the resource (zero based).

source\_map\_url_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.source_map_url "Link to this definition")

URL of source map associated with the stylesheet (if any).

owner\_node_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`BackendNodeId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.BackendNodeId "nodriver.cdp.dom.BackendNodeId")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.owner_node "Link to this definition")

The backend id for the owner node of the stylesheet.

has\_source\_url_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.has_source_url "Link to this definition")

Whether the sourceURL field value comes from the sourceURL comment.

loading\_failed_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyleSheetHeader.loading_failed "Link to this definition")

If the style sheet was loaded from a network resource, this indicates when the resource failed to load

_class_ CSSRule(_selector\_list_, _origin_, _style_, _style\_sheet\_id\=None_, _nesting\_selectors\=None_, _media\=None_, _container\_queries\=None_, _supports\=None_, _layers\=None_, _scopes\=None_, _rule\_types\=None_, _starting\_styles\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/css.html#CSSRule)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule "Link to this definition")

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule.selector_list "Link to this definition")

Rule selector data.

origin_: [`StyleSheetOrigin`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetOrigin "nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetOrigin")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule.origin "Link to this definition")

Parent stylesheet’s origin.

style_: [`CSSStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyle "nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStyle")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule.style "Link to this definition")

Associated style declaration.

style\_sheet\_id_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`StyleSheetId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId "nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule.style_sheet_id "Link to this definition")

The css style sheet identifier (absent for user agent stylesheet and user-specified stylesheet rules) this rule came from.

nesting\_selectors_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule.nesting_selectors "Link to this definition")

Array of selectors from ancestor style rules, sorted by distance from the current rule.

media_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`CSSMedia`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSMedia "nodriver.cdp.css.CSSMedia")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule.media "Link to this definition")

Media list array (for rules involving media queries). The array enumerates media queries starting with the innermost one, going outwards.

container\_queries_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`CSSContainerQuery`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSContainerQuery "nodriver.cdp.css.CSSContainerQuery")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule.container_queries "Link to this definition")

Container query list array (for rules involving container queries). The array enumerates container queries starting with the innermost one, going outwards.

supports_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`CSSSupports`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSSupports "nodriver.cdp.css.CSSSupports")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule.supports "Link to this definition")

@supports CSS at-rule array. The array enumerates @supports at-rules starting with the innermost one, going outwards.

layers_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`CSSLayer`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSLayer "nodriver.cdp.css.CSSLayer")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule.layers "Link to this definition")

Cascade layer array. Contains the layer hierarchy that this rule belongs to starting with the innermost layer and going outwards.

scopes_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`CSSScope`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSScope "nodriver.cdp.css.CSSScope")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule.scopes "Link to this definition")

@scope CSS at-rule array. The array enumerates @scope at-rules starting with the innermost one, going outwards.

rule\_types_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`CSSRuleType`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRuleType "nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRuleType")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule.rule_types "Link to this definition")

The array keeps the types of ancestor CSSRules from the innermost going outwards.

starting\_styles_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`CSSStartingStyle`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStartingStyle "nodriver.cdp.css.CSSStartingStyle")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule.starting_styles "Link to this definition")

@starting-style CSS at-rule array. The array enumerates @starting-style at-rules starting with the innermost one, going outwards.

_class_ CSSRuleType(_value_, _names\=None_, _\*_, _module\=None_, _qualname\=None_, _type\=None_, _start\=1_, _boundary\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/css.html#CSSRuleType)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRuleType "Link to this definition")

Enum indicating the type of a CSS rule, used to represent the order of a style rule’s ancestors. This list only contains rule types that are collected during the ancestor rule collection.

MEDIA\_RULE _\= 'MediaRule'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRuleType.MEDIA_RULE "Link to this definition")

SUPPORTS\_RULE _\= 'SupportsRule'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRuleType.SUPPORTS_RULE "Link to this definition")

CONTAINER\_RULE _\= 'ContainerRule'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRuleType.CONTAINER_RULE "Link to this definition")

LAYER\_RULE _\= 'LayerRule'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRuleType.LAYER_RULE "Link to this definition")

SCOPE\_RULE _\= 'ScopeRule'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRuleType.SCOPE_RULE "Link to this definition")

STYLE\_RULE _\= 'StyleRule'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRuleType.STYLE_RULE "Link to this definition")

STARTING\_STYLE\_RULE _\= 'StartingStyleRule'_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRuleType.STARTING_STYLE_RULE "Link to this definition")

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.RuleUsage "Link to this definition")

CSS coverage information.

style\_sheet\_id_: [`StyleSheetId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId "nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.RuleUsage.style_sheet_id "Link to this definition")

The css style sheet identifier (absent for user agent stylesheet and user-specified stylesheet rules) this rule came from.

start\_offset_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.RuleUsage.start_offset "Link to this definition")

Offset of the start of the rule (including selector) from the beginning of the stylesheet.

end\_offset_: [`float`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.RuleUsage.end_offset "Link to this definition")

Offset of the end of the rule body from the beginning of the stylesheet.

used_: [`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.RuleUsage.used "Link to this definition")

Indicates whether the rule was actually used by some element in the page.

[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.SourceRange "Link to this definition")

Text range within a resource. All numbers are zero-based.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.SourceRange.start_line "Link to this definition")

Start line of range.

start\_column_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.SourceRange.start_column "Link to this definition")

Start column of range (inclusive).

end\_line_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.SourceRange.end_line "Link to this definition")

End line of range

end\_column_: [`int`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.SourceRange.end_column "Link to this definition")

End column of range (exclusive).

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.ComputedStyleExtraFields.is_appearance_base "Link to this definition")

Returns whether or not this node is being rendered with base appearance, which happens when it has its appearance property set to base/base-select or it is in the subtree of an element being rendered with base appearance.

_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSProperty.value "Link to this definition")

\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSProperty.important "Link to this definition")

Whether the property has “!important” annotation (implies `false` if absent).

implicit_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\

\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSProperty.parsed_ok "Link to this definition")

Whether the property is understood by the browser (implies `true` if absent).

\[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSProperty.disabled "Link to this definition")

Whether the property is disabled by the user (present for source-based properties only).

The entire property range in the enclosing style declaration (if available).

longhand\_properties_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\

\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSProperty.longhand_properties "Link to this definition")

Parsed longhand components of this property if it is a shorthand. This field will be empty if the given property is not a shorthand.

_class_ CSSMedia(_text_, _source_, _source\_url\=None_, _range\_\=None_, _style\_sheet\_id\=None_, _media\_list\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/css.html#CSSMedia)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSMedia "Link to this definition")

CSS media rule descriptor.

text_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSMedia.text "Link to this definition")

Media query text.

source_: [`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSMedia.source "Link to this definition")

“mediaRule” if specified by a @media rule, “importRule” if specified by an @import rule, “linkedSheet” if specified by a “media” attribute in a linked stylesheet’s LINK tag, “inlineSheet” if specified by a “media” attribute in an inline stylesheet’s STYLE tag.

Type:

Source of the media query

source\_url_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSMedia.source_url "Link to this definition")

URL of the document containing the media query description.

range\__: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`SourceRange`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.SourceRange "nodriver.cdp.css.SourceRange")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSMedia.range_ "Link to this definition")

The associated rule (@media or @import) header range in the enclosing stylesheet (if available).

style\_sheet\_id_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`StyleSheetId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId "nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId")\
\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSMedia.style_sheet_id "Link to this definition")

Identifier of the stylesheet containing this object (if exists).

media\_list_: [`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`MediaQuery`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.MediaQuery "nodriver.cdp.css.MediaQuery")\
\]\]_ _\= None_[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSMedia.media_list "Link to this definition")

Array of media queries.

Commands[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#commands "Link to this heading")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each command is a generator function. The return type `Generator[x, y, z]` indicates that the generator _yields_ arguments of type `x`, it must be resumed with an argument of type `y`, and it returns type `z`. In this library, types `x` and `y` are the same for all commands, and `z` is the return type you should pay attention to. For more information, see [Getting Started: Commands](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/readme.html#getting-started-commands)
.

add\_rule(_style\_sheet\_id_, _rule\_text_, _location_, _node\_for\_property\_syntax\_validation\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/css.html#add_rule)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.add_rule "Link to this definition")

Inserts a new rule with the given `ruleText` in a stylesheet with given `styleSheetId`, at the position specified by `location`.

Parameters:

*   **style\_sheet\_id** ([`StyleSheetId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId "nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId")
    ) – The css style sheet identifier where a new rule should be inserted.
    
*   **rule\_text** ([`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – The text of a new rule.
    
*   **location** ([`SourceRange`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.SourceRange "nodriver.cdp.css.SourceRange")
    ) – Text position of a new rule in the target style sheet.
    
*   **node\_for\_property\_syntax\_validation** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`NodeId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.NodeId "nodriver.cdp.dom.NodeId")\
    \]) – **(EXPERIMENTAL)** _(Optional)_ NodeId for the DOM node in whose context custom property declarations for registered properties should be validated. If omitted, declarations in the new rule text can only be validated statically, which may produce incorrect results if the declaration contains a var() for example.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`CSSRule`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule "nodriver.cdp.css.CSSRule")\
\]

Returns:

The newly created rule.

collect\_class\_names(_style\_sheet\_id_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/css.html#collect_class_names)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.collect_class_names "Link to this definition")

) –

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]\]

Returns:

Class name list.

create\_style\_sheet(_frame\_id_, _force\=None_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/css.html#create_style_sheet)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.create_style_sheet "Link to this definition")

Creates a new special “via-inspector” stylesheet in the frame with given `frameId`.

Parameters:

*   **frame\_id** ([`FrameId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/page.html#nodriver.cdp.page.FrameId "nodriver.cdp.page.FrameId")
    ) – Identifier of the frame where “via-inspector” stylesheet should be created.
    
*   **force** ([`Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – _(Optional)_ If true, creates a new stylesheet for every call. If false, returns a stylesheet previously created by a call with force=false for the frame’s document if it exists or creates a new stylesheet (default: false).
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`StyleSheetId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId "nodriver.cdp.css.StyleSheetId")\
\]

Returns:

Identifier of the created “via-inspector” stylesheet.

force\_pseudo\_state(_node\_id_, _forced\_pseudo\_classes_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/css.html#force_pseudo_state)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.force_pseudo_state "Link to this definition")

Ensures that the given node will have specified pseudo-classes whenever its style is computed by the browser.

Parameters:

*   **node\_id** ([`NodeId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.NodeId "nodriver.cdp.dom.NodeId")
    ) – The element id for which to force the pseudo state.
    
*   **forced\_pseudo\_classes** ([`List`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.List "(in Python v3.13)")
    \[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
    \]) – Element pseudo classes to force when computing the element’s style.
    

Return type:

[`Generator`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Generator "(in Python v3.13)")
\[[`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`Dict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Dict "(in Python v3.13)")\
\[[`str`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str "(in Python v3.13)")\
, [`Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "(in Python v3.13)")\
\], [`None`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html#None "(in Python v3.13)")\
\]

force\_starting\_style(_node\_id_, _forced_)[\[source\]](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/_modules/nodriver/cdp/css.html#force_starting_style)
[#](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/css.html#nodriver.cdp.css.force_starting_style "Link to this definition")

Ensures that the given node is in its starting-style state.

Parameters:

*   **node\_id** ([`NodeId`](https://ultrafunkamsterdam.github.io/nodriver/nodriver/cdp/dom.html#nodriver.cdp.dom.NodeId "nodriver.cdp.dom.NodeId")
    ) – The element id for which to force the starting-style state.
    
*   **forced** ([`bool`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool "(in Python v3.13)")
    ) – Boolean indicating if this is on or off.
